races.name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Royal Mile Handicap

runners.name: Comic Hero
runners.spotlight: Consistent in three runs since debut, gaining a deserved first success at Kempton (7f) in February; the runner-up that day won in clearcut style next time; should be at least as good over 1m and remains unexposed; interesting candidate on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Kempton win last time has been boosted; unexposed; interesting candidate on handicap debut

runners.name: Vincenzo Peruggia
runners.spotlight: Cost 550,000gns as a yearling and ran only three times as a 2yo, winning 7f novice at Beverley (good to firm) before being hampered when last in the Group 2 Vintage Stakes at Goodwood; has not been let in lightly for handicap debut judged on that bare form, but he's completely unexposed, will stay 1m and his stable won this race 12 months ago; respected.
runners.comment: Thrice-raced as 2yo, winning at Beverley; unexposed and commands respect on handicap debut

runners.name: Kanishka
runners.spotlight: Arrives on the back of two creditable runs over 7f at Southwell, not beaten far when fourth of nine on latest; should stay 1m and he's at least as effective on turf as AW; in the mix in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Arrives after two creditable runs at Southwell; in the mix in first-time cheekpieces
runners.owner: C Booth P Boyle S Coleby & S Smith

runners.name: Utmost Good Faith
runners.spotlight: Free-goer; won his first two races (1m at Kempton and 7f at Wolverhampton) for George Boughey before finishing last of four in the face of a sterner assignment back at Kempton; his mark has risen and others may have more potential.
runners.comment: 2-3 for George Boughey but mark has risen and others may have more potential

runners.name: Strength Of Spirit
runners.spotlight: Improved with each run as a 2yo, readily winning nursery at Newbury (7f, good to firm; subsequently gelded) on last of them in July; has a long absence to overcome but he's in good hands and may still have untapped potential; should have no difficulty at all getting 1m.
runners.comment: Progressive last term; Newbury winner on final start; may still have untapped potential
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Xlviii & Mrs E Burke

runners.name: Monoceros
runners.spotlight: Well bred (his dam was second in the Oaks), cost 750,000gns as a yearling and made a winning start for Charlie Appleby at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) last summer; however, he didn't build on that and he was pulled up in a nursery on his final outing; sold for just 25,000gns in February, gelded and probably best watched on debut for a new stable.
runners.comment: Didn't progress after debut win last term; probably best watched on first run for new yard

races.name: Livescore Bet Silver Arrow Handicap

runners.name: Al Arbeed
runners.spotlight: Successful 7f debut at Southwell; just a handful of starts since, showing useful form when fourth of ten on handicap debut in a warm race on Southwell AW in October only to run poorly next time; likely to come on for last week's Kempton run but now needs to show he can hack it on turf.
runners.comment: Useful 7f form in warm Southwell handicap over 7f; now needs to show he's as good on turf

runners.name: Persuasion
runners.spotlight: Five wins include this race on course debut (good to soft) last year, when beating Gweedore; back below that winning mark but today's race should take more winning and he'll do well to make it three wins in the last four runnings for his yard.
runners.comment: Won this on his course debut last year; below that mark now; this should take more winning

runners.name: Goldmoyne
runners.spotlight: On lowly mark when arriving at this yard but he's found new reserves as the AW season has gone on and was winning for the sixth time when completing a front-running four-timer at Lingfield (1m; earlier wins at 6f/7f) recently; acted on turf in modest company in Ireland but this is more problematic.
runners.comment: Six AW wins at 6f-1m for this yard; has a very different task now back on turf

runners.name: A War Eagle
runners.spotlight: Eased right down when switched to soft ground on final 3yo start but mildly progressive in handicaps at about this trip previously, his win coming at Beverley (good to firm) last June; left Edward Bethell for 26,000gns in the autumn; 2lb out of the weights but worth a market check.
runners.comment: Mildly progressive in handicaps as 3yo; sold from E Bethell for 26,000gns; check market

runners.name: Jannas Journey
runners.spotlight: Seven wins in 2025, at 6f-1m on ground ranging from good to firm to soft, including one over C&D; however, she was beaten three times off this mark since the latest of them in September so needs to be better than ever to have a chance of making a winning reappearance.
runners.comment: Seven wins for new yard in 2025, at 6f-1m;; needs to be better than ever for a win today

runners.name: Bellarchi
runners.spotlight: Has won on good to soft, good to firm and good; made it 2-3 over C&D when beating Jannas Journey in October but she was a much lesser light in four subsequent runs last year; made a fair reappearance on AW recently but easier targets await.
runners.comment: Made it 2-3 over C&D in October; much lesser efforts since; easier targets await

runners.name: Royal Zabeel
runners.spotlight: Made his name on AW but he can do it on turf when the ground is quick, as when a cosy winner at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) in August; has since added a second Listed AW event, at Southwell in November, but the Saudi Arabia venture came to nothing in February and he has ground worries today.
runners.comment: Two Listed AW wins (7f/6f); cosy turf winner on good to firm in August; ground query today

runners.name: Midnight Gun
runners.spotlight: Faded in a 1m Listed race at Saint-Cloud (heavy) in November; had previously won a novice and a handicap (good) at that trip but the return to 7f worked well for him at Doncaster (soft) in October when he was bumped and later met trouble, only just failing against one on the crest of a wave; that strong form gives him solid claims.
runners.comment: Strong 7f handicap form when unlucky at Doncaster (soft) in October; solid claims

runners.name: Gweedore
runners.spotlight: Often leads; superb 9yo with six course wins, the latest over C&D (soft) in November; not weighted out of it off the same mark as his second to Persuasion here last April but he's had a much longer break this time and is drawn widest.
runners.comment: Six course wins, the latest over C&D in November; same mark as 2nd in this race last year

runners.name: Golden Mind
runners.spotlight: Can lead; had some smart form in defeat during 2025 alongside three wins at 6f and 7f (AW and heavy); never showed with a chance on AW comeback but this easy test could see him to advantage; has possibilities.
runners.comment: Had a good 2025 with 3 wins at 6f/7f, and smart form in defeat; this sharp test can suit

races.name: Virgin Bet Goliath Cup Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Al Qareem
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who keeps pulling out more, with 12 wins; the four last season (April-October) included career-best form; unraced here; has never won first time out but he has run creditably and is the clear form pick.
runners.comment: Has never won first time out but he has run creditably and is the clear form pick

runners.name: Roaring Legend
runners.spotlight: A poor show last May prompted a second wind operation and it was a solid campaign otherwise; never better than when second this February in 2m AW handicaps at Wolverhampton and Lingfield, but peak turf form isn't far behind it and he'll probably be on the premises.
runners.comment: Never better than in February; peak turf form not far behind; probably be on the premises

runners.name: Mount Atlas
runners.spotlight: Had a very solid campaign last season bar one blip (front-running was blamed); all but one of those races was at 1m4f, with a creditable fifth of six thrown in over the extended 1m5f at Chester in May (Group 3) but there is a bit for him to prove on this first foray over 1m6f.
runners.comment: Solid campaign last season but there's a bit for him to prove on this first crack at 1m6f

runners.name: Lady Vivian
runners.spotlight: Maiden and front-running handicap winner last spring, who found her one Group and two Listed assignments much harder afterwards but those were her best form; all those were at about 1m4f but her second from off the pace at Toulouse on final outing gave hope for this longer trip; improvement needed.
runners.comment: 2nd from off the pace at Toulouse (Listed) on final start gave hope for this longer trip

runners.name: Many Men
runners.spotlight: Excellent progress included three handicap wins (1m2f, 1m6f and 2m) last March-July and an agonisingly close call in the Melrose at York in August; close fourth in 1m6f Listed race at Ascot on final outing; better still is required but his record of improvement demands respect.
runners.comment: Better still is required but his record of improvement demands respect
runners.owner: J Finch Dr P Wilson & R Stanbridge

runners.name: Kate ORiley
runners.spotlight: Dual Wolverhampton AW winner (8.5f/1m4f) for Michael Bell, a hurdles winner in October and back on the Flat with two good AW runs (1m2f/1m3f handicaps) this year; has a bit more to prove as turf performer on the Flat and also over this new trip, with a big step forward required at this level.
runners.comment: More to prove on Flat turf and over new trip, with a big step forward required anyway

races.name: Virgin Bet Daily Price Boost Queen's Cup Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Gambino
runners.spotlight: First and second on turf in Ireland last April/August on his only two starts for Noel Meade; debut for new stable saw him take third in the 1m4f AW novice event won by Constitution Hill, followed by another encouraging run on hurdle debut at Newbury two weeks ago; now tongue tied first time; seriously unexposed and no great surprise if he starts handicap life on a good mark.
runners.comment: Seriously unexposed and no great surprise if he starts handicap life on a good mark

runners.name: Baileys Khelstar
runners.spotlight: Progressive sort as 3yo and the early stages as 4yo (effective at 1m5f-2m) and he'd be a player if returning in peak form; the potential problem is that he's unraced since a lesser show in July 2024.
runners.comment: Progressive sort as 3yo and early stages as 4yo; unraced since a lesser show in July 2024
runners.owner: Baileys Horse Feeds (F H Nash Ltd)

runners.name: Team Player
runners.spotlight: Won twice over about 1m4f (good/AW) last summer for Gemma Tutty and, tongue tied for the first time, made a successful stable debut from off the pace in 11-runner race at Newcastle (2m, AW) six weeks ago; up 5lb but that latest win came in tidy style and he has to be considered seriously.
runners.comment: Up 5lb but stable debut win (2m, AW) came in tidy style; has to be considered seriously

runners.name: Gentle Warrior
runners.spotlight: Highly reliable in handicaps, with 1m6f doing the trick when he won on good ground at Haydock and Chester in space of a week last September; needs to resume progress but enters calculations.
runners.comment: Highly reliable in handicaps, with 1m6f doing the trick for a quick double last September
runners.owner: Hambleton Racing Ltd XXXV & E Burke

runners.name: Wise Eagle
runners.spotlight: Won this prize in 2023 and had a fruitful Flat/hurdles campaign last winter before his close fourth in this; while he did not make an impact in either of his Flat runs this winter, latest hurdles form was his best in that sphere; 8lb lower than the 2025 renewal and a case can certainly be made.
runners.comment: Won this prize in 2023, fourth last year; latest hurdles form was his best in that sphere

runners.name: Almuhit
runners.spotlight: Rediscovered the knack for new stable last year, including two Flat wins in four-runner races and two places when the action got more competitive; returned to some hurdles form on latest outing but all last year's achievements came when front-running in 2m races.
runners.comment: Returned to some hurdles form last time; did well last year front-running at 2m on Flat

runners.name: Humble Spark
runners.spotlight: Breaking slowly had become a problem (better on latest start) in his five-race campaign this winter, when a notch or two below his best; 1-10 record on turf is also none too persuasive.
runners.comment: Five-race campaign this winter, when a notch or two below his best; 1-10 record on turf

runners.name: Trojan Sun
runners.spotlight: Musselburgh specialist (4-10 here) who had three wins at this track on good ground last May-October; also scored at Nottingham in October, a runaway win over 2m2f on heavy going which sees him 5lb higher today; needs to hit a new high and may need to front-run back at this trip.
runners.comment: 2025 wins included three on good ground here in May-October; needs to hit a new high

runners.name: Sportingsilvermine
runners.spotlight: His only two wins (30 races) were last spring on his first two starts for James Owen; a string of creditable efforts followed over 1m2f-1m4f but that was after a lesser effort on his only attempt at this trip.
runners.comment: String of creditable efforts over 1m2f-1m4f, but after a lesser effort on sole go at 1m6f

runners.name: Dancingwithmyself
runners.spotlight: Not the most consistent but won twice last season, latterly in clearcut fashion with a late bid at Goodwood (1m6f, good to soft) in September despite suffering trouble in running; 6lb higher today but she went close on last year's return and her yard is among the winners on AW; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Went close on last year's return; clearcut 1m6f win on penultimate start; yard in form

runners.name: Mr Lincoln
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; beaten 30l and more over hurdles in first four British starts but back to life for a short-head defeat 13 days ago (second run after wind surgery); now returns to Flat, in which sphere he's 1-9 having won a maiden in 2023 and been second in a 1m5f handicap last July; needs a second look.
runners.comment: Back to life for short-head loss over hurdles 13 days ago (second run after wind surgery)

runners.name: Moon Over Miami
runners.spotlight: Just six races and won his only three on turf; three on AW early last year before he battled his way to a narrow front-running win at Newbury (2m, good) last April; absent since and while he shaped as if he could make into a smart performer, that may be a bit more likely at 2m+.
runners.comment: Off since battling his way to a narrow front-running win at Newbury (2m, good) last April

runners.name: Shadow Dance
runners.spotlight: Career highlights (1m4f/1m6f) for Roger Varian were in 2024 and he managed only mid-division in his three handicaps (at top meetings) last July-September; rather worse in both runs (his first races on AW) for new connections early this year but he returns to turf 8lb below his career-high mark.
runners.comment: Only mid-division in 2025; rather worse in both runs (first AW races) for new connections

races.name: Virgin Bet Scottish Sprint Cup Handicap

runners.name: Lexington Blitz
runners.spotlight: A third representative in this field for Robert Cowell; came good at Catterick (5f, good) last autumn after several near misses; possibly found heavy ground against him at Doncaster on final start and has since been gelded; not ruled out if ready to do himself justice after a break.
runners.comment: Came good at Catterick last autumn; has since been gelded; not ruled out after a break

runners.name: Gangsta Man
runners.spotlight: Previously trained in Ireland, winning twice (7.5f/7f) in 2025 before changing hands for 31,000gns; has to prove his effectiveness over a sharp 5f, although his new stable does have a tremendous knack with sprinters.
runners.comment: Two wins over longer trips in Ireland last year; new yard does very well with sprinters

runners.name: Democracy Dilemma
runners.spotlight: Tough 5f specialist who usually makes the running; a respectable third at Lingfield six weeks ago should have blown away the cobwebs; concedes weight all round but he's a classy type and should go well.
runners.comment: Front-runner; respectable third at Lingfield on return; should go well despite topweight

runners.name: Heavenly Heather
runners.spotlight: Scooped a big pot over 7f at Newcastle (200-1) last Easter and has shown even better form over 5f and 6f there since; however, she has achieved significantly less in two turf runs and needs to be right at her best back on grass.
runners.comment: Three wins at Newcastle but has achieved significantly less in two turf runs

runners.name: Jer Batt
runners.spotlight: A losing sequence is mounting but he has a win and a third from just two attempts over C&D, and the latter was first time out last season; 7lb lower here and he's of obvious interest if cherry ripe after 161 days off (has had wind surgery); one of two very plausible candidates for the Barrons.
runners.comment: Hasn't won for a while but good third over C&D on 2025 reappearance and now 7lb lower

runners.name: Against The Wind
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive and posted another personal best when third at Southwell (5f, AW) on reappearance; his turf form is at a lower level but he still won at Thirsk last spring and finished third in a big field at York's Dante meeting (both 5f, good to firm); race-fit and should be in the thick of things again if a low draw doesn't count against him.
runners.comment: Personal best with Southwell third; drawn wide but should be in the thick of things again

runners.name: Tropical Storm
runners.spotlight: More miss than hit in 2025, winning 5f Listed race at York (good to firm) in May but failing to make the frame otherwise; now returns from a gelding operation on a handy mark and with his stable in form; big chance if he's on a going day.
runners.comment: More miss than hit in 2025 but returns on handy mark after gelding op; big chance

runners.name: Rhythm N Hooves
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer who won off 2lb lower at Newbury (5f, good to firm) last summer; held his form well without winning on the AW over the winter; no obvious reason why he won't be involved again but one or two of these could be better handicapped.
runners.comment: Holding his form well; in the mix but one or two of these could be better handicapped

runners.name: Pilgrim
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed his finest hour when winning the 5f 3yo handicap at Royal Ascot in 2024; seen only twice last season and down the field on both his AW outings this term, but he met trouble last time and is probably in much better form than his finishing position suggests; undoubtedly well handicapped again and this C&D winner is interesting with a good-value claimer booked.
runners.comment: Falling in the weights and a C&D winner; very interesting with good-value claimer booked

runners.name: Eternal Sunshine
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; better than ever last season, completing hat-trick in the Portland at Doncaster (extended 5f, good) in September and going close at York (5f, good) the following month; may not have reached her ceiling just yet and well drawn if ready to roll after six months off.
runners.comment: Had excellent 2025, winning the Portland; well drawn if ready to roll after six months off

runners.name: Vintage Clarets
runners.spotlight: Ten-time winner whose mark is now 3lb lower than for his latest success; probably needed the run when well held at Southwell two weeks ago, but didn't show much and may require another before hitting his stride.
runners.comment: Ten-time winner; probably needed reappearance; may require another to shake off the rust

runners.name: Canons House
runners.spotlight: Four wins last year included a 5f Racing League event at Southwell in August, and he didn't get the rub of the green when down the field on his two subsequent starts; may still be unexposed and he's worth a market check after his winter break.
runners.comment: Four wins last year and had excuses on last two starts; may still be unexposed
runners.owner: M McHale J Munroe M Guy & D Walshe

runners.name: Archduke Ferdinand
runners.spotlight: Below par in 2025 after winning at Redcar (6f, good to firm) in May; now 1lb lower but this is a warm race for his reappearance and he'll have to be right at the top of his game.
runners.comment: Below par in 2025 after May Redcar win; will have to be in top form after layoff

runners.name: Jordan Electrics
runners.spotlight: Successful 16 times in all, seven of those wins coming during a stellar 2024; 0-8 last term, and although his mark has slipped he has no great record fresh and may be of more interest with a run under his belt.
runners.comment: 16 wins in all but none last season; may be of more interest with a run under his belt
runners.owner: B Jordan Brian Jordan S Jordan & N McConnell

races.name: Virgin Bet Supports Safe Gambling EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Qualifier) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: The Kalonji Man
runners.spotlight: Too green to do himself justice in last week's Brocklesby (won by a stablemate), beating only one home after a slow start; however, on paper that was a much stronger race than this one, and it would be no surprise at all to see him figure much more prominently with the run behind him.
runners.comment: Green when well held on last week's debut; no surprise at all to see him fare much better

runners.name: Magical El
runners.spotlight: 42,000euros yearling; Make Believe half-sister to Listed-placed 7f winner Asian Daze (including 2yo; RPR 109); dam dual bumper winner, out of unraced half-sister to 6f 2yo Group 3 winner Habaayib; cost more than most of these and makes some appeal on pedigree; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Cost more than most of these and makes some appeal on pedigree; worth a market check

runners.name: Midnight Curfew
runners.spotlight: 39,000euros yearling; early foal; closely related to 5f/6f 2yo winner Where's Clare (RPR 85); dam French Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner (97), half-sister to Qatari 1m2f Listed winner Emperor Maximus; has a plausible pedigree for a race of this nature.
runners.comment: Early foal; closely related to a 5f/6f 2yo winner; plausible pedigree for a race like this

runners.name: Tricky Jenny
runners.spotlight: 19,000gns yearling; by Ardad; dam 1m AW 2yo winner (RPR 56), closely related to winners Maftoon (1m/1m1f) and Overpriced Mixer (7f/1m Flat and 2m hurdle); first 2yo runner of the season for a very capable Newmarket yard; must be respected.
runners.comment: 19,000gns yearling; first 2yo runner of the season for a very capable Newmarket yard

runners.name: Jaydees Dream
runners.spotlight: 13,000euros yearling; by Dubawi Legend; dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 64), half-sister to winners Skeaping (1m2f Flat/2m hurdle), Time Najd (KSA 1m1f/1m2f) and Windy Cove (2m hurdle); pedigree suggests he may come into his own over longer distances.
runners.comment: 13,000euros yearling; pedigree suggests he may come into his own over longer distances

runners.name: Stay On Tab
runners.spotlight: Â£5,000 yearling; by Harry Angel; dam 7f-1m3f maiden (RPR 68), closely related to 7f-2m2f Flat/2m4f hurdle winner Flint Hill, out of 1m6f winner; has plenty of stamina on the dam's side of his pedigree; others preferred.
runners.comment: Â£5,000 yearling; has plenty of stamina on the dam's side of his pedigree; others preferred

runners.name: State Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Â£20,000 yearling; half-sister to French Listed-placed 6.5f/7f winner Summer Sail (RPR 102); dam unraced half-sister to 6f-1m winner Beau Jardine, out of Listed-placed 7f/1m winner; stable operates at around 7% with its 2yos; watch the market.
runners.comment: Â£20,000 yearling; stable operates at around 7% with its 2yos; watch the market

runners.name: Adonius
runners.spotlight: Â£32,000 yearling; Soldier's Call half-brother to winners Golden Paradise (5f 2yo; RPR 71) and For Fake (Italian 5f-6f 2yo); dam placed four times at 5f (65), half-sister to winners Mother Country (7f/1m) and Banddar (5f 2yo); already gelded; stable has a modest strike-rate with 2yos.
runners.comment: Â£32,000 yearling by Soldier's Call; stable has a modest strike-rate with 2yos

runners.name: Katies Knight
runners.spotlight: Â£15,000 yearling; by Cotai Glory; dam placed 7f/1m AW (RPR 65), half-sister to French winners Harmless (7f 2yo/1m2f Listed), Ghabnah (1m) and Samuraj (6f-1m); early foal, already gelded; stable has its share of 2yo winners and Rossa Ryan is a positive booking.
runners.comment: Early foal; stable has its share of 2yo winners and Rossa Ryan is a positive booking

runners.name: Super Alpha
runners.spotlight: 45,000euros yearling; by Bated Breath; brother to 6f 2yo winner Juliet Sierra (including Group 3; RPR 98), closely related to two winners including Juliet Foxtrot (7f-9.5f including 2yo/US Grade 1; 109), half-brother to four winners; dam 7.5f/1m winner (including 2yo/Listed; 106); fits the bill on pedigree; interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: 45,000euros yearling; by Bated Breath; fits the bill on pedigree; interesting newcomer

runners.name: River Jordan
runners.spotlight: Â£9,000 yearling; Ubettabelieveit half-brother to winners Evening Song (6f-1m1f including 2yo; RPR 77), Offiah's Boy (1m2f/11.6f; 64) and Larrsen (1m; 61); drawn on the rail; yard 8-100 with 2yos in last five seasons.
runners.comment: Â£9,000 yearling; drawn on the rail; yard 8-100 with 2yos in last five seasons

races.name: Livescore Bet A Total Betting Experience Handicap

runners.name: Thecoffeepoddotco
runners.spotlight: 0-7 on turf but none of them were in handicaps and she's proved consistent on AW since, winning two 5f Lingfield races at the start of the year; needs a bit more for the return to turf, especially if the ground eases, but sharp tracks clearly suit and her fitness is a plus; yard also runs Wheels Of Fire.
runners.comment: In good form on AW; needs a bit more back on turf but track should suit and fitness a plus

runners.name: Water Of Leith
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner, most recently on Wolverhampton AW last month; finished a respectable sixth of 12 in this race last year but overall track record doesn't scream course specialist and he needs a lot to go right.
runners.comment: Prolific winner, latest on AW in March; plenty to find on most C&D efforts
runners.owner: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing

runners.name: Ziggys Triton
runners.spotlight: Winner of one of two C&D starts (good; has more to prove on slower) in August; two AW wins in December torpedoed his mark and he's 12lb higher than his latest turf run in October but he isn't a forlorn hope to transfer the winter improvement back to grass; the less rain the better.
runners.comment: 1-2 over C&D; 12lb higher than latest turf run following AW wins; may yet improve on grass

runners.name: Wheels Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Has spent most of his handicapping life over 6f, as when winning at Windsor in August, but he didn't lack for pace when close up in competitive 5f handicaps at Goodwood (good and heavy) last year; can come on for recent front-running AW return; has prospects for trainer who's 4-13 here.
runners.comment: Most handicaps over 6f but fair mark and two good thirds in 5f races at Goodwood last year

runners.name: Montezuma
runners.spotlight: Just five handicap runs under his young belt, doing well to get as close as he did after a very slow start in the first of them; raced at mostly that 6f trip after and had to be chivvied along early in sole 5f run, when a close C&D third; nevertheless looks worth another go at 5f and not ruled out.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo; creditable close third over C&D in sole 5f start; not ruled out
runners.owner: B Jordan Brian Jordan S Jordan & N McConnell

runners.name: Zarzyni
runners.spotlight: Below best on latest course start in October but has a strong C&D record otherwise, three wins include this race (good to soft) last year; now 2lb lower and primed for a good run if he avoids trouble in running.
runners.comment: Strong C&D record with three wins, including this race last year; primed to go well

runners.name: Al Hussar
runners.spotlight: Copes well with different ground surfaces; all four wins at 5f but although he has won after a short break he took time to find his form in 2025; usually wears blinkers nowadays but not this time; possibly other days.
runners.comment: Acts on all ground; four 5f wins; took time to find his form in 2025; possibly other days

runners.name: Classy Al
runners.spotlight: Won three 5f handicaps (all on good) last summer; finished a good third to a stablemate in the 2024 running but he's often none too bright at the stalls, which has counted against him over C&D, and he may need this anyway.
runners.comment: Good 3rd in the 2024 running but slow starts have often counted against him over C&D

runners.name: Woohoo
runners.spotlight: Four sprint wins in 2025 on good to firm and good (also acts on good to soft) and she would have been close up in a Racing League race at Windsor in August but for some serious interference; not seen after one later run that month; improvement may well continue in 2026.
runners.comment: Four sprint wins in 2025; may yet continue improvement but off since August

runners.name: Pop Star
runners.spotlight: Has not distinguished himself on Newcastle AW for new yard but returns to turf on a healthy mark; second to Ziggy's Triton over C&D (good) in August and has big turnaround at the weights; drawn widest but revival on the cards.
runners.comment: Low-key AW season for new yard but on a healthy mark and ran well over C&D in August

runners.name: The Bell Conductor
runners.spotlight: Ought to be well treated off a 19lb lower mark than last spring but he's been treading water on the AW this winter; made the running last time out (first-time visor, now goes in cheekpieces); questions linger.
runners.comment: Dropped 19lb since last spring but treading water now and questions remain

runners.name: Arnhem
runners.spotlight: Good turf spell in the autumn included a winning yard debut at Haydock (good) and a close second over C&D (soft); has run well on AW since but looks to have his share of weight; trainer runs five and won this in 2024.
runners.comment: Made a winning yard debut in the autumn, later close up over C&D; tough off current mark

runners.name: Curious Rover
runners.spotlight: Off since November but he was a very good runner-up on his reappearance over C&D last April (good; has won on soft) and later won here in August; lost his way subsequently but he's 2lb lower than the summer win and looks a likely type for this race.
runners.comment: Good 2nd over C&D on return in 2025; C&D winner in August; now 2lb lower
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

races.name: Peter Sunderland Cheltenham Certainty Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: First Candidate
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who is 0-4 over hurdles but has run well to finish second on last two starts (2m, soft/good to soft), most recently in a big-field handicap at Kelso; leading claims for last year's winning yard.
runners.comment: Point winner; runner-up in big field on handicap debut at Kelso and has leading claims

runners.name: Atlantic City
runners.spotlight: 0-9 in Ireland for Ciaran Murphy and unraced since last October, but she's finished third over hurdles on four occasions (2m-2m4f, various ground), showing a fair level of ability in the process; makes her British debut with her new trainer in fine form and could be involved.
runners.comment: 0-9 in Ireland but has shown ability and makes British debut with new trainer in fine form

runners.name: Cappa Lass
runners.spotlight: 8l runner-up in Irish mares' maiden point (3m, good to yielding) last April on second start and sold for 30,000euros in May; this half-sister to useful 2m hurdle winner Cottesloe Sunshine has Harry Cobden booked for her rules debut, for which she wears a hood; the betting may be informative.
runners.comment: Point runner-up who makes rules debut with Harry Cobden booked; betting may be informative

runners.name: Flash Bardot
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat for Jack Channon up to autumn 2024; however, her form went the wrong way last year and she was sold for 10,000gns in December; needs to turn things around on this hurdle/stable debut
runners.comment: Useful on the Flat in 2024 but form went wrong way last year; changed hands for 10,000gns

runners.name: Dolly Drama
runners.spotlight: Encouraging third on debut in Wetherby bumper (2m, soft) in November and similar form when fourth of seven at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) last month; this half-sister to the stable's smart performer Minella Drama is open to improvement and could have an each-way chance on hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Made the frame in both bumpers and she could have an each-way chance on hurdle debut

runners.name: Pink Walls
runners.spotlight: 0-9 on the Flat for Lisa Williamson and achieved very little; makes hurdle/stable debut after a long absence and she would be a shock winner.
runners.comment: 0-9 on the Flat and achieved very little; makes hurdle/stable debut after a long absence

runners.name: Diamond Grace
runners.spotlight: Backed into 11-1 from 18-1 when soundly beaten fifth of eight on debut in bumper at Warwick (2m, soft) in February; this half-sister to Scottish National winner Macdermott may be one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in her sole bumper and she may be one for further down the line

runners.name: Rose Light
runners.spotlight: Very modest on the Flat nowadays and tailed off last month on her first two hurdle starts; a transformation is needed.
runners.comment: Very modest on the Flat nowadays and tailed off last month on her first two hurdle starts

races.name: Pertemps Network Challenger Two Mile Hurdle Series Final Handicap Hurdle (Series Final) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Mr McLoughlan
runners.spotlight: Novice who won three times in the autumn (2m, good to soft/good) and ran well when third behind two unexposed rivals at Stratford (2m, soft) last month; a strong pace seems likely, which should see him to good effect.
runners.comment: Third behind two unexposed rivals at Stratford; strong pace should see him to good effect

runners.name: Williethebuilder
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row at Kempton (2m, good) in the winter before another good run when fourth at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft), where he would have been a bit closer but for late interference; soft ground may not have been ideal in the County Hurdle three weeks ago; might not be far away.
runners.comment: Improved this winter; soft ground may not have suited at Cheltenham; might not be far away

runners.name: Ice In The Veins
runners.spotlight: Made the running when winning two novice hurdles in November (2m3f/2m, good); ridden with restraint and pulled hard when failing to threaten in February in his first two handicaps (2m, good to soft/soft); could yet be capable of considerably better for his top yard, and a return to more prominent tactics might be the catalyst for an improved performance.
runners.comment: Pulled hard when no threat in first two handicaps; could yet be capable of much better

runners.name: Marche dAligre
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice in a row at the end of last year (2m, soft) then tailed off at Ascot (2m3f, soft); following wind surgery he took a step back in the right direction at Newbury (2m, good to soft) in February but was still soundly beaten, and very much needs to build on that latest effort.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction at Newbury in February but very much needs to build on it
runners.owner: Billam Roe Swindell Wilson & The Molds

runners.name: Frontier Prince
runners.spotlight: He needs to find something extra on his last three performances but this novice hurdle winner (good to soft) is now 2lb lower than when third of 15 on handicap debut at Cheltenham (2m, soft) in November, and perhaps he'll be aided by the first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Needs to better his winter form but perhaps this 5yo will be aided by the tongue-tie

runners.name: Tashan
runners.spotlight: Despite failing to settle he showed a good attitude to win a 4yo handicap at Exeter (2m, good to soft) in November; pulled up at Ascot in December but that was his first run on soft going and he bled from the nose; very lightly raced and could be suited by the big-field scenario; retains potential.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced 4yo who could be suited by the big-field scenario; retains potential
runners.owner: The High Altitude Partners & A Lavelle

runners.name: Smart Decision
runners.spotlight: Won four of his five starts in 2025 and went close off 1lb higher than today over C&D (good to soft) in January; below par on last two starts but the 2m5f trip then soft ground are possible excuses; no surprise if he bounces back with a good performance in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D in January; possible excuses on last two starts; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Dance And Glance
runners.spotlight: 6yo who has shown improved form this season, winning at Ascot (1m7f, soft) in December then runner-up at Sandown (2m, good); well-beaten fifth in the gruelling William Hill Hurdle at Newbury (2m, heavy) in February; has had a break since and could play a leading role if back to his best.
runners.comment: Improved form this term and can be excused latest run in the gruelling William Hill Hurdle

runners.name: Inedit dAmour
runners.spotlight: Ex-French 5yo who won a maiden hurdle easily at Catterick (1m7f, good) on New Year's Day on his third British start; however, soundly beaten in two handicaps since (2m3f/2m1f, good to soft) and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Easily won maiden hurdle at Catterick in January but soundly beaten in two handicaps since

runners.name: Were Red And Blue
runners.spotlight: Won at Huntingdon (2m, soft) in December and has continued in good form, finishing second to Williethebuilder at Kempton (2m, good) then third of 18 in the Imperial Cup at Sandown (2m, soft); solid claims.
runners.comment: Good form continued when third in the Imperial Cup at Sandown and he holds solid claims

runners.name: Bucephalus
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who was second over C&D (good) in December and bounced back from being pulled up in the William Hill Hurdle at Newbury (heavy) to make all in four-runner handicap at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft); has a career-high mark in this hotter race but he did it comfortably last time; could make a bold bid.
runners.comment: Career-high mark but has run really well (including over C&D) on two of last three starts

races.name: Pertemps Network Challenger Stayers Hurdle Series Final Handicap Hurdle (Series Final) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Blue Carpet
runners.spotlight: Made a solid start to hurdling career on good ground; second on handicap debut at Newbury in November and went one better next time in taking fashion at Wetherby (again about 3m on soft) in February; raised 7lb but it's easy to think he has significantly more to give.
runners.comment: Raised 7lb but it's easy to think he has significantly more to give after his Wetherby win

runners.name: Secret Trix
runners.spotlight: 6-26 over hurdles, including a win with first-time cheekpieces in this race in 2024 (soft) and he is 1lb lower today; ridden by 7lb claimer last four outings, with a big return to some form on latest (second at Market Rasen) and that has been followed by his second wind operation; cheekpieces (absent on last four starts) return; too well handicapped to be ignored.
runners.comment: Won this race in 2024; 1lb lower mark today; back in some form latest; wind surgery since

runners.name: Jakar Du Moulin
runners.spotlight: Given wind surgery before handicapping on last three starts, on last two winning at Ascot (2m7f) in December and Doncaster (3m, again good to soft; from I Am Max) three weeks ago; the latter was by 7l from the front, since which he's been handed an 8lb rise but this 7yo retains potential.
runners.comment: Handicapping last three starts, on last two winning at Ascot and Doncaster; latter by 7l

runners.name: Heather Honey
runners.spotlight: Won three in a row last April-June (about 2m7f, good) and resumed her progress with convincing wins at Catterick (3m1f, good) in November and Haydock (3m, good to soft) one month ago; raised another 6lb and in grade but such a progressive mare cannot be dismissed.
runners.comment: Raised another 6lb and in grade after a convincing C&D win but she's progressing well

runners.name: Porter In The Park
runners.spotlight: Never better than when placed under Cameron Johnstone-Baker at Ascot (2m7f), Kempton (3m, again on good) and Warwick (3m2f, good to soft; behind Super Survivor) on last three outings; a bit extra is needed to win this warm race.
runners.comment: Never better when placed under Cameron Johnstone-Baker last three runs; needs a bit extra

runners.name: I Am Max
runners.spotlight: Short-headed on his only start here; won a Hexham chase in October; hasn't done as well since, finishing runner-up to Jakar Du Moulin when returned to hurdling at Doncaster (3m) on latest start but he needs to build on that markedly; stable in good form.
runners.comment: Second to Jakar Du Moulin when returned to hurdling at Doncaster (3m); stable in good form

runners.name: Ernest Gray
runners.spotlight: Did well in cheekpieces last year, winning at Worcester (2m7f, good) in May and Aintree (3m, good) in October; not so good behind Super Survivor here (good to soft) in December but a return to form today looks possible for in-form yard; place hopes.
runners.comment: Won two starts back; a return to form looks possible for in-form yard; place hopes

runners.name: Walden
runners.spotlight: Won a good-ground maiden last April and two of his three handicaps this winter; latest was in 18-runner race at Ascot (2m7f, good to soft) seven weeks ago in which he travelled well and asserted on the run-in to score by just over 6l; 8lb higher today but more in the tank; major player.
runners.comment: 2-3 in handicaps; stylish when taking 18-runner race at Ascot (2m7f, good to soft) latest

runners.name: Super Survivor
runners.spotlight: Back in good form this winter, including on last two starts (good to soft) when he prevailed narrowly over C&D in December and battled back for a clearcut win at Warwick (3m2f; from Porter In the Park) four weeks ago; raised another 4lb but the return to a sharper test is more of a worry.
runners.comment: C&D win in December; battled back for a clearcut win at Warwick (3m2f) on latest outing

races.name: Unibet Middle Distance Veterans' Handicap Chase (Series Final)

runners.name: Triple Trade
runners.spotlight: Two wins this season (2m/2m4f, soft/good) and 10l third behind an 8yo and a 7yo at Cheltenham (2m4f, soft) in January; 20l fourth in another competitive handicap at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft) in February but that form is working out well and he's back against fellow veterans today; chance.
runners.comment: Two wins this season and last two defeats came against younger rivals in tough handicaps

runners.name: Filanderer
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in the Ultima at Cheltenham last month but that was his first attempt at a staying trip and he failed to settle out wide; went to the Festival having won five of his previous six starts (2m3f-2m5f, various ground) and he could bounce back with a big effort now back down in trip and grade.
runners.comment: Pulled up in the Ultima at Cheltenham but highly progressive previously; could bounce back

runners.name: Lord Baddesley
runners.spotlight: Easily won three-runner handicap at Newbury (2m4f, good) on reappearance; jumped left when 6l third of four at right-handed Ascot (2m5f, good) next time, and his 13l sixth of nine at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft) came in a race that worked out extremely well; heavy going excuses him being tailed off at Warwick and he could go well now back on better ground.
runners.comment: Some good runs this season and heavy ground excuses latest effort; could go well

runners.name: Le Milos
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in the Stayers' Veterans' Final at Sandown (3m, good) in January two starts ago and he's not ruled out today; however, he's been inconsistent this season and was soundly beaten at Warwick (2m4f, heavy) in February; he may prefer a longer trip than this nowadays; fourth in this last year.
runners.comment: Runner-up in Stayers' Veterans' Final in January but soundly beaten at Warwick since

runners.name: Outlaw Peter
runners.spotlight: Sent off favourite at Exeter (2m3f, good to soft) four weeks ago and returned to form with 1l second to Genois, finishing clear of the remainder; wears first-time cheekpieces and he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Returned to form with 1l second to Genois at Exeter and he's on the shortlist
runners.owner: The Stewart Family Dench Ferguson & Mason

runners.name: The Flier Begley
runners.spotlight: Won this last year (good) and followed up at Market Rasen (2m5f, good) in June off a mark 1lb higher than today's; pulled up/tailed off over fences on next two starts but his recent outing over hurdles was fairly encouraging and he could build on that now back chasing.
runners.comment: Won this last year; ran okay in recent novice hurdle; could build on it now back chasing

runners.name: Genois
runners.spotlight: Won at Wetherby (2m3f, soft) on Boxing Day before good second there (2m3f, soft) in January, and he returned to winning ways at Exeter (2m3f, good to soft) four weeks ago when clear with Outlaw Tom; this rejuvenated 10yo still has mileage in his mark and is firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Rejuvenated 10yo who has won two of his last three and is firmly in calculations

runners.name: Minella Drama
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent nowadays but ran well in second when facing an impossible mission behind Protektorat at Kelso (2m7f, good to soft) last time; now 5lb lower than when third in the Old Roan Chase at Aintree (2m4f, good to soft) in October and this C&D winner is capable of being involved.
runners.comment: Inconsistent nowadays but has dropped to a handy mark and he ran well last time

runners.name: Dreal Deal
runners.spotlight: Irish challenger who could be in the picture judged on his good form at Killarney last summer, and he's one to watch in the betting; however, he's been soundly beaten on his last three starts and has to get back on track.
runners.comment: Irish challenger who is one to watch in the betting, but soundly beaten on last three runs
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Philip Smith & Nigel OHare & P J Rothwell

runners.name: Neon Moon
runners.spotlight: He won a four-runner handicap at Ascot (2m5f, good) in November but has failed to kick on subsequently, proving no match for Genois and Outlaw Peter when fifth at Exeter (2m3f, good to soft) four weeks ago.
runners.comment: Won in a small field at Ascot in November but has failed to kick on subsequently

runners.name: Courtland
runners.spotlight: He ran well in defeat in the main last year and is effective when fresh; could give a good account on this first start since December but there's been minimal movement in his mark this season and he may again prove vulnerable in this competitive contest.
runners.comment: Ran well in defeat in the main last year but minimal movement in his mark and this is warm

runners.name: Whistleinthedark
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent last year but won twice (3m/2m4f, good/soft), those victories coming on the back of respective breaks of 89 and 120 days; may be best caught fresh nowadays so is worth a second look after 113 days off.
runners.comment: Inconsistent but his last two wins have come off the back of similar breaks to this one

runners.name: The Widdow Maker
runners.spotlight: Won in a small field at Exeter (2m3f, good) last March before 17l sixth in this race (good); he's run well in defeat on both starts this season but needs something extra if he's to come out on top today.
runners.comment: 17l sixth in this last year; in good form on both outings this season but needs extra

runners.name: Guard Your Dreams
runners.spotlight: Brought down at the first fence in the Plate at Cheltenham last month; made most of the running when winning easily at Warwick (2m4f, heavy) in February and, although now 8lb higher, he was rated higher over hurdles earlier in his career; versatile ground-wise; one to note.
runners.comment: Won easily at Warwick in February and brought down at first fence at Cheltenham last month

runners.name: Dubai Days
runners.spotlight: His last six wins have come in the months of February, March or April and he returned to form to win at Ayr (2m4f, soft) 40 days ago; runner-up in this race two years ago (soft) and won over C&D (good to soft) last February; that latest win reads well and a 4lb rise isn't harsh; possible player.
runners.comment: This is his time of year and he won at Ayr 40 days ago; runner-up in this race in 2024

runners.name: Fugitif
runners.spotlight: Winless since December 2023 and hasn't posed a serious threat this season; however, he was best of the rest behind easy winner Guard Your Dreams at Warwick (2m4f, heavy) last time and continues to drop down the weights; handles various ground; not ruled out in first-time visor (replacing cheekpieces).
runners.comment: Best of the rest behind easy winner Guard Your Dreams at Warwick; mark continues to fall

runners.name: Numitor
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in this race last year and same outcome on first two runs this season; 13l fourth behind Genois at Exeter (2m3f, good to soft) last month following wind surgery and a break but he could improve for that outing, and he won this race in 2024; not written off.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction at Exeter and could take another today; won this race in 2024
runners.owner: Paul G Jacobs And Wetumpka Racing

races.name: JCB Fastrac Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Heads Or Harps
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a 13l win at Ayr (2m, soft) and has finished second there on three of his five outings this term, including over an extended 2m4f the last twice; ought to be competitive again.
runners.comment: 2m Ayr win last spring and second there three times this term, including at around 2m4f

runners.name: Imperial Bede
runners.spotlight: Evidently not the force of old but he built on an encouraging C&D reappearance when winning in first-time cheekpieces at Catterick (2m3f, soft) in February; made early mistakes when a disappointing favourite at Hereford since but it's possible he'll be happier back on a left-handed course and likely he's still on a winnable mark if putting his best foot forward.
runners.comment: Catterick win in February; poor run since but good shout if putting best foot forward

runners.name: Count Adhemar
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 6yo; improved form since switched to fences, finishing second in 2m handicaps at Ludlow (soft) and Newcastle (good to soft) in recent months; the return to a longer trip won't need to unlock much improvement.
runners.comment: Good second in 2m handicaps in recent months; won't need to improve much for longer trip

runners.name: I Shut That dOr
runners.spotlight: His form took off with three wins over fences last winter; however, his good run came to an abrupt halt at Stratford last March and he hasn't been seen since; no great record fresh to date but the betting is perhaps the best guide to his chance.
runners.comment: Three wins last winter but off since stopping quickly at Stratford 390 days ago

runners.name: Royal Deeside
runners.spotlight: Successful twice over 2m4f at Hexham (good) this season; bounced back from a couple of poor runs when third of 13 at Carlisle (2m4f, good to soft) 13 days ago but his rather in-and-out record of late suggests he's no good thing to back that up.
runners.comment: Two Hexham wins this season; back in form when third latest but isn't the most consistent

runners.name: Bonza Boy
runners.spotlight: Added to his strong chase record at Exeter with a win over the extended 2m1f there (good to soft) in January; just as good when runner-up over 2m4f on heavy at Wincanton since; bold show likely off the same mark.
runners.comment: Another Exeter win in January and just as good when second at Wincanton since; a player

runners.name: Victoria Milano
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on some of her old hurdle form for Alastair Ralph but she looked held when falling in a 2m5f handicap hurdle here on her reappearance in January; down the field at Ludlow since and hard to know what to expect back chasing (well beaten on previous attempt).
runners.comment: Well held in two hurdles runs for new yard and well beaten in previous outing over fences

races.name: JCB Hydrogen Novices' Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Big Storm Brewing
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the tenth attempt, at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) last month, and followed up at Musselburgh (3m, good to soft); climbing the weights but clearly progressive and unexposed over this trip.
runners.comment: Up in the weights but clearly progressive having won his last two; unexposed at the trip

runners.name: Brookside La
runners.spotlight: Won on handicap debut at Wetherby (2m3f, soft) on Boxing Day before 4l third of 17 here (2m5f, good to soft) in January, when shaping as though this longer trip could suit; in excellent hands to continue to progress.
runners.comment: Kept on well over 2m5f here in January and this longer trip could suit

runners.name: Mighty Fleur
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two bumpers and three novice hurdles; disappointing in novice hurdle at Uttoxeter in December and well beaten on chase/handicap debut at Newcastle in January; return to form needed now back hurdling.
runners.comment: Has shown promise but needs to bounce back from two lesser shows (chase debut last time)

runners.name: Springtime Legend
runners.spotlight: Made all with something to spare at Hereford (2m5f, heavy) in February on second handicap start and another good run when third at Wincanton (3m, good to soft) last month; might not be far away.
runners.comment: Won at Hereford in February and good third at Wincanton since; might not be far away

runners.name: Times Are Green
runners.spotlight: Just the one good run in his three novice hurdles but that was when 11l second at Chepstow (2m7f, good to soft) in December; early faller when 18-1 for recent handicap debut at Newbury (3m, good to soft); his stable is in excellent form and he could run well in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Early faller when 18-1 on handicap debut; yard is in excellent form; cheekpieces go on

runners.name: Kenzo Des Bruyeres
runners.spotlight: Easily won two Irish points (3m, good to yielding/soft) last autumn; soundly beaten when fourth at Ffos Las (2m4f, heavy) on his two hurdle starts but he may yet be capable of better; makes handicap debut.
runners.comment: Two-time point winner; soundly beaten on both hurdle starts but may be capable of better

runners.name: Largy Go
runners.spotlight: Won sole Irish point last March (3m, yielding to soft), after which he was sold for Â£305,000; placed on his first two hurdle starts (2m, heavy/soft) this year; his dam's wins came over 2m7f/3m and he could relish this step up in trip on handicap debut; has obvious potential off opening mark.
runners.comment: Point winner who was placed on first two hurdle starts and could relish this longer trip

runners.name: Kadjar Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Signs of promise in novice hurdles (including on good to soft) and runner-up over fences in handicap at Doncaster (3m, soft) in February; reverts to hurdles having unseated over fences last month; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Runner-up off this mark over fences in February; not ruled out now back over hurdles

runners.name: Lawtan
runners.spotlight: Stayed on strongly to win a 4yo chase in France (2m3f, good to firm) last October; 33-1 when 14l fifth on stable debut in novice hurdle at Uttoxeter (2m4f, soft) three weeks ago; the step up in trip may be a plus on this first handicap start and he's not discounted.
runners.comment: Stayed on strongly for 4yo chase win in France over 2m3f; not discounted on handicap debut

races.name: JCB Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Ski Lodge
runners.spotlight: Has a win and a second (over C&D) from two runs here; bit disappointing at Wetherby five weeks ago after looking dangerous early in the straight, but cheekpieces and a return to Haydock could see a revival.
runners.comment: Bit disappointing last time but cheekpieces and a return to Haydock could see a revival
runners.owner: Noel Fehily Racing Syndicate - Ski Lodge

runners.name: Solar System
runners.spotlight: Four wins (2m1f-2m5f) over hurdles; made an encouraging start over fences when 3l third of six at Southwell (3m, soft) last month; solid claims if building on that run with cheekpieces now fitted.
runners.comment: 4-14 over hurdles; pleasing start over fences and solid claims if building on that run

runners.name: Major Fortune
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner over hurdles; just as good over fences and holding his form well, beaten off only on run-in when second at Leicester (2m6f; stays 3m1f well) four weeks ago; handles any ground; leading contender.
runners.comment: Prolific hurdles winner, just as good over fences; second at Leicester latest; big player

runners.name: One Last Glance
runners.spotlight: Seems better than ever at the age of nine and he followed a 2m4f win at Leicester (heavy) with a good second there over 2m6f (good to soft); stays at least 3m and he receives lots of weight all round; respected.
runners.comment: Arrives in form after win and good second at Leicester; receives lots of weight all round
runners.owner: Fred Camis Ray Fielder & Mel Clarke

runners.name: Cerendipity
runners.spotlight: Well treated on his best form, now 9lb lower than when winning at Newcastle (2m7f, good to soft) in March 2025, but he has been struggling, including with his jumping; cheekpieces off, tongue-strap on.
runners.comment: 9lb lower than for last win but has been struggling; cheekpieces off, tongue-strap on

runners.name: Jupiter Des Bordes
runners.spotlight: Looks an out-and-out stayer; ground out victory at Warwick (3m, soft) in December and ran well over 3m4f here next time, but he was well below par on latest start and others appeal more.
runners.comment: Ran well over 3m4f here in December but well below par last time and others appeal more

runners.name: Saladins Son
runners.spotlight: Most consistent since chasing debut, twice making the frame over C&D (good to soft/soft) and again running creditably when a close third of six at Wincanton (3m1f, heavy); hard to knock and should be thereabouts again.
runners.comment: Running well this term, including over C&D; hard to knock and should be thereabouts again

runners.name: Jacks Parrot
runners.spotlight: Creditable fourth in the Tommy Whittle over C&D (soft) just before Christmas and has run to a similar level both starts since; no obvious reason why he won't be thereabouts again but he'll need something extra to win.
runners.comment: Fourth over C&D in December and creditable runs since; however, more required to win

runners.name: Laganhill
runners.spotlight: Dual soft-ground hurdle winner at around 2m; has looked a thorough stayer over fences, 4l second of four over 3m at Exeter (heavy; first-time cheekpieces) six weeks ago; in the mix again off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: Second of four at Exeter six weeks ago; in the mix again off an unchanged mark

runners.name: If Not For Dylan
runners.spotlight: Veteran who retains enthusiasm, and he jumped well and made all at Doncaster (3m, good to soft) in February, but he's back up 6lb and probably in a stronger race here.
runners.comment: Veteran who retains enthusiasm; jumped well and made all at Doncaster; back up 6lb

runners.name: Fortunedefortunata
runners.spotlight: Mostly campaigned in competitive handicap chases when with Gordon Elliott, and his form has fallen off a cliff; changed hands for Â£3,000 in February; needs to be revitalised by a switch of stables.
runners.comment: Badly out of form in the last 12 months and needs to be revitalised by a change of stables

runners.name: My Bobby Dazzler
runners.spotlight: Put everything together when winning at Hereford (3m1f, soft) six weeks ago under a positive ride from Toby McCain-Mitchell; not discounted but he's up 5lb and this is a stronger race.
runners.comment: Put everything together when winning at Hereford; up 5lb and in a stronger race

races.name: Cumberland Show Saturday 6th June Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tap Tap Shamie
runners.spotlight: Beat a subsequent winner when landing an Irish point in November (3m, yielding to soft); bought for Â£48,000 soon after; bettered that when finishing runner-up in an 18-runner Wincanton novice hurdle (1m7f, good to soft) on stable debut in February (third has won since); steps up in trip here and looks the one to beat.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; good second on stable/hurdles debut; 2m3f should suit; one to beat

runners.name: Maccarellu
runners.spotlight: Ex-French Flat winner; fair third of four at Leicester on stable debut in November (1m7f, soft) but well-beaten sixth at Huntingdon last time (2m4f, soft); place chance.
runners.comment: Ex-French; fair third on stable debut but well beaten over 2m4f last time; place chance

runners.name: Last Man Standing
runners.spotlight: Third in a Newcastle bumper in December (2m1f, good to soft) but has finished last on his other three starts, including on last month's hurdles debut (80-1).
runners.comment: Third in a bumper on second start; last in his other 3 races, including on hurdles debut

runners.name: Aristelle
runners.spotlight: Fair third of six in a bumper here in December (2m1f, soft) but well beaten either side of that, last time on Tapeta at Newcastle; plenty to prove on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Third of six in a bumper here in December; well beaten either side of that; hurdles debut

runners.name: Dream On Mick
runners.spotlight: Bought for Â£42,000 in January after winning an Irish maiden point (3m, soft); quite interesting on hurdles/stable debut.
runners.comment: Bought Â£42,000 after Irish point win in January; quite interesting on hurdles/stable debut

runners.name: McElhattan
runners.spotlight: Bought for Â£42,000 after Irish point win in November; hasn't shown much in a bumper or two races over hurdles; cheekpieces now tried
runners.comment: Â£42,000 buy after Irish point win in November; modest efforts under rules; headgear tried

runners.name: Jet Noir
runners.spotlight: Creditable third in an Irish point in January (3m, good/yielding); well beaten in a Newcastle bumper on stable debut last month (12-1); best watched for now.
runners.comment: Irish point third who was well beaten in a bumper on stable debut; best watched for now

runners.name: The Grey Venture
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on fourth start in Irish points in November (3m, yielding); well-beaten 11th of 12 in a Newcastle novice hurdle (2m4f, good to soft) on rules/ stable debut; much more needed.
runners.comment: Irish point winner in November but needs to improve markedly on his first run over hurdles

runners.name: The Shanachie
runners.spotlight: Fair sixth of nine in a Kelso bumper (2m, heavy) on debut but was well beaten on his first run over hurdles at Newcastle three weeks ago (2m1f, good to soft; 22-1); longer trip should suit but more needed.
runners.comment: Fair run in a Kelso bumper on debut; well beaten on his hurdles debut but 2m3f should suit

runners.name: Patricks Wells
runners.spotlight: Beat a couple of decent sorts when winning an Irish point in April 2023 (3m, good) and was subsequently bought for Â£30,000; tailed off when a 25-1 shot for a Wetherby novice hurdle later that year and has been off since; best watched after such a long break.
runners.comment: Won a decent Irish point in April 2023; probably best watched after 882-day break

races.name: Feel Good Friday 29th May Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: I Wish You
runners.spotlight: Consistent; battled on well to beat a subsequent winner by a short head in a Wetherby novice handicap chase in February (3m, soft); now 7lb higher; could go well but may be better on softer ground.
runners.comment: Game winner at Wetherby in February; up 7lb; a possible but may prefer softer ground

runners.name: Orkney Blue
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D handicap on good to soft when back from a break in February; excuses for lesser run last time (heat stress); could bounce back.
runners.comment: Won C&D handicap on good to soft in February; excuses for lesser run last time; chance

runners.name: You Some Girl
runners.spotlight: Won a three-runner handicap at Newcastle on chase debut in October (2m7f, good); no easy task when pulled up in Lincolnshire National last time; unexposed as a chaser and should improve.
runners.comment: Won 3-runner race on chase debut; stiff task when pulled up last time; should progress

runners.name: Eaton Anne
runners.spotlight: Improved on first chase run when second of four at Wetherby last time (3m, good to soft; cheekpieces first time, wasn't fluent); blinkers now tried; quite interesting.
runners.comment: Improved on chase debut when 2nd of four at Wetherby last time; new headgear; a possible

runners.name: Big Zouk
runners.spotlight: Winning pointer on soft/heavy; off the mark on chase debut at Ayr in January (3m, heavy) but pulled up at Kelso last time; could go well but drier ground a slight concern.
runners.comment: Winning pointer; off the mark on chase debut in January; pulled up last time; best on soft

runners.name: Benmore
runners.spotlight: Two wins in small-field chases this season, the second over 2m5f here last month (good to soft); up 2lb; stamina to prove on first run at this trip.
runners.comment: Two wins this term, the latest over 2m5f here last month; chance but first run at 3m

runners.name: Loulou Madrik
runners.spotlight: Placed on two of his six runs over hurdles (tried in different headgear on last two runs); had four runs without winning when pointing in Ireland and perhaps best watched in quite a competitive race on his chase debut.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish pointer; placed twice over hurdles but has no easy task on chase debut

races.name: Bell & Plate 24th June Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Ballin Bay
runners.spotlight: Has won his last three races, all at Sedgefield, two 3m3f handicap hurdles and last time (second run with cheekpieces) a 3m2f handicap chase; these conditions fine and should go well again despite career-high mark (5lb higher than last win).
runners.comment: Has won two hurdles and a chase at Sedgefield on last three starts; should go well again

runners.name: Dr Seb
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over 2m7f at Uttoxeter in the summer of 2024 (good); was then off for 587 days and fair sixth at Wincanton on return to action last month (3m, good to soft; form not worked out); should come on for that run.
runners.comment: Two Uttoxeter wins in summer of 2024; then off 587 days; should improve for latest run

runners.name: Ramo
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since Ludlow success in November 2023 (2m5f, good to soft); two fair runs over this sort of trip at Market Rasen and Ffos Las in February and could go well again.
runners.comment: Last win was on good to soft in November 2023; two fair runs in February; could go well
runners.owner: C B Compton & L Di Franco

runners.name: Adveram
runners.spotlight: Beat Guernesey (2lb better off) by a neck at Hexham in December (2m7f, heavy) but pulled up both starts since (tried in new headgear last time); cheekpieces back on but plenty to prove at present.
runners.comment: Hexham winner in December but pulled up both starts since; plenty to prove now

runners.name: Kyntara
runners.spotlight: Useful staying handicap hurdler in his prime; fair run at Cheltenham on first two starts this season but not been at his best on last three starts.
runners.comment: Useful stayer in his prime; fair runs first two starts this term but below par since

runners.name: Guernesey
runners.spotlight: Has won just two of his 41 races over hurdles but nearly made it three when beaten a neck by Adveram (2lb worse off) at Hexham in December (2m7f, heavy); hasn't done as well since and was a long way behind Ballin Bay last time.
runners.comment: 2-41, but close 2nd at Hexham in December; plenty to find with Ballin Bay on recent form

runners.name: Cooler Than Me
runners.spotlight: Three sound runs since cheekpieces went on in February, winning at Market Rasen (2m4f, good to soft) and Taunton (2m3f, heavy) and finishing second at Taunton (2m3f, good to soft); interesting despite stepping up both in grade and in trip.
runners.comment: 3 sound runs in cheekpieces in February; interesting contender up in both grade and trip

runners.name: Robert dOres
runners.spotlight: Goes well fresh for he has won twice after a break over 2m7f at Hexham this season (good to soft); these conditions suit; should go well after another absence despite rise in grade and being 1lb out of the handicap.
runners.comment: Two wins at Hexham, both after a break this season; has had another break; should go well

runners.name: Jupiter Des Mottes
runners.spotlight: Won a Newcastle novice for Lucinda Russell last season (2m4f, soft); hasn't done quite as well since and was pulled up on first run back with Nicky Richards at Haydock in February after being hampered; capable of better than he has shown; chance
runners.comment: Newcastle novice winner last season; pulled up only hurdles run this term; better expected

runners.name: Maura Jeanne
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 3m-plus last season, the first time over 3m2f on good to soft at Kelso; has run a couple of fair races this term and has a chance on her best form with a visor replacing cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Two wins over 3m-plus last term; has run a couple of fair races this season; new headgear

runners.name: Rocheval
runners.spotlight: Won two juvenile hurdles two seasons ago, the second over 2m1f here (soft); fair form this season, including a decent fourth over C&D in December (soft); not sure this drier going will be ideal but could run into a place.
runners.comment: Two juvenile hurdle wins; fair form this term, including a fourth here; each-way chance

races.name: Ladies Night 4th July Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: King Kodiak
runners.spotlight: Outran 150-1 odds when placed in novice hurdle here (2m1f, soft) last month, his fifth hurdling start, but jumped poorly and was safely held in Sedgefield handicap (2m1f, good) two days later; effectively 7lb higher today and comes with risk; cheekpieces refitted.
runners.comment: Third in novice here in March; jumped poorly on handicap debut two days later; risky

runners.name: Sattam
runners.spotlight: Came good over hurdles at seventh attempt, with wide-margin success at Musselburgh (1m7f, good to soft) in February, and was not discredited when clear second under penalty at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) a few days later; not yet proven over this far but remains of strong interest.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner in February and clear second under penalty soon afterwards; respected

runners.name: Belvedere Bhoy
runners.spotlight: Never looked dangerous when fourth on last month's handicap debut at Ayr (2m, soft) but kept on in the straight and wasn't beaten far in the end; had wind surgery soon afterwards and now moves up in trip with Sean Bowen booked; should have more to offer.
runners.comment: Kept on over 2m on handicap debut; up in trip with Sean Bowen booked

runners.name: Blended Stealth
runners.spotlight: 1-38 over hurdles, the win gained at Hexham (2m, good) last May; his good days have become infrequent, though, and he's 3lb out of the weights today; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Just 1-38 over hurdles and his good days have become infrequent
runners.owner: D Mullen & Exors Of G Barker

runners.name: Gonnino
runners.spotlight: Lacked fluency on last month's chasing debut and now quickly reverts to hurdling; has claims if judged on his second off today's mark at Musselburgh (1m7f, soft) in December but his record is rather mixed.
runners.comment: Quickly back hurdling after underwhelming chase debut; has rather mixed record

runners.name: Rock N Roll Champ
runners.spotlight: Gave pretty good account before fading into fifth at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) on Tuesday but he's 0-11 over hurdles now and has to compete from just out of the weights.
runners.comment: Ran quite well at Newcastle on Tuesday but is 0-11 over hurdles now

runners.name: Kicksaftersix
runners.spotlight: In excellent form over fences last spring, winning three times at Hexham (3m, good/good to soft); can cope with drop back in trip for this first hurdling start since 2022 but has fitness to prove after 294-day layoff.
runners.comment: Triple 3m chase winner last spring; reverts to hurdling after 294-day absence

runners.name: Gone With The Tide
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles, having been pulled up on both outings this season; had wind op during 111-day break; others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: 0-8 over hurdles, having been pulled up on both outings this season; had wind op

runners.name: Takeyoutotheisland
runners.spotlight: Unable to land a significant blow in three 2m1f novice/maiden hurdles, and might have been flattered by proximity in tactical race here last time; that said, he's open to improvement over this new trip on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Unable to land a significant blow in novice company; up in trip for handicap debut

runners.name: Je Mr Dawson
runners.spotlight: Twice well beaten (2m1f/2m) since fairly encouraging seasonal/hurdle debut over C&D (soft) in November but moves back up in trip for this handicap debut and remains unexposed.
runners.comment: Yet to build upon his fairly encouraging hurdle debut but remains unexposed

races.name: Ladies Night 4th July Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Lord De Vinci
runners.spotlight: Chasing has not really gone to plan this year (unplaced all four attempts) but he shaped well when fourth in C&D hurdle (soft) on seasonal debut in December and is now 8lb lower; headgear removed; worth a second look.
runners.comment: Chasing has not gone to plan this year but he reverts to hurdling on a good mark

runners.name: Different Drum
runners.spotlight: Gained both Flat wins on the AW but arguably his best performance in that sphere was when going close here (2m1f, soft) last summer; kept on for respectable third on last month's handicap hurdle debut at Huntingdon (2m4f, good), and the return to this stiffer track ought to suit.
runners.comment: Placed on handicap hurdle debut at Huntingdon last month; this stiffer track ought to suit

runners.name: Knockeranna Rose
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at Sedgefield last month but made a fairly encouraging handicap debut when fourth over C&D (soft) two starts ago and, now 5lb lower, enters each-way calculations.
runners.comment: Well beaten last month but ran quite well over C&D on handicap debut two starts ago

runners.name: Out On Her Own
runners.spotlight: Safely held in first two handicaps (3m/2m5f, good) but returned from break with keeping on second here (2m1f, soft) last month and, on that evidence, this move back up in trip looks the right move.
runners.comment: Improved form when keeping-on second over 2m1f here last month; one to consider

runners.name: Shouldvebeenshav
runners.spotlight: Made a low-key start to her hurdling career but stuck to her task pretty well when fourth in 2m1f novice here (soft) last month and ought to be suited by the move back up in trip for this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Kept on well for fourth in 2m1f novice here last month; handicap debut today

runners.name: Blazing Court
runners.spotlight: Won a chase for a different stable in summer 2024 but has looked out of sorts over fences and hurdles this season; revival needed.
runners.comment: Out of sorts over fences and hurdles this season; revival needed

runners.name: Au Plaisir
runners.spotlight: Placed in big-field Irish bumper in September; unable to get seriously competitive in three maiden/novice hurdles for new stable this year but there was some promise at Newcastle (2m1f, good to soft) last time, and he should have more improvement to come in handicaps.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise in Newcastle maiden in February; can improve in handicaps

runners.name: Bray Dale
runners.spotlight: Placed at big odds on handicap debut at Ayr (2m, soft) last March but has not shown much this season (five runs); wore cheekpieces last time; visor tried today; 3lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Has not shown much this season and is 3lb out of the weights; new headgear tried

runners.name: Hobnail
runners.spotlight: Showed no significant promise when a big-priced outsider for three 2m novice/maiden hurdles in January/February; up in trip for handicap debut; 14lb out of the weights; significant improvement required.
runners.comment: Showed no significant promise in his qualifying runs; 14lb wrong on handicap debut

runners.name: Hang On
runners.spotlight: Tended to race too freely and showed only minor promise in three novice hurdles (2m3f-2m4f) towards end of 2023; makes handicap debut after mammoth absence and with a hood (worn on bumper debut) refitted; market helpful.
runners.comment: Showed only minor promise in novice hurdles in 2023; returns from mammoth absence

races.name: Flat Season Opener Monday 18th May Handicap Chase

runners.name: Jamesieconn
runners.spotlight: Held on gamely to make all in four-runner Doncaster chase (3m2f, good to soft) last month and is now 2-4 for current stable; has more on his plate here and needs to prove himself on a right-handed track but still respected in current form.
runners.comment: Gamely made all at Doncaster last month and is now 2-4 for current stable; respected

runners.name: Girls Best Friend
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo who scored on seasonal/handicap/chase debut at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good to soft) in November and arguably bettered that performance when a close second at Exeter (3m, good to soft) last month; still unexposed at around 3m; leading contender.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 7yo; 2m4f chase winner in November and close second over 3m last month

runners.name: Steal The Moves
runners.spotlight: Has mixed record over fences this season but was seemingly aided by first-time cheekpieces at Ffos Las (3m, heavy) in February, where he looked sure to win when nicely clear inside the final furlong but idled and was mugged close home; also suited by much quicker ground; no surprise if he goes well again.
runners.comment: Looked sure to win at Ffos Las in February but idled and was mugged close home

runners.name: Tory Hill
runners.spotlight: Back from layoff with three disappointing runs this season and now 0-4 over fences; needs combination of drier ground and first-time cheekpieces to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Badly out of form this season; needs first-time cheekpieces to make a big difference

runners.name: Don Brocco
