runners.spotlight: Belied 40-1 odds when keeping-on second (no threat to winner) at Hexham (4m, soft) last month and is also well suited by this track but is far too inconsistent to rely upon and would appeal more on soft/heavy ground.
runners.comment: Ran well in Hexham marathon last month but is too inconsistent to rely upon

runners.name: Voleur De Terres
runners.spotlight: Was beginning to look a lost cause but suddenly struck form with wins at Leicester (2m4f, heavy) and Warwick (3m, soft) in February; this race looks more competitive but further progress is possible if the forecast quicker ground suits.
runners.comment: Suddenly struck form with two slow-ground wins in February; further progress possible

runners.name: Kingofthegame
runners.spotlight: Ended 2024 in poor form over fences but returned from long absence with fairly encouraging hurdle run in January; remains unexposed at 3m and lightly raced in general; sports refitted cheekpieces; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Returned from long absence with fairly encouraging hurdle run in January; not ruled out

runners.name: Super Citizen
runners.spotlight: Long in the tooth now but won here (3m2f, soft; refitted cheekpieces) in the autumn and was placed over C&D (good to soft) on last two appearances, more recently when keeping on to snatch modest second in higher-grade company; ought to make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Won here (3m2f) in the autumn and was placed over C&D on last two appearances

runners.name: Baptism Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Useful for Henry de Bromhead in his prime but pulled up all five hurdle/chase runs since returning from long layoff for new yard last February.
runners.comment: Pulled up all five hurdle/chase runs since returning from long layoff last year

races.name: Book In Advance Save Â£5 Handicap Hurdle (Div I)

runners.name: Moyganny Phil
runners.spotlight: Got going too late when third on 2m Huntingdon handicap debut five weeks ago and this lightly raced 6yo rates a likely improver now stepping up in trip for his in-form stable.
runners.comment: Got going too late on 2m handicap debut, suggesting this trip will suit; yard flying

runners.name: Wind Your Neck In
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice in February (including C&D on soft) before making it 14th time lucky over hurdles in 2m4f Sedgefield handicap (good) last month; he and the runner-up came well clear of the third so he should competitive off 6lb higher.
runners.comment: Finally off mark over hurdles last month and should remain competitive off 6lb higher

runners.name: Two Brothers
runners.spotlight: Finished strongly to force a dead heat with Ryebridge over C&D (good to soft) on his handicap hurdle debut 13 days ago; wore cheekpieces on that occasion but they are now replaced by the visor he wore regularly during his Flat career; retains handicapping scope on his useful form in that sphere.
runners.comment: Dead heated with Ryebridge on recent C&D handicap hurdle debut; respected up 5l
runners.owner: On Top Racing Ltd & Two Brothers

runners.name: Ryebridge
runners.spotlight: Strong in the market and showed improved form when sharing the spoils with the reopposing Three Brothers in a C&D handicap (good to soft) 13 days ago; in the right hands to keep on improving.
runners.comment: Improved when dead heating with Three Brothers over C&D last month; may do better again

runners.name: Micks Jet
runners.spotlight: Bumper/hurdle winner who completed the set in a 2m3f Wetherby handicap chase (good to soft) last winter; not seen since a below-par third over fences at Market Rasen last April; lower mark back hurdling but got to be a chance she'll need this after nearly a year off.
runners.comment: Not seen for just under 12 months so got to be a chance she'll need this outing

runners.name: Matching Energy
runners.spotlight: Won Hexham bumper (good to soft) on rules debut last June and was the only one to give a highly promising rival any sort of race before last-flight fall on 2m1f course hurdle debut (soft) in December; nowhere near that level twice since (23l fifth to Las Canals at Sedgefield latterly) but could come into his own in handicaps; hooded for the first time now stepping up in trip; worth a look in the betting.
runners.comment: Well behind Las Canals latest but retains potential for handicaps; hooded now up in trip

runners.name: Las Canals
runners.spotlight: French bumper winner; reportedly bled from the nose when well held in Hereford maiden hurdle in December and left that behind with a hood added (retained) when springing a 33-1 surprise in a Sedgefield maiden hurdle (2m1f, good) three months later; that form will need improving upon to defy this opening mark but his unexposed profile provides hope.
runners.comment: Left British debut behind when 33-1 winner of maiden; more required now handicapping

runners.name: Heritier
runners.spotlight: His last win was over C&D in late 2024 but 0-11 in the meantime and hasn't delivered an effort of note this campaign.
runners.comment: C&D winner in late 2024 but hasn't been threatening to add to tally lately

runners.name: Chumlee
runners.spotlight: 11yo who is lightly raced for his age; hasn't threatened on his completed starts over fences this season (fell early latest); is a dual hurdle winner but suspicion he might prove vulnerable to some of the much younger legs in this line up.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner; returns to timber after a winless spell over fences but he is 11 now

races.name: Book In Advance Save Â£5 Handicap Hurdle (Div II)

runners.name: Disco Annie
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back winner over 2m1f here in November 2024 and confirmed her liking for the track when third over 2m1f last month; however, while returning here is clearly a good thing, she did finish last at Musselburgh two weeks ago; the cheekpieces she's worn the last twice are left off (usual tongue-tie remains).
runners.comment: Last latest but fact she's saved a lot of her best runs for this track provides hope

runners.name: Devon Skies
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick in November (2m5f/2m4f, good-soft) and bounced back from Sedgefield blip to win at Catterick (2m3f, soft) in January; only a respectable fourth at Wetherby next time but has been eased 2lb since.
runners.comment: Four wins in productive campaign; respectable fourth latest

runners.name: Yapper Hill
runners.spotlight: Posted his best effort when fourth of eight in 2m handicap hurdle at Wetherby in February but well beaten at Hexham ten days ago; a first-time tongue-tie is reached for on the back of that.
runners.comment: Fourth in 2m handicap in February but well held at Hexham ten days ago; tongue-tie added

runners.name: Red Cadillac
runners.spotlight: Came good with hard-fought success on seasonal return here (2m1f, soft) in November and has built on that in two Ayr outings since, winning over an extended 2m4f on soft last time; a further 8lb rise demands another career best but he has to be respected in his current mood.
runners.comment: Course winner; made it 2-3 for the season at Ayr last month but another 8lb higher now

runners.name: Kado Sacree
runners.spotlight: Hasn't really built on his hurdle debut fourth in three further outings around 2m but this 3m Irish point winner has the profile of a likely improver now handicapping over a longer trip; gets the vote for in-form stable, with confidence increased should the betting vibes be strong.
runners.comment: In good hands and appeals as a likely improver now handicapping up in trip

runners.name: Tarbuck
runners.spotlight: Now 3lb lower than when defeating Red Cadillac to win on handicap debut at Uttoxeter (2m4f, soft) 14 months ago but has looked out of sorts on his three outings this season.
runners.comment: Now 3lb below his winning mark but has looked out of sorts this season

runners.name: Half Track
runners.spotlight: Yet to show much spark this term and he was pulled up in handicap at Newcastle (2m7f) 67 days ago; it remains to be seen whether a first-time visor and refitted tongue-tie combination makes a difference.
runners.comment: Not shown much spark this term & remains to be seen whether new headgear combination helps

runners.name: Rauzan
runners.spotlight: Won four in a row over hurdles in Ireland in May-July 2024; down in weights and first sign of promise since coming to Britain when third of 14 over 2m5f at Kelso (good to soft) 14 days ago; well treated if he can build on that.
runners.comment: First promise for this yard when third at Kelso latest; well treated if building on that

races.name: South West Rail Solutions Ltd. "National Hunt" Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Dutch Corner
runners.spotlight: Third against useful rivals on first two hurdling starts and he ran another promising race when second of 14 at Exeter (2m, good to soft) last month; looked a bit tricky last time but that form puts him firmly in the picture and he would have finished closer but for a bad mistake two out.
runners.comment: Placed behind Sober Glory at Newbury and was runner-up at Exeter last time; respected

runners.name: Hardly Surprising
runners.spotlight: Owned by JP McManus when winning his Irish point; badly hampered on his hurdling debut but he's found improvement with two placed efforts (2m3f, soft) since; has leading claims on his latest third at Doncaster and he has shaped as though this slight drop back in trip could suit; key player.
runners.comment: Sets standard on his third at Doncaster and he's open to more progress back in trip

runners.name: Limier dArthel
runners.spotlight: French bumper winner in April 2024; didn't get very far (fell at first) on hurdling/stable debut in January but he travelled well for a long way when a close second at Exeter (2m, good to soft; hood added) last month; wandered around in closing stages but that was a creditable effort in a big field and he should improve for the experience; respected.
runners.comment: French bumper winner who was a close second over hurdles at Exeter last month; respected

runners.name: Ask A Sainte
runners.spotlight: Close third at Wincanton (1m7f, good) in the first of his two bumper runs last spring; tailed off on hurdling debut in November but he left that form well behind when a front-running fourth at Wincanton (1m7f, soft) the following month; was beaten around 15l but that was a promising effort in a big field and he's a player if he can find more improvement.
runners.comment: Promising fourth when front-running in big field at Wincanton in December; in the mix

runners.name: JPR Two
runners.spotlight: 120,000euros 3yo; half-brother to three winners, notably his owner's JPR One (smart 2m-2m3f hurdle/chase); 9-2 for an Exeter bumper (2m, soft) in December but he weakened into a remote eighth of 15; now goes hurdling after a break and he needs to leave that initial form a long way behind.
runners.comment: Well-related 5yo but he was well held in sole bumper; now goes hurdling after a break

runners.name: Saffron Belle
runners.spotlight: Big prices and she's been beaten a long way in two bumpers this year; can only be watched on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Struggled in two bumpers this year and can only be watched on hurdling debut

runners.name: Dantes Rocco
runners.spotlight: Placed on Irish point debut in December; remote fourth of six on hurdling debut at Newbury (2m4f, soft) in February but he found lots of improvement on the drop to 1m7f when third at Wincanton (good) 12 days ago; needs to build on that but he has a good pedigree and is open to more progress.
runners.comment: Good third when dropped to 1m7f at Wincanton and he's open to more progress; shortlisted

runners.name: Handmedownastar
runners.spotlight: Easily justified favouritism in an Irish maiden point late last year; well backed into 5-1 on hurdling debut at Fontwell (2m1f, soft) last month but he faded into a well-held third; should benefit from that run but this looks a tougher and he needs improvement.
runners.comment: Point winner but was well-held third on hurdling debut at Fontwell; needs improvement

runners.name: Warfleet
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a Market Rasen bumper in July 2024 but he's had only two runs since and was pulled up on his hurdling debut last spring; has plenty to prove after 13 months off and he's already with his third different trainer; hood added.
runners.comment: Pulled up on hurdling debut last spring and he now returns for another new yard; opposable

runners.name: Docteur Romeo
runners.spotlight: Well held in sole bumper in Decemeber 2024 and he returned after a long absence with a pulled-up effort on hurdling debut at Exeter (2m, good to soft) last month.
runners.comment: Well held in sole bumper and was pulled up on his hurdling debut last month

runners.name: Poets Muse
runners.spotlight: Her point win is nothing to shout about and she's been beaten 56l and 46l in two hurdle runs (2m5f/2m3f) this year; no appeal.
runners.comment: Her point win is nothing to shout about and she's been tailed off in both hurdle runs

runners.name: Mrs Pen
runners.spotlight: Massive prices and she's finished tailed off in two hurdle runs at Exeter (2m, good to soft) this spring.
runners.comment: Massive prices and she's finished tailed off in two hurdle runs at Exeter (2m) this spring

races.name: Les Drew Hello Matey I'm 80 Handicap Chase

runners.name: Begin The Luck
runners.spotlight: Won the corresponding race in 2023 and finished second in it 12 months ago (heavy); always tended to do well this time of the year and the more rain the better for him; goes on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Won the corresponding race in 2023 and finished second in it 12 months ago; shortlisted

runners.name: Ideallko
runners.spotlight: Did his winning for Evan Williams in batches, latterly when notching a hat-trick the winter before last; on a fair mark back from a short break but needs a revival after a few poor efforts in recent times.
runners.comment: On a fair mark back from a short break but needs a revival after a few poor efforts

runners.name: Palawan Du Mazet
runners.spotlight: Below form on good ground in December but finished first in his other three chases (all on soft/heavy; disqualified on first occasion); only raised another 3lb for 2m4f Leicester success in February and could yet be capable of better.
runners.comment: Finished first in three of his four chases; could be capable of better

runners.name: Jack Doyen
runners.spotlight: Wins have all come in late March, latterly off 5lb lower at Hereford ten days ago, so it's fair to say this is his time of year; adaptable as regards ground and remains fairly treated now back in form.
runners.comment: Wins have all come in late March; remains fairly treated now back in form; considered

runners.name: Jacks A Legend
runners.spotlight: 2m4f point winner who's done most racing under rules over shorter; tough task in a C&D hunter chase when last seen ten months ago and, while better off into ordinary handicap company for his new yard, it's possible he'll need this at his age.
runners.comment: Better off into ordinary handicap company for his new yard; may need this at his age

runners.name: Tractor Jack
runners.spotlight: Only seen sporadically over hurdles the past two years, latterly when not looking to see out 3m a month ago; needs a deal of improvement on today's chasing debut.
runners.comment: Only seen sporadically over hurdles; needs a deal more on today's chasing debut

runners.name: Devilsrollthedice
runners.spotlight: Well backed ahead of his handicap debut over hurdles in January but never looked happy; again came in for support over fences four weeks ago, in first-time cheekpieces, but was again pulled up; evidently felt capable of better.
runners.comment: Come in for support last twice; pulled up each time but evidently felt capable of better

runners.name: Firefly Lane
runners.spotlight: Course regular who's regressed in recent times; beaten a long way from 10lb out of the handicap last time and has a similarly tough task from 9lb wrong today.
runners.comment: Beaten long way from 10lb out of the handicap latest; similarly tough task from 9lb wrong

races.name: South West Rail Solutions Ltd. Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Jackeline
runners.spotlight: Close third over hurdles at Chepstow (2m, good) in October but she's not taken to fences this winter and has been pulled up in last two starts; bit to prove but she's on a dangerous mark back in this sphere and could be a big factor if she can revive; blinkers worn in last two starts are removed.
runners.comment: Pulled up in last two chases but reverts to hurdling on a dangerous mark

runners.name: Green Sky
runners.spotlight: Flat winner who was a creditable fourth on Polytrack (1m5f) on her penultimate run in February; 0-12 over hurdles but she's not had many chances in handicaps and is well treated on her best form in this sphere; needs a close look back over hurdles.
runners.comment: 0-12 over hurdles but she's well treated on best form in this sphere; needs a close look

runners.name: Getarose
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who has not finished closer than 13l to a winner and was laboured in a Hereford handicap (2m3f, good) last month; dropped another 3lb but she needs a transformation back in trip.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden who has not finished closer than 13l to a winner; down the list

runners.name: Parikarma
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner with three on the Flat and three over hurdles; didn't fire at Taunton last month but her last win was over C&D (good) and she's well handicapped if she can bounce back near her best; has possibilities in change of headgear.
runners.comment: C&D winner and she's a player if she can bounce back from a disappointing run at Taunton

runners.name: Reel Orange
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden who was disappointing at Hereford ten days ago, but she finished runner-up in Exeter handicap (2m2f, good to soft) on her stable debut last month; that form puts her in the picture and first-time cheekpieces could give her a lift.
runners.comment: 11-race maiden but has claims on her second at Exeter two runs back; cheekpieces added
runners.owner: J M Dare T Hamlin J W Snook

runners.name: Eskimo Sam
runners.spotlight: Down the field in four runs for Tom Lacey and she returned after long absence with well-held fifth on handicap/stable debut at Exeter (2m2f, good to soft) last month; still early days but she needs improvement on this drop back in trip; hood reapplied.
runners.comment: Unexposed 7yo but she needs improvement on this drop back in trip; hood reapplied

runners.name: Northern Air
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground point winner who won a maiden hurdle (2m, good to soft) for Fergal O'Brien last January; disappointing in three chase runs for Dan Skelton this winter but she still has low mileage and is on a dangerous mark back over hurdles; needs checking in market for new yard.
runners.comment: Disappointing in three chase runs for Dan Skelton and he needs major revival for new yard

races.name: Happy 45th Birthday Andy Hurtley Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Calimystic
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 7yo; record of 1-3 over hurdles and he's won at Newbury (2m6f, good) and Sandown (3m, soft) in his two chase runs this winter; up another 6lb but he travelled strongly on his penultimate run and is open to more progress on this drop back in trip; big player.
runners.comment: 2-2 over fences this winter and he's open to more progress back in trip; big player

runners.name: Bumpy Evans
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who is 5-8 over fences and he found more progress when runner-up in the Durham National (3m5f, good) in October; this is a different test on his return but he still looks on a workable mark and was successful over 2m4f last spring; has worn a hood in all of his chase runs but the headgear is missing today.
runners.comment: Record of 5-8 over fences and he's not ruled out back in trip on his reappearance

runners.name: Kap Ouest
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner who made it second time lucky over fences with an easy win at Exeter (2m2f, good to soft) last month; this is much tougher off 9lb higher but that was a clear personal best last time and he could have a lot more to offer as a chaser.
runners.comment: Unexposed chaser who was an easy winner at Exeter last month; respected up 9lb

runners.name: Royal Jewel
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good to soft) in the autumn and he got back on the scoresheet at Taunton (2m7f, heavy) in February; ran another good race when third at Taunton (good) last month and he should be in the mix again on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: C&D winner who scored at Taunton in February and was a good third there latest; respected

runners.name: Scarface
runners.spotlight: Four-time chase winner and his last success was in this race (off 2lb lower) last year; has been in and out this season but he bounced back with a fair third in Class 2 handicap at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft) two weeks ago; has possibilities if he can back that up and he's versatile ground-wise.
runners.comment: C&D winner who bounced back with a fair third at Newbury latest; each-way claims

runners.name: Hardy Du Seuil
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles in November and has done well on his belated return to fences, following a near-miss at Carlisle with a creditable third at Newbury (2m, good to soft) in February; still looks feasibly treated and he was a dual chase winner (2m/2m4f) in his youth; dangerous back up in trip.
runners.comment: Has done well on belated return to fences including a near-miss at Carlisle; shortlisted

runners.name: Nickelforce
runners.spotlight: Had form figures of 23243221 last year and he was as good as ever when scoring at Wincanton (2m4f, good) in November; has won when fresh in the past but he's 7lb higher than last time and this is highly competitive on his return.
runners.comment: Ended last year with a win but he's 7lb higher here and this is highly competitive

runners.name: Tahmuras
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on all four starts last year but he turned things around with a rallying second at Windsor (2m6f, soft; first-time blinkers) in January; 2lb higher after a break but he remains well treated on old form and this return to quicker ground should be fine; possibilities.
runners.comment: Turned things around with a second at Windsor and has claims if he can back that up

races.name: South West Rail Solutions Ltd. Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Top Guy
runners.spotlight: Just one win from ten starts over hurdles but that came at Aintree (3m1f) in December and he bounced back with a creditable second at Market Rasen (2m7f, good to soft) last month; nudged up 2lb but he's still unexposed as a stayer and has possibilities back up in trip.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Market Rasen (2m7f) last time and he's still unexposed as a stayer; dangerous

runners.name: Rickety Bridge
runners.spotlight: Won a Southwell maiden (2m4f, soft) in March 2024 but he's had only six runs since and has struggled in his last three handicaps; may have needed his comeback run (2m7f) in February but he needs a major revival back up in trip; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Lightly raced in recent years and has struggled in last three handicaps; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Striking A Pose
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner who is on a dangerous mark but he was pulled up twice in December and beat only rival at Wincanton (3m, good) last month; has something to prove and yard also runs Walk Tall.
runners.comment: Five-time hurdle winner but he's struggled in three of his last four runs; passed over
runners.owner: S Clare G Kennington & C Knowles

runners.name: Call Me Legend
runners.spotlight: Has form figures of 431122 since switched in handicaps and that latest second was a near-miss at Exeter (2m7f, good) 13 days ago; up another 3lb but he's versatile ground-wise and is open to more progress over staying trips; strongly respected with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Has form figures of 431122 in handicaps and that latest second was a near-miss; key player

runners.name: Walk Tall
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who looked a nice prospect when runner-up in a bumper at Exeter last February; hasn't set the world alight in five goes over hurdles this term but he kept on well when a 12l second in a Hereford handicap (2m6f, soft) in February; should have more to offer in this sphere and he needs watching in market on this step up to 3m2f.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 6yo who was runner-up over 2m6f last time; one to keep an eye on at new trip

runners.name: Crossgales King
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt over hurdles when justifying favouritism in a Wincanton handicap (3m1f, good to soft) last month; up 5lb but he stayed on strongly for that comfortable win and is open to more progress at this new trip; key player.
runners.comment: 6yo who won at Wincanton (3m1f) last month and he's open to more progress; key player

runners.name: Sams Amour
runners.spotlight: Triple hurdle winner (2m2f-2m5f) but she struggled in her final three runs last year and was reported to have bled from the nose on chase debut in December; finished runner-up over C&D (good) last summer but she needs a big turnaround after a break.
runners.comment: Triple hurdle winner who has form over C&D but she needs a big turnaround after a break

races.name: Newton Abbot On Course Bookmakers Fillies' "Junior" National Hunt Flat Race (GBB Race)

runners.name: Gustes Dance
runners.spotlight: 95,000euros 2yo; half-sister to winners Great Dance (bumper) and Louise Demonmirail (French 2m2f hurdle); 4-1 at Wincanton (1m7f, good to soft) last month and she showed clear promise with her 8l third of 14; sets a fair standard on that form and she's open to progress with hood added on her second start; key player.
runners.comment: Sets standard on her debut third at Wincanton last month and she's a big player; hood on

runners.name: Numero Ventotto
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam bumper winner, sister to 2m3f/2m5f hurdle winner Touchy Feely, closely related to useful bumper and 2m3f-2m7f hurdle/chase winner Sure Touch, out of useful 2m3f/2m5f chase winning close relative of Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Long Run; has a striking pedigree and she's an interesting newcomer for top yard.
runners.comment: Has a striking pedigree and she's an interesting newcomer for top yard
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Racing & Robert Waley-Cohen

runners.name: Enya
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on Warwick debut (2m, good to soft) in November and she returned after wind surgery with a creditable third at Catterick (1m7f, good; 6-4f favourite) last month; needs to take another step forward here but she's a promising type who should have more of offer in bumpers.
runners.comment: Finished placed in both her runs and she's a player if she can take another step forward
runners.owner: Sheep As A Lamb Syndicate Gary Gillies

runners.name: Agreymiracleseeker
runners.spotlight: 50,000euros foal, 82,000euros yearling; half-sister to five winners including Loaves And Fishes (1m 2yo/1m2f; RPR 98), Shalaa Asker (prolific 5f-7f; 89) and The Jean Genie (1m2f; 87); dam 11.6f Listed winner (103), half-sister to Katchit; interesting newcomer and she needs a close look for in-form yard.
runners.comment: 82,000euros half-sister to five winners; interesting newcomer and she needs a close look
runners.owner: G C Myddelton & V E C Taylor

runners.name: My Lady dEx
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to winners Iron D'Ex (2m4f chase) and Happy D'Ex (point/bumper and 2m3f/2m4f hurdle); dam 11.5f-1m5f AQPS Flat winner, half-sister to French Listed-placed 2m5f/2m7f chase winner Azrou d'Ex; yard 2-2 in bumpers this season and interesting to see how she figures in market.
runners.comment: Yard 2-2 in bumpers this season and interesting to see how she figures in the market

runners.name: Lundy View
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam lightly raced in bumpers/over hurdles (best effort at 2m6f); yard 0-37 in bumpers in recent years and she's probably a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: Yard 0-37 in bumpers in recent years and she's probably one for later on

runners.name: Midnight Moment
runners.spotlight: Â£30,000 3yo; second foal; dam 2m4f-3m hurdle winner, half-sister to useful winners Kayf Blanco (2m-2m5f hurdle/chase) and Land Of Vic (bumper and 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase), out of unraced half-sister to Cheltenham Gold Cup winner See More Business; plenty to like on paper and she needs a close look on debut.
runners.comment: Â£30,000 3yo; plenty to like on paper and she needs a close look on debut

runners.name: Moonlight Ballet
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; unraced dam out of 1m Listed winning half-sister to 1m2f-2m winner Northern Frontier, is granddaughter of Listed-placed 6f 2yo winning half-sister to US 8.5f Grade 1 winner Include Betty; yard 1-49 in bumpers in recent years and she's probably one for later on.
runners.comment: Yard 1-49 in bumpers in recent years and she's probably one for later on

runners.name: Nuala Rocket
runners.spotlight: 3,500euros 3yo; second foal; dam unraced sister to point winner, half-sister to winners Stormyisland Ahead (2m-3m hurdle/chase), Sara's Smile (2m/2m1f hurdle) and Amzac Magic (2m4f/2m5f hurdle); has quite a bit of stamina in her pedigree but she needs checking in market on debut.
runners.comment: Has quite a bit of stamina in pedigree but she needs watching in market on debut

runners.name: Saint Canary Rock
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to 2m hurdle winner City Of Diamonds; dam unraced half-sister to 11.7f Flat and useful 2m/2m1f hurdle/chase winner King Of The Picts, out of unraced half-sister to 1m1f Group 3 winner Utrecht; yard 3-14 in bumpers this season and market should be informative on debut.
runners.comment: Yard 3-14 in bumpers this season and market should be informative on debut

runners.name: Seedling
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam 2m5f hurdle winner, closely related to useful bumper/2m-2m6f hurdle winner Spirit Of Kayf, half-sister to smart 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Cheddleton, out of useful bumper and 2m-2m6f hurdle/chase winner; makes some appeal on pedigree and yard is 21% in bumpers this season.
runners.comment: Makes some appeal on pedigree and yard is 21% in bumpers this season

runners.name: Shelightsupmylife
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to five Flat winners including Cruck Realta (6f-1m2f including Listed; RPR 97), Davy's Dilemma (7f-2m; 93) and Universal Circus (6f/7f; 80); dam 1m2f winner (61); yard 0-9 in bumpers in recent seasons and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Half-sister to five Flat winners but yard is 0-9 in bumpers in recent seasons

runners.name: Frenati
runners.spotlight: Weakened on her Doncaster debut in November but she returned from a break with a brave win at Stratford (2m, soft) last month; that was a major step forward but she only won by a nose and the form does not looks strong; has some work to do under a penalty.
runners.comment: Narrow win at Stratford but the form does not looks strong; has work to do under penalty

runners.name: Whodunit
runners.spotlight: Out of a half-sister to three bumper winners but she made a low-key start at Wincanton (1m7f, good to soft; 28-1) in February and was a long way behind Guste's Dance; passed over.
runners.comment: Struggled at Wincanton in February and was a long way behind Guste's Dance; passed over

races.name: Thank You GRP Solutions For Sponsoring Maiden Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Killer Whale
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two 1m AW runs in late 2025, finishing a 6l second behind an exciting stablemate of Chasing Time at Kempton in December; out of a Cesarewitch winner so the step up in trip should suit.
runners.comment: Modest form in two 1m runs in late 2025; dam a Cesarewitch winner so new trip looks a plus

runners.name: Chasing Time
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal of a French Group-placed 1m1f winner (RPR 104), herself a sister to Group-placed 1m winner Flowrider; gelded prior to debut; stable always command huge respect in AW maiden/novice events.
runners.comment: Dam a Group-placed 1m1f winner (RPR 104); yard always feared in these races

runners.name: Sinocentric
runners.spotlight: In need of the experience when fourth of six, beaten 9.5l, in a 1m4f novice at Southwell 24 days ago; bred to do much better at some point.
runners.comment: Modest fourth of six on last month's debut but bred to leave that behind him in time

races.name: In Remembrance Of Aggrey Burke Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Coloane
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner but she made a promising return from a 108-day break here two weeks ago; talented claimer takes off a valuable 3lb; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Promising run after a break here two weeks ago; shouldn't be too far away

runners.name: Always Fearless
runners.spotlight: Readily saw off eight rivals over C&D last month; reportedly never travelled when disappointing at Lingfield since; capable of bouncing back in this field.
runners.comment: C&D win last month was followed by a modest run at Lingfield; capable of bouncing back

runners.name: Yehudi
runners.spotlight: 0-15 but he has gone close on occasions, including over C&D in December; another new combination of accessories tried this afternoon; inexperienced apprentice booked.
runners.comment: 0-15 but has gone close, including over C&D; new combination of accessories tried today

runners.name: Book Of Life
runners.spotlight: Hung away a winning opportunity over C&D in February and neither run since has been as good; more minor money perhaps his best hope.
runners.comment: Second over C&D in February, when rather throwing the race away; not so good twice since

runners.name: Poetic Force
runners.spotlight: Recorded the 18th win of his long career when leading late over C&D in February (for Myla Coppins); ran well behind a thriving rival at Lingfield next time but he failed to fire over C&D a fortnight ago (finished behind Coloane); he has bounced back from quiet runs before so can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: C&D winner in February; one good run and one lesser effort have followed

runners.name: Nala The Lioness
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; sent off at big odds and well beaten in two runs for new stable after a 400-day absence; down in trip/weights but has a point to prove.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two handicaps for new yard after a 400-day layoff; plenty to prove

runners.name: Not So Sobers
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner and a fair maiden hurdler; didn't achieve much in his first four Flat runs but he was sent off favourite for a 1m6f handicap at Chelmsford in February and plugged on for a close third; can do better in this sphere but dropping back to 8.6f not sure to suit.
runners.comment: Unexposed on the Flat and ran well last time but that was 1m6f; drop in trip the query

runners.name: Orbital Chime
runners.spotlight: Two wins over 6f for previous trainers; unplaced in two runs for new stable (7f), catching the attention of the stewards on the first occasion, and hopes appear to rest on this first attempt at further than 7f having a positive effect.
runners.comment: Unplaced in two 7f runs for new yard; down in weights; first run over further than 7f

races.name: Remembrance Of Ferdinand Addo Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C & D) (GBB)

runners.name: Constant Star
runners.spotlight: Promising debut when second of seven in a slowly-run 1m maiden at Lingfield last month; open to plenty of improvement and he is high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Promising 2nd in a slowly-run 1m maiden last month; open to plenty of improvement

runners.name: Safe Zone
runners.spotlight: She has displayed some ability in her two 6f runs here this year and should prove better suited by today's extra furlong; has more to offer.
runners.comment: Some promise in two 6f runs and this extra furlong should suit; each-way shout

runners.name: Thai Princess
runners.spotlight: Low-key debut effort when finishing last of the four runners in a 6f fillies' novice at Kempton 17 days ago; new trip/tongue tie can prompt improvement.
runners.comment: Modest debut effort last month but the new trip/tongue tie should have a positive effect

runners.name: Action Reaction
runners.spotlight: Modest form in her two 1m runs last month, albeit the second effort represented improvement; drop to 7f not sure to suit on pedigree.
runners.comment: Modest form in two 1m runs last month; handicaps should offer a better opportunity

runners.name: Rogue Desire
runners.spotlight: Sent off the 7-2 third favourite for her Salisbury debut (7f, good to firm) last August but she was unable to land a telling blow, beaten 4.5l into third at the finish; bred to do better at some point.
runners.comment: Satisfactory debut when third at Salisbury (7f) last August; open to improvement this year

runners.name: Thimble
runners.spotlight: Pedigree suggests she is unlikely to come into her own until tackling 1m2f+ but she did shape with some promise at Kempton in February, only weakening in the final furlong.
runners.comment: Bred for further but February's Kempton debut (7f) wasn't without encouragement

runners.name: Imelda
runners.spotlight: 17,000gns yearling; third foal; half-sister to 1m4f AW winner Nancy P (RPR 67); unraced dam closely related to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Nations Alexander; doesn't have a daunting standard to aim at on debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a 1m4f AW winner; she doesn't have a daunting standard to aim at on debut

runners.name: Difford Girl
runners.spotlight: 66-1 when tailed off in a 9.4f novice here three weeks ago; can't be recommended.
runners.comment: 66-1 when tailed off in a 9.4f novice here three weeks ago; can't be recommended

races.name: Thank You Opus Foundation Handicap

runners.name: Cayman Tai
runners.spotlight: Just one win to his name after 21 starts but he has a lot of ability for one racing at this level and he found only the flourishing Yorkshire Glory too strong at Newcastle last week; 1lb rise negligible and obvious claims, for all he looks one to tread carefully with at short prices.
runners.comment: Well up to winning off this mark and found only a flourishing rival too strong last week

runners.name: Valsharah
runners.spotlight: 0-17 when trained by Charles Hills and beaten on in three runs for new stable too, albeit his latest second at Kempton was much more encouraging; runs off the same mark today and a tongue tie and cheekpieces are worn together today (worn independently in the past).
runners.comment: 0-20 but latest 2nd was a solid effort; new combination of accessories; contender

runners.name: Brian The Snail
runners.spotlight: Slow-starting veteran who is having his 100th outing this evening; he has won 14 times already and his latest Newcastle fourth was a solid effort; should go well again.
runners.comment: Having his 100th racecourse start tonight; solid 4th last time; each-way claims once again

runners.name: Punchbowl Flyer
runners.spotlight: Series of creditable efforts this year and returning to 6f is no bad thing; only one win to his name since June 2021 though.
runners.comment: Just one win since June 2021 but he has been performing with credit in defeat this year

runners.name: Megaphone
runners.spotlight: Went the wrong way for George Boughey last year and no sign of a revival for new yard this winter.
runners.comment: Tumbling down the weights but showing no sign of taking advantage

runners.name: Pianoforte
runners.spotlight: Should be well handicapped now but he hasn't built on a good fourth at Newcastle in December in three subsequent runs; connections reach for cheekpieces tonight.
runners.comment: Promising 4th in December but he hasn't built on it since; now tried in cheekpieces

runners.name: Due Date
runners.spotlight: Four wins in a purple patch in the second half of 2025; struggled at big odds in three runs this year; enough to prove for now.
runners.comment: Had a good 2025 campaign but well beaten at big odds in three runs this year
runners.owner: A Bangs & Miss J A Challen

runners.name: Kirkdale
runners.spotlight: Still seeking his first handicap success but he has run creditably in four starts since dropping back to 6f; can miss the break but he has solid form claims otherwise.
runners.comment: Four solid efforts since dropped to 6f; the start is an issue but solid form claims

runners.name: Lequinto
runners.spotlight: No AW win since December 2021 but there have been some good runs on both Polytrack and Tapeta this year; slipping down the weights and he holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: No AW win since December 2021; arrives in fair form though and he has an each-way shout

races.name: Thank You To Omari For The Support Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Gemmari
runners.spotlight: Helped set the race up for the closers when favourite here last time, having previously won easily over 1m6f round Chelmsford; now has to prove his revised mark is within range coming back down in trip.
runners.comment: Helped set the race up when favourite here last time; has to prove this mark within range

runners.name: Codiak
runners.spotlight: Didn't need to improve to win an uncompetitive C&D novice in the winter but did it impressively and he bettered that at Southwell (off 2lb lower than today) next time, coming from off a steady pace to get up in a busy finish; remains open to further improvement.
runners.comment: Came from off a steady pace to get up in a busy finish last time; open to more improvement

runners.name: Pride Of Donegal
runners.spotlight: Consistent in defeat all winter and the combination of a first-time visor and uncontested lead was enough to get him back to winning ways over C&D five weeks ago; a 4lb rise demands more.
runners.comment: Made all in a first-time visor over C&D five weeks ago; a 4lb rise demands more

runners.name: Charmaine
runners.spotlight: Novice win as a 3yo came after four months off, which is a positive as she returns from a six-month absence; looked to find this sort of mark just beyond her after two victories last spring (Tapeta, Polytrack) but this is less competitive.
runners.comment: Looked to find this mark beyond her after two wins last spring but this less competitive

runners.name: Bulldog Spirit
runners.spotlight: Debuts for a fourth yard having been picked up from Brian Ellison last autumn for 7,500gns; won first time out as a 2yo and on his comeback last spring, which gives hope, but there's not much room for manoeuvre off this mark.
runners.comment: Debuts for a fourth yard; not much room for manoeuvre off this mark on comeback

runners.name: Polling Day
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground win last summer came off a 4lb lower mark and he's entitled to need this at his age back from seven months off; trainer also saddles Bulldog Spirit.
runners.comment: Entitled to need this at his age after seven months off; trainer also runs Bulldog Spirit

runners.name: Maasai Mara
runners.spotlight: Lost his way over hurdles, running poorly in two runs back from a break last November; the usual cheekpieces are replaced by a first-time hood and it's hard to believe he's still up to this sort of mark on the Flat.
runners.comment: Lost his way over hurdles; hard to believe he's still up to this sort of mark on the Flat
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late K W Bell

races.name: Founding Member Of ACCI WRPA Handicap

runners.name: Amerjeet
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate and hasn't always looked the most eager under pressure; conditions no problem and on a dangerous mark so can't rule out another prominent showing.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but conditions hold no terrors and he is down to a career-low mark

runners.name: Toomuchforme
runners.spotlight: Revived by a visor of late, third at Newcastle in February and then getting the better of Shalaa Asker at Southwell 25 days ago; 3lb higher today and the stable also runs Blue Force.
runners.comment: Revived by visor on last two starts, beating Shalaa Asker at Southwell last month; solid

runners.name: Beaming Light
runners.spotlight: On a competitive mark but his three wins have come over 1m and dropping to 6f didn't help matters ten days ago; now has his first 5f run.
runners.comment: Three wins over 1m; on a good mark but dropping to 5f is an obvious query

runners.name: Candy Warhol
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won three times at Wolverhampton last year, including at this trip; closely matched with Blue Force on their C&D run in December; sharper for his recent reappearance; stable also runs Phoenix Beach.
runners.comment: C&D winner; closely matched with Blue Force on December's C&D run; should go well
runners.owner: Homecroft Wealth Paul J Dixon & Partners

runners.name: Phoenix Beach
runners.spotlight: Not straightforward (hangs right) but these conditions suit him well and he wasn't beaten far into third here two weeks ago; one of two contenders for Scott Dixon.
runners.comment: Faces his optimum conditions and ran well for third here two weeks ago; contender

runners.name: Shalaa Asker
runners.spotlight: Three 5f wins this winter and he found only Toomuchforme too strong at Southwell last month; 1lb better off for a head defeat; chance.
runners.comment: Three AW wins this winter and also ran well behind Toomuchforme at Southwell last month

runners.name: Blue Force
runners.spotlight: Beat Candy Warhol over C&D in December off this mark; unplaced in five runs since but he is well drawn and a revival wouldn't be a huge shock; stable also runs Toomuchforme.
runners.comment: C&D win off this mark in December; unplaced five times since but could revive from stall 1
runners.owner: John And Tony Jarvis And Partner

runners.name: Betweenthesticks
runners.spotlight: Conditions no problem and he returns from an absence on his last winning mark; record fresh doesn't suggest this will be his day though.
runners.comment: C&D winner and returns on his last winning mark; record fresh points to looking elsewhere
runners.owner: J A Swinburne & Mrs Ruth A Carr

races.name: Thank You, Ina Deans Lifelong Carer Handicap

runners.name: Karakula Dancer
runners.spotlight: He has turned in a series of good efforts in AW handicaps this year, winning at Lingfield and Kempton (1m) and placed on his other three starts; not seen to best effect when third over C&D 19 days ago and he commands respect.
runners.comment: Two wins and three placed efforts from five AW runs this year; solid claims

runners.name: Zoustellar
runners.spotlight: Ran her best race so far when keeping on for fourth at Newcastle two weeks ago, her first attempt at 1m; she would have finished closer granted a clear run too and she now has something to build on.
runners.comment: Improved effort when unlucky-in-running 4th at Newcastle (1m) last month; unexposed

runners.name: Lexington Express
runners.spotlight: Finished behind Karakula Dancer and Amazing Anita at Southwell last month; left that well behind with an emphatic C&D win 11 days ago but she did enjoy the run of the race there and she has an 8lb higher mark to contend with today.
runners.comment: Easy C&D win 11 days ago but she was able to dictate; behind two of these on previous run

runners.name: Panthere Noir
runners.spotlight: Two low-key efforts at Newcastle last month (7f); continues to drop down the weights but others bring more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Two low-key runs at Newcastle last month; down in the weights but not a solid option

runners.name: Amazing Anita
runners.spotlight: 0.75l behind Karakula Dancer when they finished second and third at Southwell last month (1m; cheekpieces first time); open to further progress after just five starts.
runners.comment: Closely matched with Karakula Dancer on latest Southwell run; not yet exposed

races.name: Join Our Syndicate At Brownsbarn Thoroughbreds Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Is This For Real
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground 2m4f point winner who landed a valuable Kelso bumper for this yard; off the mark over hurdles on third attempt when taking Newcastle maiden (2m1f, good to soft) in January but pulled up on handicap debut at Haydock (2m3f, good to soft) last month; drops back in distance here and warrants another chance.
runners.comment: Won Newcastle maiden in January; pulled up on handicap debut but chance on earlier form

runners.name: Cinammon Coco
runners.spotlight: Hurdles (2m1f, good) and Flat (1m4f, AW) wins for Joseph O'Brien last year; 12-1, pulled up in a Fakenham handicap (2m, good to soft; first-time tongue-tie, now removed) on stable debut in March; best watched.
runners.comment: 12-1, pulled up in a Fakenham handicap on stable debut last month; best watched

runners.name: Forever Penywern
runners.spotlight: Won a couple of sellers (1m3f/1m2f) on the Flat for William Haggas last year; improved on hurdle debut when third of four finishers in a Stratford juvenile (2m2f, soft) last month and holds place claims.
runners.comment: Third in a Stratford juvenile on his second hurdles start last month; holds place claims.

runners.name: Maestrow
runners.spotlight: Hasn't shown much in a couple of bumpers at Cheltenham and Kempton; in good hands but needs to show more on this hurdles debut; yard also runs Moves Like Mason.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much in a couple of bumpers at Cheltenham and Kempton; hurdles debut.

runners.name: Katarcice
runners.spotlight: Bought for Â£28,000 after runner-up in two 3m Irish points; had wind op prior to creditable third in bumper here (good) last month; has potential now switched to hurdles; yard also runs Is This For Real.
runners.comment: Creditable third in bumper on rules debut over C&D last month; hurdles debut.

runners.name: Karthala
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden on the Flat, now has a mark of 60; headgear worn on last five starts is absent on this hurdles debut; best watched.
runners.comment: Flat maiden with a mark of 60; regular headgear left off on hurdles debut; best watched.

runners.name: Moves Like Mason
runners.spotlight: Second in final attempt in Irish points but failed to threaten in two hurdles events when last seen in May and may be more interesting in handicaps shortly.
runners.comment: Failed to threaten in two hurdles events when last seen in May.

runners.name: Papa Oscar
runners.spotlight: Poor maiden on the Flat and pulled up on hurdles debut over C&D in January when 400-1.
runners.comment: Poor maiden on the Flat and pulled up on hurdles debut over C&D in January (400-1)

races.name: Ross Pike Memorial Mares' Handicap Chase (Challenger Mares' Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Taxus Baccata
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper and over fences for Matt Crawley last season but has improved since undergoing wind surgery for her new trainer this year; appreciated the step up in trip when winning for the second time in succession at Wincanton (3m1f, good) 12 days ago; up 7lb but should be hard to beat.
runners.comment: Stepped up in trip when winning for the second successive time at Wincanton 12 days ago.

runners.name: Overabottleofred
runners.spotlight: Had a straightforward task when winning her second race over fences at Market Rasen (3m, good to soft) last month; up 7lb but still on the upgrade and should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Had a straightforward task when winning over fences at Market Rasen last month.

runners.name: Geturguccion
runners.spotlight: She ran very well when second in a bumper on her rules debut at Newbury in March 2024 and filled the same position in a couple of hurdles contests last term; has yet to fire in a couple of races over fences in November/December but is in good hands and is not one to give up on.
runners.comment: Yet to fire in a couple of races over fences in November/December but is in good hands.

runners.name: Moonlit Potter
runners.spotlight: Scored over hurdles for Henry Daly in February 2024 but has yet to win over fences and was pulled up at Ffos Las (3m, heavy) in January; needs to show more.
runners.comment: Has yet to win over fences and was pulled up at Ffos Las in January.

races.name: Bedfordshire Racing Club Remembers Dave Loveridge Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Tara Iti
runners.spotlight: Eight wins over hurdles for Gary Moore/James Owen but none beyond around 2m1f; below form in three runs in this sphere and one run on the Flat for current yard; well handicapped if he can return to form.
runners.comment: Below form in three runs in this sphere and one run on the Flat for current yard.

runners.name: U S Marshall
runners.spotlight: Point winner who has yet to get off the mark over hurdles; dropped in class and showed more when fifth of 12 finishers at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) three weeks ago in first-time cheekpieces (retained) and one of the likelier ones.
runners.comment: Dropped in class and showed more when fifth at Newcastle three weeks ago.

runners.name: Kentucky River
runners.spotlight: A fair 2m1f Flat winner (on good to firm ground) for Ollie Sangster in August; yet to translate that form to hurdles for his new yard but steps up in trip on this drop in class and must be of interest.
runners.comment: Yet to show any form over hurdles but steps up in trip and drops in class.
runners.owner: J R B Williams & Emdells Limited

runners.name: Hiero Sport
runners.spotlight: Scored at Fakenham (2m4f, good) in October 2024 off 2lb lower and should be sharper following his first run for 12 months when down the field here in December; hood is reinstated along with first-time tongue-tie; possibilities.
runners.comment: Should be sharper after his first run for 12 months when down the field here in December.

runners.name: Out Of It
runners.spotlight: Beaten 52l, 30l and 33l in his three outings over hurdles so far; steps up in trip on his handicap debut and needs to show improvement.
runners.comment: Beaten 52l, 30l and 33l in his three outings over hurdles so far.
runners.owner: Mrs C S Wilson And Miss C Lees-Jones

runners.name: Mcflo
runners.spotlight: Yet to be placed from six starts under rules and fared no better when sixth of eight here (2m3f) last month on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Yet to be placed from six starts under rules.

runners.name: Sullivan Bay
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been seen over hurdles since February last year but was only beaten a head at Plumpton in December 2024 when 11-8 favourite; runner-up on the Flat twice recently so fitness will not be an issue and he must be of interest.
runners.comment: Not seen over hurdles since February 2025 but was runner-up twice on the Flat recently.

races.name: Brian Knight Loves His Family Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Homme dUn Soir
runners.spotlight: Has three wins over fences but after finishing last of four at Hereford last month, he exploited a lower hurdles mark when successful at Wincanton (1m7f, good) 12 days ago; needs to use that momentum back over the larger obstacles.
runners.comment: Exploited a lower hurdles mark when successful at Wincanton 12 days ago.
runners.owner: Jb Harper And Cavalry And Guards

runners.name: Flamelco
runners.spotlight: Has three wins over fences, including one over C&D; 2lb lower than for his last win at Plumpton last April but has been below his best on his last three starts, the latest when tailed off over hurdles.
runners.comment: 2lb lower than for his last win last April but has been below his best recently.

runners.name: Icare Grandchamp
runners.spotlight: Landed his third success over fences at Plumpton (2m1f, soft) in February; shaped as if his stamina gave out when third of five finishers here (2m4f, good to soft) last month; may be worth another chance back in trip on better ground.
runners.comment: Landed his third success over fences at Plumpton in February; third here last month.

runners.name: Rumoursareflying
runners.spotlight: Landed his third success over fences at Ludlow (2m, good) last month after his challenger fell at the last obstacle; up 7lb but still on the upgrade and should not be far away.
runners.comment: Landed his third success over fences at Ludlow last month.

runners.name: Park Annonciade
runners.spotlight: All three wins over hurdles when trained in Ireland; running well at present and second of five at Plumpton (2m1f, good to soft) last month; threatening to get his head in front for this yard.
runners.comment: Running well at present and second at Plumpton last month.

runners.name: Harel Du Marais
runners.spotlight: Back to his best after a three-month break when landing his fourth win over fences at Stratford (2m1f, soft) last month; up 5lb but recorded back-to-back victories around this time last year so not to be taken lightly.
runners.comment: Back to his best when landing his fourth win over fences at Stratford last month.

races.name: Best Odds Guaranteed At Betano Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Hello Sweety
runners.spotlight: Likes to dominate and got off the mark at Fakenham (2m, soft) in December on her ninth start over hurdles; followed up over the same C&D (good to soft) when making all last month; 12lb higher here but difficult to oppose while in this mood.
runners.comment: Has won her last two starts, both at Fakenham, and will be hard to catch around here.

runners.name: Time Interval
runners.spotlight: Last of three wins over hurdles came at Warwick last May off 6lb higher; doesn't look ready to take advantage.
runners.comment: Last of 3 wins over hurdles came at Warwick last May off 6lb higher; has to raise game.

runners.name: King Ulanda
runners.spotlight: Landed his second win over hurdles on his reappearance at Carlisle (2m1f, good) in October and ran well on his next three starts; pulled up at Haydock last month but was in the process of running a good race when falling two out at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) 16 days ago in first-time cheekpieces (retained); decent ground looks to be the key and he should run well.
runners.comment: Won his second race over hurdles at Carlisle in October; fell when close up last time.

runners.name: Pergamon
runners.spotlight: Got his hurdling career off to a perfect start when winning two in a row at Plumpton and Kelso last May; hasn't really gone on from that promise in handicaps and resumes following a five-month break.
runners.comment: Won his first two hurdle starts but has not progressed as expected.

runners.name: Sleeping Late
runners.spotlight: 0-7 and was below form following a short break when eighth at Stratford (2m, soft) last month; needs to bounce back on this drop in grade on better ground.
runners.comment: 0-7 and was below form following a short break when eighth at Stratford last month.

runners.name: Tilehurst
runners.spotlight: A bumper winner for Henry Daly in October 2024, he ran his best race over hurdles when beaten half a length at Taunton (2m, good) last month following a six-month break; looks sure to pay his way in the coming months and holds claims.
runners.comment: Ran his best race over hurdles when beaten half a length at Taunton last month.

runners.name: Sweeper System
runners.spotlight: Built upon previous form when coming good at the fifth attempt over hurdles at Wetherby (2m, good to soft) last month; up 7lb but if the better ground was the reason for his improvement then he must also be considered here.
runners.comment: Improved form when coming good on his fifth start over hurdles at Wetherby last month.

runners.name: Il Va De Soi
runners.spotlight: His two wins came on soft in April 2024; out of sorts over fences and hurdles this term; drops back in trip but his best form has been over further.
runners.comment: Out of sorts over fences and hurdles this term; drops back in trip.

races.name: Bet 10 Get 30 At Betano "Junior" National Hunt Flat Race (GBB Race)

runners.name: Running Happy
runners.spotlight: Â£6,500 3yo who's the second foal of an unraced mare; trainer doesn't have a good record in these races but had a bumper debutant win easily at Wincanton on Wednesday.
runners.comment: Â£6,500 3yo; trainer had a bumper debutant win easily at Wincanton on Wednesday

runners.name: Kewland
runners.spotlight: Plenty of stamina on the dam's side; trainer had three newcomers win bumpers this season (from 27 runners), one at a big price, and it will be interesting to see what the market has to say.
runners.comment: Plenty of stamina on the dam's side; interesting to see what the market has to say

runners.name: Padel Whacker
runners.spotlight: Brother to the yard's Shutfrontdoor, who won on his bumper debut last spring, and a half-brother to three more stable winners; trainer 4-16 (seven others placed) in these races this season and he's an interesting contender.
runners.comment: Brother to the yard's Shutfrontdoor/half-brother to three more stable winners; interesting
runners.owner: J Hinds A Head & G L Moore

runners.name: Boy Oh Boy
runners.spotlight: Boasts a Flat pedigree, being out of a dual 1m winner for William Knight (some stamina on that side); rare bumper runner for his yard; the market will guide.
runners.comment: Boasts a Flat pedigree; rare bumper runner for his yard; the market will guide
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Kenneth Sneath

runners.name: Island Getaway
runners.spotlight: 17,500euro yearling, 55,000euro 3yo whose dam was a smart hurdler in France; trainer had a newcomer win a Warwick bumper recently and he's a likely type on paper.
runners.comment: Trainer had a newcomer win a Warwick bumper recently and he's a likely type on paper

runners.name: Jack The Master
runners.spotlight: Second foal of a point winner, herself a sister to Josephine Marcus, who won an Irish bumper on debut; trainer doesn't have a great record in bumpers in recent times, certainly compared to previously.
runners.comment: Second foal of a point winner; yard doesn't have a great record in bumpers in recent times

runners.name: Ramli
runners.spotlight: Â£20,000 3yo who's a half-brother to some useful Flat winners at up to 1m; didn't run for Nick Littmoden; the market will be the best guide.
runners.comment: Â£20,000 3yo who's a half-brother to some useful Flat winners at up to 1m

runners.name: Westbrooke Boy
runners.spotlight: Won a weak junior bumper over C&D last November (rain-softened good to soft ground) on debut before a poor effort on soft at Chepstow; has since undergone wind surgery; needs better under a penalty.
runners.comment: Undergone wind surgery since a poor run on soft ground; needs better under a penalty

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Alliteration
runners.spotlight: Debut bumper winner at Galway last October; placed in all three maiden hurdles, including twice at this track (2m-2m2f); down in trip again after finishing second to a Gordon Elliott-trained odds-on chance over 2m5f at Leopardstown; cheekpieces and tongue-tie fitted now; capable of getting in the mix.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, placed on all three maiden hurdle outings, capable of getting in the mix

runners.name: Simply Natural
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; 0-4 in bumpers, best effort when beaten a short head at Naas in December; winner subsequently placed in a Grade 2 bumper at the DRF; runner-up to an experienced rival on hurdling debut over C&D; this looks like a stronger race, but open to improvement.
runners.comment: Second to an experienced rival on hurdling debut over C&D, this race appears stronger

runners.name: Salsinha
runners.spotlight: Had no trouble justifying favouritism on debut in Cork fillies' bumper on debut last July (good); still looked raw when fourth on heavy ground on her hurdling debut at Navan in November, rallied after losing her place, finished 1 1/4l behind today's rival Cool Native; picked up valuable experience when third in a Listed contest at Thurles; holds a major chance.
runners.comment: Good effort in a Listed hurdle gives her a chance of reversing Navan form with Cool Native

runners.name: Shesakindofmagic
runners.spotlight: Limerick bumper winner last season (yielding-to-soft); returned from absence to chase home long odds-on winner in four-runner C&D mares' hurdle on New Year's Day; disappointing run here two months ago leaves her plenty to find with Alliteration; first time with a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Bumper winner. C&D seventh leaves her plenty to find with Alliteration, tongue-tie on now

runners.name: Cool Native
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden point at Loughbrickland last season on her fourth start for leading producer Denis Murphy; made a pleasing start over hurdles when third in a 22-runner maiden at Navan in November; her renewal of rivalry with fourth-placed Salsinha could have a big bearing on the outcome.
runners.comment: Point winner, one place in front of Salsinha on hurdling debut, likely to go close

runners.name: Poetic Twist
runners.spotlight: Hurdling newcomer has shown ability in bumpers; promising fourth on debut at Cork behind subsequent Grade 2 bumper winner Moonverrin; soundly beaten at Ayr in February; Jack Kennedy rides the stable's Cool Native
runners.comment: Hurdling newcomer has shown ability in bumpers, Jack Kennedy rides Cool Native

runners.name: Brosna Queen
runners.spotlight: Won a Punchestown bumper (good-to-yielding) last June on her second start; well beaten in two Listed bumpers after a break; found to be clinically abnormal following her hurdling debut at Wexford last month.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, excuse offered (clinically abnormal) following hurdling debut at Wexford

runners.name: Jos Secret
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point, and in a 2m Cork bumper last season before finishing in mid-division in a competitive 2m4f mares' bumper at this venue; did not appear again until odds-on favourite for a maiden at Catterick a month ago; failed to threaten a 15l winner; may do better in a stronger-run contest.
runners.comment: Useful bumper form, needs to step up on the form of her hurdles debut at Catterick

runners.name: Orinoco Walk
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three bumpers; down the field in two maiden hurdle outings, including a Leopardstown contest in which Alliteration was second.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three bumpers, down the field in two maiden hurdle outings

runners.name: Cool As A Cat
runners.spotlight: Dam 2m/2m4f hurdle winner for Des McDonogh, sister to Jim Dreaper-trained hurdle/chase winner Mr Cosmopolitan; best watched.
runners.comment: Dam 2m/2m4f hurdle winner, sister to 2m hurdle/chase winner Mr Cosmopolitan, best watched

runners.name: Lady Fromentro
runners.spotlight: Made little impression at Wexford on debut; second-string for the trainer of Salsinha.
runners.comment: Made little impression at Wexford on debut; second-string for the trainer of Salsinha

runners.name: Baby Fish
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in two Navan outings; second-string for the trainer of Simply Natural.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in two Navan outings, second-string for the trainer of Simply Natural

runners.name: Mollys Star
runners.spotlight: A distant fifth in a maiden hurdle at Navan; well beaten in a Cork bumper; up against it.
runners.comment: A distant fifth in a maiden hurdle at Navan, well beaten in a Cork bumper, up against it

runners.name: Thurman
runners.spotlight: Modest form in two races before going lame at Leopardstown's Christmas meeting in 2024; absent since, best watched.
runners.comment: Went lame at Leopardstown's Christmas meeting in 2024, absent since, best watched

runners.name: Ur My Fav
runners.spotlight: Only 4yo in the field; no merit in her three runs; can be left out of calculations.
runners.comment: Only 4yo in the field, no merit in her three runs, can be left out of calculations.

runners.name: Clash Gold
runners.spotlight: Has failed to build on a mildly promising bumper debut; lacks hurdling experience.
runners.comment: Has failed to build on a mildly promising bumper debut lacks hurdling experience

runners.name: Mind The Steps
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in two point-to-points and has struggled in two maiden hurdle attempts.
runners.comment: Pulled up in two point-to-points and has struggled in two maiden hurdle attempts

runners.name: Strong Contender
runners.spotlight: Poor last of three finishers in a 3m maiden hurdle at Sligo late October; unlikely to feature
runners.comment: Poor last of three finishers in a maiden hurdle at Sligo late October, unlikely to feature

races.name: BOYLE Sports Mares Handicap Chase (Listed Race)

runners.name: Kilbarry Saint
runners.spotlight: Off the mark chasing on tenth attempt in 2m5f Clonmel mares' beginners last month (soft): highly-tried on occasions, notably when second to Only By Night at Exeter last year and to Spindleberry at Clonmel in November; back in handicap company and can go well.
runners.comment: Recent Clonmel winner back in handicap company and can go well
runners.owner: Mrs Con OKeeffe & Tadhg OSullivan & Anthony OKeeffe

runners.name: Ballybawn Belter
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over fences when easy winner of 2m4f Limerick Listed handicap at Christmas (soft-to-heavy); out of frame in competitive Leopardstown handicaps twice since, most recently running well for long way and may have got a bit closer but for mistake last; drops in grade, one to consider.
runners.comment: Good run in better-class Leopardstown contest latest; contender if she stays

runners.name: Ad Caelum
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over hurdles; 0-9 over fences but has been set some stiff tasks this season; lots to find with Kilbarry Saint on recent Clonmel running and 4lb out of handicap.
runners.comment: Lots to find with Kilbarry Saint on recent Clonmel running and 4lb out of handicap

runners.name: May Call You Back
runners.spotlight: Improved dramatically from a low base to record four wins in 2025 including 2m Tramore handicap chase in October; also good second in 2m Navan handicap chase, possibly found 3m4f stamina test too much at Punchestown latest; 4lb wrong here but could go well if she stays.
runners.comment: 3m4f too far latest; 4lb wrong here but could go well if she stays

runners.name: Mystical Goddess
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner won Clonmel maiden hurdle last year and some decent efforts in defeat in graded races; 0-5 chasing but decent placings including here in November and last month at Limerick when only weakening late in finishing third in 2m4f handicap; cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: Recent Limerick run a solid one; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Broomfield Bijou
runners.spotlight: Won mares' beginners' at Wexford in 2024, her sole win over fences from 11 attempts; fine comeback run on yard debut when clear second in 2m2f Thurles handicap in February, despite being badly hampered by leader's fall at the last; didn't seem to stay this trip at Leopardstown last month so stamina concerns.
runners.comment: Didn't seem to stay this trip at Leopardstown last month so stamina concerns

runners.name: Bioluminescence
runners.spotlight: Won Grade 3 novice hurdle and Grade 2 novice chase, both 2m6f Limerick mares' events; last season's highlight was a second in C&D Mares' Listed Novice; below best in three runs this term although latest second to classy Dinoblue in 4-runner 2m Opera Hat Chase at Naas was a solid effort over an inadequate trip; drops in class and top-weight respected despite conceding lots of weight all round.
runners.comment: Respected but top-weight vulnerable conceding lots of weight all-round

runners.name: Kiltealy Park
runners.spotlight: Won this race last year off 4lb lower mark (soft) in first-time blinkers and good comeback run early this term in Galway Blazers; seen just twice since, well held here in November and 3m looked beyond her at Leopardstown over Christmas; doesn't come here in the same form as last year and cheekpieces now replace blinkers.
runners.comment: Last year's winner doesn't come here in the same form; cheekpieces replace blinkers

runners.name: Crescent Moon
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in 2m4f novice handicap hurdle here last season; best of five attempts chasing this term when fair fifth (Mystical Goddess third) in 2m5f mares' handicap here in November; pulled up latest two outings so caution advised; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Ran well here in November but pulled up the last twice; caution advised

runners.name: Me Wee Bonnie Lass
runners.spotlight: 3-7 over hurdles but not the same level a chaser after eight attempts, okay run in 2m5f Cheltenham mares handicap in December, most recently chased home a ready 9l winner in similar Navan contest in February; 3lb wrong here so improvement needed.
runners.comment: Couple of efforts in mares' handicaps give her place claims but 3lb wrong here

runners.name: Secret She Keeps
runners.spotlight: Won 2m5f Thurles mares' beginners' chase in 2022 for Henry de Bromhead; only six runs since then, including fourth behind Kiltealy Park in this race last year, absent since pulled up behind several of these here in November; hard to fancy on return, especially given she's 6lb wrong here.
runners.comment: Last year's fourth but hard to fancy on recent form and 6lb wrong too

races.name: Blackrock College AFC Ladies National Handicap Chase

runners.name: Vaureal
runners.spotlight: Second for this rider when favourite for this race last season and a C&D winner for her last November; kept ticking over with a cross-country run and a hurdles outing in February; strong chance.
runners.comment: Second in this race last year for this rider, also a C&D winner, likely player

runners.name: Lisleigh Lad
runners.spotlight: Won hunter chases last season at this venue and Cork; two point-to-point runs during the autumn campaign well below best; stepped up on previous handicap chase form when second to today's rival Harbour Highway at Leopardstown last month; rider Elizabeth Gale 2-2 on AW at Dundalk this year.
runners.comment: Second to Harbour Highway at Leopardstown, that rival may have the upper hand again

runners.name: Binge Worthy
runners.spotlight: Point and dual hurdles winner; ordinary chase form; no threat in first-time cheekpieces in 3m4f National Trial at Punchestown; a little better at Leopardstown but needs to find extra; cheekpieces discarded, blinkers tried.
runners.comment: Point and dual hurdles winner, ordinary chase form, cheekpieces discarded, blinkers tried.

runners.name: Beggars Rock
runners.spotlight: Won a Kilbeggan beginners' chase (2m4f, good) last May for Declan Queally; placed over 2m4f in the first of two outings for Gordon Elliott in January; pulled up here on the second; has joined Ian Donoghue who tries a new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Pulled up here in January, has joined Ian Donoghue who tries a new headgear combination.

runners.name: Choccabloc
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner and fair 128-rated hurdler for Nicky Henderson, stayed 2m5f; beaten 14l in second over C&D in January before being pulled up in a 3m event at Navan; suffered the same fate in a Limerick race won by Kings Hill; questions to answer.
runners.comment: Second here in January, pulled up in two of his last three starts,  questions to answer

runners.name: Aurea Fortuna
runners.spotlight: Comfortably justified favouritism at Punchestown last October (2m7f, good to yielding) when returned to fences, adding to his summer hurdles win at Galway; a long way below his best on heavy at Cheltenham in November; better at Leopardstown at Christmas; trainer has saddled the winner of this in two of the last three seasons; Aine O'Connor won it in 2017.
runners.comment: Yard has supplied the winner of this in two of the last three years, rider won it in 2017

runners.name: Thats About Right
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner; both his chase wins have come at Thurles on good ground; last two runs have been respectable but looks held by Harbour Highway and Lisleigh Lad on Leopardstown running; rider Phidelma Elvin won this in 2023.
runners.comment: Held by Harbour Highway and Lisleigh Lad on Leopardstown running, rider won this in 2023

runners.name: Lucky Lyreen
runners.spotlight: Peak rating of 135 over hurdles; won a 3m handicap chase at Down Royal (yielding-to-soft) in January, beating subsequent Ulster National winner Born Braver; in rear the Grand National Trial at Punchestown on latest; could go well if first-time blinkers do the trick; trainer teamed up with rider Charlotte Butler to win this in 2024.
runners.comment: Beat the subsequent Ulster National winner on penultimate start, rider won this in 2024

runners.name: The Gradual Slope
runners.spotlight: Won a valuable staying handicap chase at the 2024 Punchestown festival; ordinary form since then, although did well to maintain contact with top-class performers when 66-1 outsider of four at Down Royal on St Patrick's Day; flattered by proximity but the run implies a return to form; rider doing well in point-to-points, 8-22 this season.
runners.comment: Last success was in May 2024, interesting after his latest run, rider doing well in points

runners.name: Dutch Schultz
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose type; most recent of two chase wins came in a 2m1f Killarney handicap chase in August 2024; best effort last year when fourth in a 24-runner handicap chase at the Punchestown festival. best form over shorter than this and on better ground; lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: Best form over shorter trips than this and on better ground, lacks a recent run.

runners.name: Kings Hill
runners.spotlight: Thurles maiden hurdle winner in 2024 (2m, soft to heavy); took on strong opposition in a pair of 3m beginners' chases; beaten 33l when fifth of nine finishers on handicap chase debut at Navan (3m, heavy); came good over 2m2f at Limerick; top amateur rider won this in 2022.
runners.comment: Came good over 2m2f at Limerick last month, top amateur rider won this in 2022

runners.name: Quornofamonday
runners.spotlight: Gained a second chase success with a good display over 2m2f at Thurles in February; pulled up at the same venue last time after a 7lb rise; vulnerable on overall form.
runners.comment: Won at Thurles in February, pulled up at the same venue last time after a 7lb rise;

runners.name: Dancing Jeremy
runners.spotlight: Four chase wins include the valuable 2m4f Tim Duggan Memorial at Limerick at Christmas 2024; has run only four times since then; fortunate to win a 2m2f Thurles handicap chase in February, after the leader fell at the last; well beaten off revised mark at Limerick.
runners.comment: Fortunate to win a 2m2f Thurles handicap chase in February, well beaten off revised mark

runners.name: Harbour Highway
runners.spotlight: Navan bumper winner in March 2024; lost form after a promising third in a Leopardstown maiden hurdle last March; weak form over fences until coming good with a Leopardstown defeat of today's rival Lisleigh Lad; 6lb higher now but holds a big chance for last year's winning jockey and multiple champion point-to-point rider Maxine O'Sullivan.
runners.comment: Beat Lisleigh Lad at Leopardstown, big chance for Maxine O'Sullivan who won this last year

runners.name: Wheres Frankie
runners.spotlight: Three-time chase winner; out of form; pulled up at Navan and Leopardstown on last two starts.
runners.comment: Three-time chase winner, pulled up at Navan and Leopardstown on last two starts

runners.name: Cavalry Master
runners.spotlight: Veteran six-time winner; a shadow of his former self in points and cross-country races, though not disgraced at 100-1 in a handicap chase at Galway last October.
