runners.comment: Veteran six-time winner, a shadow of his former self in points and cross-country races.
runners.owner: Peter Maher & M J Halligan & Jeffrey Brennan & Alan E Smith

runners.name: Lord Lariat
runners.spotlight: Without a win since landing the 2022 Irish Grand National; fell four out in a handicap hurdle here on New Year's Day; has come down 12lb in the ratings since pulled up in last year's Irish Grand National; rider Joanna Walton was aboard for a C&D win in 2021; needs a major revival but not ruled out.
runners.comment: 2022 Irish Grand National winner, also a C&D winner, needs a major revival, not ruled out

races.name: Tom Quinlan Electrical Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: He Cant Dance
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; useful form in five hurdling starts, never out of the first three; ran on well to take third at Navan in January (2m6f, soft to heavy); beaten 15l third in a Leopardstown Listed handicap two months ago (3m, heavy); new headgear combination tried,
runners.comment: Useful form in five hurdling starts never out of the first three, should be thereabouts

runners.name: Sine Qua Non
runners.spotlight: Won Lingstown point-to-point by a huge margin in November 2024; fair third on Navan bumper debut (2m, soft); off for over a year before third (beaten favourite) in a 2m3f maiden hurdle at Naas last month; improvement on the cards.
runners.comment: Has run only twice since a 2024 point win, should be better for last month's 2m3f Naas run

runners.name: Petit Secret
runners.spotlight: In the lead when falling at two out in a point-to-point for Pat Doyle in 2023; off the mark on stable debut in a bumper at this venue later that year; harmed his chance with an untidy round of jumping at Leopardstown last month after 820 days off.
runners.comment: Course bumper winner in 2023, harmed chance on comeback with an untidy round of jumping

runners.name: Midnight Jet
runners.spotlight: Killarney bumper winner last May (2m1f, yielding)  for Sam Curling; beaten 13l in third when 4-6 for hurdles and stable debut at Cork; slightly tame effort when stepped up in trip at Leopardstown in December.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, has shown potential to win a maiden hurdle, can make his presence felt

runners.name: Treasure Memory
runners.spotlight: Second to subsequent bumper winner Apache Tribe in a point-to-point last season; promising third in a 2m Galway maiden hurdle in October (yielding) before solid third of six in a Punchestown bumper; showed enough when third over 2m4f here in December to suggest he can win a maiden.
runners.comment: Two promising hurdle runs including over 2m4f at this venue, might prefer further

runners.name: Dubhain
runners.spotlight: Point winner, third in small-field bumpers at Down Royal and Ayr; raced too keenly on hurdling debut at Ayr and paid the penalty when weakening from the last; fair fifth at Down Royal last month; this race has greater depth.
runners.comment: Point winner, placed in small-field bumpers, fair hurdles form but this is demanding

runners.name: Minella Diamond
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner and second on hurdling debut in 2023; has come back from a two-year layoff; good second to 114-rated rival in a 2m maiden at this venue, fell here next start; fourth in a Limerick novice face last time; would get 12lb from He Can't Dance in a handicap.
runners.comment: Bumper winner, fair maiden hurdler, would get 12lb from He Can't Dance in a handicap

runners.name: Largy Star
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam winning hurdler, family associated with stamina; won two points on the northern circuit for Cormac Abernethy during the autumn campaign; no match for subsequent Cheltenham also-ran Love Sign d'Aunou in a Naas bumper.
runners.comment: Won two points, no match for Cheltenham also-ran Love Sign d'Aunou in a Naas bumper

runners.name: Le Questionnaire
runners.spotlight: Placed in two points Britain last December; He Can't Dance is the stable's number one.
runners.comment: Placed in two points Britain last December, He Can't Dance is the stable's number one

runners.name: Barnpark
runners.spotlight: Walk In The Park gelding easily won a Boulta maiden in November for Ian McCarthy; runner-up won his next start and now bumper-placed in Britain, third-placed Desertmore News has won twice over hurdles in Britain; might have been expected to do better when third in a bumper at Thurles; remains a good prospect.
runners.comment: Point-to-point reads form well, a little below expectations in a bumper, useful prospect

runners.name: Glenary Prince
runners.spotlight: Second foal; Poet's Word gelding, dam Forge Meadow was a bumper/smart hurdle winner for Jessica Harrington; trainer also runs bumper winner Midnight Jet.
runners.comment: Newcomer whose dam was a smart hurdler, trainer also runs bumper winner Midnight Jet

runners.name: Siege Of Carthage
runners.spotlight: Good pedigree; made a bad mistake four out when last of six at Dromahane on his only start in the 2024 autumn points campaign; chased home an easy winner in a bumper at Gowran four weeks ago.
runners.comment: Reserve, good pedigree, chased home an easy winner in a bumper at Gowran four weeks ago

runners.name: Whos Going
runners.spotlight: Two poor displays; stable has a strong contender in Jack Kennedy's mount He Can't Dance.
runners.comment: Two poor displays, stable has a strong contender in Jack Kennedy's mount He Can't Dance

runners.name: Ri An Stoirm
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in a maiden hurdle at Navan in February; one of the stable's outsiders here.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a maiden hurdle at Navan in February, one of the stable's outsiders here

runners.name: Another Fool
runners.spotlight: Decent French pedigree; making no impact when falling six out at Tinahely last November on only point-to-point start.
runners.comment: Decent French pedigree; making no impact when falling six out on only point-to-point start

runners.name: Itllbeworthit
runners.spotlight: E32,000 yearling; fourth foal; brother to five-time Flat winner Cherry Pink; dam unraced half-sister to Listed-placed middle-distance winner Mutebah, out of half-sister to US 1m2f Grade 2 winner Makderah; worth a look for future refernce
runners.comment: By Elzaam, brother to five-time Flat winner Cherry Pink, worth a look for future reference

runners.name: King Of The Greys
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to a point/hunter chase winner; dam unraced half-sister to bumper/hurdle winner Polly Peachum; from the family of the great Denman; shaped reasonably well when fourth in a maiden point at Ballindenisk last November; tough task in this grade.
runners.comment: Shaped reasonably well when fourth in a maiden point, faces a tough task in this grade

runners.name: Little Big John
runners.spotlight: E9,000 at three; third foal; half-brother to hurdle/chase winner Chapel Green; unraced dam out of half-sister to 1m2f Group 2 winner Polaire; makes debut for small Tipperary stable.
runners.comment: Half-brother to hurdle/chase winner Chapel Green, makes debut for small Tipperary stable

runners.name: Seek A Richer Life
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; Westerner gelding; dam placed in points, sister to a bumper winner; from a strong family, but is set a stiff task here first time out.
runners.comment: Westerner gelding is from a strong family but is set a stiff task here first time out

runners.name: Regno
runners.spotlight: Has finished in mid-field in two maiden hurdles; will need to obtain a handicap mark.
runners.comment: Has finished in mid-field in two maiden hurdles, will need to obtain a handicap mark

runners.name: Shortt Fuse
runners.spotlight: Naas run a month ago suggests she might have a chance in a handicap in due course.
runners.comment: Naas run a month ago suggests she might have a chance in a handicap in due course

runners.name: Cometothinkofit
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on debut; trainer has a useful candidate for this in the shape of Sine Qua Non.
runners.comment: Tailed off on debut, trainer has a useful candidate for this in the shape of Sine Qua Non

runners.name: Papagei
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner for James Ferguson; no wins since 2024; now rated 73 in that sphere; mid-field on hurdling debut  at Naas; posed no threat when falling three out at Downpatrick last Sunday.
runners.comment: Three-time Flat winner for James Ferguson, no encouragement from first two hurdle runs

runners.name: Leo Messy
runners.spotlight: Three-figure odds and down the field on both starts; can be ruled out.
runners.comment: Three-figure odds and down the field on both starts, can be ruled out

runners.name: Clone Cross
runners.spotlight: Best of five point-to-point runs a 12l third at Loughrea last October; remote sixth on hurdling debut at Downpatrick last Sunday.
runners.comment: Places in a point-to-point, remote sixth on hurdling debut at Downpatrick last Sunday

runners.name: Mor Go Leor
runners.spotlight: Ran once in 2024; already a veteran when winning a maiden point at Ballynoe 12 months ago; no impact in a recent maiden hurdle at Cork.
runners.comment: Already a veteran when winning a maiden point last season, no impact at Cork recently

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Novice Handicap Hurdle Series Final (Listed Race)

runners.name: Fillyoureye
runners.spotlight: Kilbeggan bumper winner a year ago on good ground; has raced on contrasting ground so far hurdling, notably second to a smart mare at Punchestown and most recently nicely improved when running away with albeit weaker 2m7f maiden at same venue in February (soft-to-heavy); should make his mark in handicaps and opening mark looks competitive.
runners.comment: Punchestown maiden winner shortlisted off a workable opening mark
runners.owner: Mrs N Flynn & John Joseph Flynn

runners.name: Tip Of The Wings
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner last season off the mark hurdling in 2m Limerick maiden in February (heavy), making all to beat a long odds-on favourite and subsequent winner emphatically; clear second to an all-the-way winner in 2m Navan novice following month when conceding plenty of weight; type to make his mark in handicaps and good chance he'll improve for this step up in trip; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Progressive 2m novice of late; good chance he'll improve for this step up in trip

runners.name: Vitorio Piel
runners.spotlight: Punchestown festival bumper (yielding) winner last term, got off the mark over hurdles at fourth attempt when dropped back to 2m in 4-runner Leopardstown novice last month, outpacing odds-on favourite from last (heavy); type to keep improving, stays at least 2m5f; one to consider on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Leopardstown novice winner one to consider on handicap debut

runners.name: Chase The Moon
runners.spotlight: Backed at big prices when scoring on hurdling debut at Gowran (2m, heavy) and best run yet on handicap debut over 2m7f at Punchestown in February, having to wait going to last but finishing his race out strongly on similar ground to win going away (third has won both chase starts since); 5lb rise looks workable, should go well.
runners.comment: Won going away over 2m7f at Punchestown; 5lb rise looks workable, should go well

runners.name: Soldierstown
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; promise in bumpers and off the mark in 2m4f Fairyhouse maiden hurdle (soft) in January; out of depth both starts since in good-class contests won by Zanoosh and makes handicap debut now; rider's claim a positive.
runners.comment: Maiden winner here out of depth of late; handicap debut and rider's claim a positive

runners.name: Mystic Malina
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; won weak maiden at Clonmel on second hurdles start and most recently relished step up to 3m to land Cork handicap last month, appreciating better ground under hold-up ride to win going away; 6lb rise okay, can go well if ground dries out.
runners.comment: Relished 3m to win at Cork; contender, especially if ground dries out

runners.name: Court Compliance
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on first three runs in maidens before landing 2m4f Punchestown contest in February, making all for clear-cut success (third has won since); unlikely to get such a soft lead here and possible stamina concerns on handicap debut.
runners.comment: All-the-way Punchestown maiden winner won't get such a soft lead here; stamina concerns

runners.name: Panjandrum
runners.spotlight: Point winner in Britain; bumper winner on soft ground at Clonmel in November on Irish debut and most recently off the mark hurdling (having burst blood vessel first occasion) in steadily-run 3m Limerick maiden last month (Fou De Toi poor third); much stiffer task here but evidently going the right way.
runners.comment: Limerick maiden winner faces much stiffer task here but evidently going the right way

runners.name: Found A Diamond
runners.spotlight: Likes a stamina test, second win coming over 3m1f at Navan in December in first-time cheekpieces (soft-to-heavy); creditable efforts off revised mark twice since, latest when third (Minella Emperor second) in 3m Pertemps qualifier at Haydock on better ground; place claims.
runners.comment: Solid Haydock run latest; trip suits and ground okay; place claims

runners.name: Daydream Nation
runners.spotlight: Point winner won a Gowran maiden hurdle last October and most recently relished step up in trip for handicap debut win over 3m at Leopardstown last month, coming off pace to assert from last (soft); progressive staying type but has 8lb rise to contend with.
runners.comment: Relished 3m at Leopardstown; progressive staying type but has 8lb rise to contend with

runners.name: Winning Smut
runners.spotlight: Twice successful early in the season on joining this yard, notably valuable 2m7f Galway handicap (good); didn't feature in Pertemps Qualifier at Cheltenham in October and absent since; likes good ground so possibly best watched on return here.
runners.comment: Good form on good ground early in season; lacks a recent run and ground concerns

runners.name: Divaboriva
runners.spotlight: Wexford bumper winner (soft) is 0-4 hurdling, standout run when second in 2m6f Thurles maiden; well below that level in 3m Grade 3 Cork novice latest and stamina concerns now.
runners.comment: Thurles maiden second but stamina doubts after recent Cork run at 3m

runners.name: Star Of The Week
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo whose three maiden runs have been at around 2m, most recently making all for decisive win at Naas in February (heavy); considerable jump in trip here for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Naas maiden winner a nice prospect but huge jump in trip here for handicap debut

runners.name: Blow Wind Blow
runners.spotlight: Bolted up in weak Thurles maiden in January (2m6f, heavy) and much better form month later in 2m4f handicap here, asserting from last to win snugly on similar ground; hit with 11lb rise so needs to improve again but quite likely.
runners.comment: Two heavy-ground wins of late; 11lb higher but type to keep improving; shortlisted

runners.name: Yeshil
runners.spotlight: Second in the Goffs Defender Bumper at the 2024 Punchestown festival; ran only once last season but returned from long absence to beat Vitorio Piel (made all) in 2m5f Punchestown maiden in November (heavy); contested graded novices' since, decent third at Limerick but too keen and didn't jump well when pulled up at Leopardstown at DRF; drops to handicap company and don't rule out.
runners.comment: Out of depth latest; needs to jump and settle better; drops to handicap company

runners.name: Hens Tooth
runners.spotlight: Point and debut bumper winner returned from a 619-day absence in December, coming good at third attempt with much improved effort in 2m6f Navan maiden in late February (heavy) under positive ride; every chance he'll stay this longer trip and should go well but Townend prefers Fillyoureye.
runners.comment: Navan maiden winner could go well on handicap debut but Townend prefers Fillyoureye

runners.name: Melbourne Shamrock
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 7yo came good on handicap debut in first-time cheekpieces when made plenty of use of in 2m7f Naas contest in February (heavy); dropped away tamely two out in 3m Pertemps Final at Cheltenham following month on quicker ground (blinkered); return to easier ground and cheekpieces back on so don't be surprised at a bounce back.
runners.comment: Ground possibly too quick at Cheltenham; respected on Naas win back on softer ground

runners.name: Minella Emperor
runners.spotlight: Won two bumpers on good ground last year; 0-6 hurdling but some good placings at up to 3m; tailed off in Pertemps Final at Cheltenham last time and yard look to have stronger contenders here.
runners.comment: Tailed off in Pertemps Final at Cheltenham; yard look to have stronger contenders

runners.name: Sept Etoiles
runners.spotlight: Got the trip well in winning 2m4f Down Royal maiden at Christmas (yielding) but beaten too soon for trip to be a reason at Thurles following month; back from a break and remains unexposed on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Thurles run a non-event; back from a break and remains unexposed on handicap debut

runners.name: Fou De Toi
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 5yo; placed on sole start in France and has underperformed market expectations on both starts for current yard this term, fair front-running fourth over 2m5f at Thurles and didn't appear to stay 3m when poor third to Panjandrum at Limerick; doesn't appear an obvious one on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Underperformed market expectations on both starts for current yard; not an obvious one

runners.name: Gin Tonic
runners.spotlight: Promising second on sole point-to-point start; made most for winning hurdles debut at Wexford (2m4f, good to yielding) and better in defeat when second to a progressive type here in 2m5f novice; never landed a blow however when favourite in similar Thurles contest last time so questions to answer entering handicap company.
runners.comment: Thurles run raises questions now entering handicap company

races.name: RYBO Handicap Hurdle (Listed Race)

runners.name: Mode Avion
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in 4yo C&D handicap in February (heavy) and better again when a clear second to a strong favourite in 2m1f Limerick conditions event last month on similar ground under today's rider, conceding plenty of weight; 1lb wrong but could go well off light weight.
runners.comment: Progressive 4yo could go well off light weight

runners.name: Davy Crockett
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 150-rated hurdler Mystical Power landed odds on opening three starts, including novice hurdles at the festival meetings of Galway and Listowel; way below best on heavy ground this term in Graded novices at Leopardstown and Naas however; remains unexposed on handicap debut, drying ground would suit; Walsh's pick.
runners.comment: Heavy ground against him this term; unexposed on handicap debut, drying ground would suit
runners.owner: John P McManus & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Bokamsin
runners.spotlight: Nicely improved on fifth attempt hurdling to land 2m Navan novice last month, making all in beating Tip Of The Wings (runs in 4.25) 6l in first-time tongue-tie; unlikely he'll be able to dominate in same way here but progressive and opening mark seems okay.
runners.comment: Much improved in tongue-tie to dominate at Navan; opening mark seems okay

runners.name: Putapoundinthejar
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins last term came at 2m, including Punchestown handicap off 117; quiet enough hurdles campaign this season, lots to find on latest DRF effort (heavy); better ground will suit.
runners.comment: Ground at DRF wouldn't have suited; potential improver on better ground

runners.name: Zillow
runners.spotlight: Both hurdles wins over 2m, including Listed handicap here in November; has struggled off revised mark since however, twice not looking to stay 2m5f, latest in Martin Pipe at Cheltenham; return to 2m in his favour and cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: C&D winner in November hasn't stayed 2m5f twice since; cheekpieces added and drops in trip

runners.name: Messerschmitt
runners.spotlight: Hasn't added to Galway 3yo maiden win although last month's return from winter break was encouraging, not fluent last when fourth of 18 behind Mondo Man in Imperial Cup at Sandown; runs off same mark here, rider's claim a positive.
runners.comment: Imperial Cup fourth one for the shortlist with rider's claim a positive

runners.name: Karbau
runners.spotlight: Smart novice hurdler last season (placed in Grade 1s) and, although defeated at short odds, produced a career-best RPR when second in 2m Naas Grade 3 (heavy) on return from nine months off; beaten favourite in County Hurdle latest but steady pace and not clear run didn't suit so sixth of 23 was a solid run in the circumstances; Townend's pick.
runners.comment: Things didn't pan out in his favour in County Hurdle; Townend remains loyal and respected

runners.name: Playtime
runners.spotlight: Handicap win over 1m4f (good) at Chester the highlight of five-race Flat career for Andrew Balding; off the mark hurdling on latest of four attempts, despite racing keenly in ordinary 2m Leopardstown maiden last month (soft-to-heavy); ideally wants decent ground so any drying will suit on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Leopardstown maiden winner would appreciate ground drying on handicap debut

runners.name: Powerful
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on handicap debut over this trip at Punchestown in November (heavy) and returned there on latest nicely improved to win over 2m5f in January; had a break since and reverts to 2m off 8lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Dual Punchestown winner likes going right-handed; 8lb higher after a break

runners.name: Kotkito Bello
runners.spotlight: On a roll earlier in the season, hat-trick of wins included over hurdles at Punchestown and Thurles; DRF fifth wasn't a bad run upped in grade but other may now be better handicapped (1lb wrong here).
runners.comment: Sequence ended in DRF; handicapper may have his measure now

runners.name: Murcia
runners.spotlight: Won a Grade 1 juvenile hurdle at Aintree last year; lightly raced this season, but DRF fourth of 19 to Bowensonfire in 2m handicap was a fine run; didn't count in County Hurdle latest but can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Didn't count in County Hurdle latest but can't be discounted on DRF run previously

runners.name: Helvic Dream
runners.spotlight: 9yo was second to Lord Erskine in this last year and later first past the post in the Galway Hurdle (demoted for interference); back from winter break when midfield in County Hurdle last month (close up behind Karbau and Hello Neighbour), tiring from last; entitled to come on for that.
runners.comment: Last year's second; comeback run in County wasn't bad and entitled to come on for that
runners.owner: Mrs Caroline Hendron & Mrs M Cahill

runners.name: Kalix Delabarriere
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner but has been poor on last three starts in handicaps, pulled up after never counting at DRF in February; cheekpieces added but very hard to be confident in (1lb wrong).
runners.comment: Pulled up after never counting at DRF; cheekpieces added but very hard to be confident in

runners.name: Missus Beeton
runners.spotlight: In good form last year for Shane Nolan and better again this year in cheekpieces, backing up Down Royal success with decisive 2m2f Leopardstown success last month under hold-up tactics; up an aggregate of 19lb so more needed from 2lb out of handicap but in such good form has to be respected.
runners.comment: Hat-trick seeker has 19lb rise to overcome and 2lb wrong; still respected

runners.name: Lord Erskine
runners.spotlight: Fine dual-purpose performer won this last year (beating Helvic Dream) off 9lb lower; won twice on Flat afterwards, back from a break when well held back over hurdles at DRF in February; 13yo probably needs a career-best over hurdles to follow up here.
runners.comment: Last year's winner now a 13yo and needs a career-best to follow up

runners.name: Hello Neighbour
runners.spotlight: Grade 1-winning juvenile hurdler last season; sustained an injury over the summer and has posted decent RPR's in three defeats since returning at Christmas, latest when 10th of 23 in County Hurdle after not clear run (only 1l behind Karbau); more needed but don't rule out.
runners.comment: Not clear run in County when only 1l off Karbau; more needed but don't rule out

runners.name: Sea Of Sands
runners.spotlight: Also-ran in this last year (off 1lb lower) but made winning return to hurdles in 2m Punchestown conditions event in December (heavy); keen and never counted when pulled up at DRF last time however; bit to find.
runners.comment: Also-ran in this last year (off 1lb lower); pulled up at DRF latest, has a bit to find

runners.name: Puturhandstogether
runners.spotlight: Last season's Fred Winter winner at Cheltenham never threatened on return there last month in 2m5f Coral Cup; previously hampered at least twice when well held behind Zillow over today's C&D; Walsh prefers Davy Crockett.
runners.comment: Cheltenham also-ran hampered here previously; Walsh prefers Davy Crockett

runners.name: Sony Bill
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins in France in spring 2024; yet to score for Willie Mullins in six attempts but set some stiff tasks, most recently didn't seem to stay 2m5f of Coral Cup at Cheltenham; return to 2m may suit but yard look to have stronger contenders.
runners.comment: Didn't stay in Coral Cup; return to 2m may suit but yard look to have stronger contenders

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Total Enjoyment Mares INH Flat Race (Listed Race)

runners.name: Lizzie Twigg
runners.spotlight: Poet's Word sister to 1m-1m4f AW winner Trishuli River and half-sister to Italian hurdle winner Natris; winning debut in Down Royal mares' bumper last month, seeing it out well under strong handling; more needed but possible.
runners.comment: Strongly-ridden for Down Royal debut win; more needed but possible

runners.name: Wonderful Everyday
runners.spotlight: Did well to beat some the geldings on debut in Goffs Defender Bumper at Punchestown a year ago (2m, yielding) but has run nowhere near that level in both starts since on softer ground, dropping out tamely at Huntingdon and never sighted in Grade 2 at DRF in February; sets the standard on her Punchestown form but hard to be confident in on recent efforts.
runners.comment: Sets the standard on her Punchestown form but hard to be confident in on recent efforts

runners.name: Lilannbee
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at fourth attempt when allowed to dictate at Punchestown in December (yielding); out of depth in Grade 2 mares' event at DRF since; more exposed than several of these.
runners.comment: Punchestown winner out of depth in Grade 2 at DRF; more exposed than several of these

runners.name: Some Pretender
runners.spotlight: Had two subsequent winners behind when justifying favouritism at Galway on debut in August (2m1f, good); stiffer task since in Listed mares' events on much softer ground, decent third at Gowran and front-running third to a warm favourite at Navan (second narrowly beaten in Grade 2 afterwards), racing a bit keen; entitled to be thereabouts, especially if settling better.
runners.comment: Decent efforts in similar events of late; should be thereabouts

runners.name: Belladaball
runners.spotlight: Respectable efforts both here and at Down Royal (fourth to Lizzie Twigg) in February when ridden handier; more needed.
runners.comment: Respectable efforts both here and at Down Royal in February; more needed

runners.name: Moyneard
runners.spotlight: Sister to winning hurdlers Coco Kolada (2m1f) and Long Draw (2m4f/3m); long-absent until staying-on second to front-running Kiltybo at Thurles in February (soft-to-heavy) but beaten a lot further by same rival at Naas following month, having been held up in a steadily-run affair; may yet improve, especially in a truly-run race.
runners.comment: Steady pace may not have suited at Naas; may yet improve, especially in a truly-run race

runners.name: Something Fabulous
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in second bumper, nicking it before the home turn at Tramore in August (good); two months later didn't get home as well over 2m3f at Cork when third; back from winter break with hood fitted; probably wants better ground.
runners.comment: Tramore winner back from winter break with hood fitted; probably wants better ground

runners.name: Voladora Nora
runners.spotlight: Pick of three bumper efforts when third at Listowel; back from winter break and needs to improve plenty to feature.
runners.comment: Listowel third returns from winter break and needs to improve plenty to feature

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Nevermindtherain
runners.spotlight: Second foal; half-sister to last year's 7f/1m 2yo winner Puerto Rico (inc Group 1; RPR 116); dam Group-placed 1m 2yo winner (102), sister to winners Misty For Me (7f-1m2f Group 1 inc Irish 1,000 Guineas) and Ballydoyle (1m 2yo Group 1); by a classy sire and from a quality family; top yard is in form so on the shortlist.
runners.comment: By a classy sire and from a quality family; top yard is in form so goes on the shortlist
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Derrick Smith & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Aedhmar
runners.spotlight: Ninth foal; half-sister to seven winners inc Head Mistress (5f 2yo inc Listed; RPR 97), Dollerina (Group-placed 7f; 97), Rule The Waves (8.4f 2yo; 94) and Crags (US 5f turf); by a quality sire and related to several smart sorts; cost 270,000gns as a yearling; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Related to several smart sorts; cost 270,000gns as a yearling; on the shortlist

runners.name: Celestial Crown
runners.spotlight: Third foal; closely related to 1m2f winner Mao Shang Wong (inc AW; RPR 80), half-sister to 6f 2yo winner Chicago Pope (83); dam 7f AW winner (99), from the family of 1m2f-1m4f Group 1 winner Ghaiyyath; by a first-crop sire and cost E60,000 as a yearling; the market can guide.
runners.comment: By a first-crop sire and cost E60,000 as a yearling; the market can guide

runners.name: Matriarchal
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to winners Captain Britain (Australian 6f-1m) and Customcut (Italian 8.5f); dam French 9.5f winner, half-sister to 1m4f/12.5f Group 2 winner Loxley; a 45,000gns vendor buyback as a yearling; yard can get debut winners but likely best watched.
runners.comment: 45,000gns vendor buyback as a yearling; yard can get debut winners but likely best watched

runners.name: Velozee
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Betty Loch (7f) and Scots Pine (Omani/UAE 7f-1m1f), out of 7f Listed winning half-sister to US/Canadian 1m Grade 1 winner Master Of The Seas; E92,000 yearling is by a quality sire and from a respected yard; check the market.
runners.comment: E92,000 yearling is by a quality sire and from a respected yard; check the market

runners.name: Woodlawn Lily
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to winners Anthaia (Italian 5f-6f inc 2yo Listed; RPR 99), Mi Esperanza (5f; 92), Marinas Tina (US 7.5f/1m turf) and Sphere Of Fire (Italian 6f-1m); dam prolific Italian 5f-6f winner (inc 2yo); by a useful sire; related to several speedy sorts; seems yard second-string.
runners.comment: By a useful sire; related to several speedy sorts; seems yard second-string

runners.name: Your Song
runners.spotlight: Closely related to French Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner Tea Dance (RPR 84), half-sister to four winners inc Galinea (French 6.5f-1m 2yo inc AW; 92); dam French Group-placed 5f 2yo winner (93); by a useful sire and cost 32,000gns as a yearling; yard has trained a 2yo winner this season; respected.
runners.comment: Cost 32,000gns as a yearling; yard has trained a 2yo winner this season; respected
runners.owner: Miss Kerri Radcliffe & Arthur P Hoyeau & Giselle De Aguiar

races.name: Down Syndrome Centre Cork Rated Race

runners.name: Oh Cecelia
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D maiden last May but didn't progress from that over various trips; sold for 9,000GBP in January and got back to form in winning a 5f Naas handicap on stable debut; raised 7lb for that but is race-fit and has to be respected; fifth in the 1.57 Curragh Friday.
runners.comment: C&D winner last year got back to form at Naas; raised 7lb for that; fifth Friday at Curragh

runners.name: Shadow Run
runners.spotlight: Was regressive in three maiden runs last year but an improved effort on handicap bow on return after being gelded; was beaten a half-length by Oh Cecelia then and is 1lb wrong with her at today's weights but there still shouldn't be much between them; blinkers retained.
runners.comment: Beaten a half-length by Oh Cecelia at Naas but is 3lb better off today and should go close

runners.name: Joyful Tidings
runners.spotlight: Beat the useful Arugam Bay to win a Tipperary maiden on debut last year over this trip; wasn't as good over 6f afterwards; gelded since and a hood is tried; needs to return to that debut effort.
runners.comment: Maiden winner over this trip not as good over 6f after; needs to return to debut effort

runners.name: Amiata
runners.spotlight: Won a Navan maiden (yld) over this trip last August; wasn't beaten far on his next three starts between 5f-6f, the latter two at Dundalk; the return to 5f and easy ground should suit so is one to consider.
runners.comment: Maiden winner over this trip; easy ground should suit so is one to consider

runners.name: Midnight Dusk
runners.spotlight: Was a ready winner of a Dundalk maiden over 6f on second start but wasn't as good in a conditions event there since; reportedly wasn't at home on easy ground at this track on debut so that is a concern.
runners.comment: AW winner reportedly wasn't at home on easy ground at this track on debut

runners.name: Nakamura
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back nurseries at Navan and this track last term over 6f; ran a cracker to be fourth in the Birdcatcher at Naas next time off 1lb higher than today; has plenty of early speed so should be comfortable at this trip; looks the stable selected so respected.
runners.comment: Dual 6f winner incl' here; has plenty of early speed so should be comfortable at this trip

runners.name: Michaels Well
runners.spotlight: By a leading sire, especially of sprinters; both runs over 6f last year; improved from his narrow Naas debut defeat to win at Navan on soft next time; no easy task under top-weight dropped to 5f on return but still progressing so respected.
runners.comment: No easy task under top-weight dropped to 5f on return but still progressing so respected

runners.name: Chicago Pope
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark on final start last year when winning a Naas maiden over this trip, narrowly beating Michael's Well; the cheekpieces worn then are left off and has been gelded since; has early speed and could cope with this trip but looks the stable second-string.
runners.comment: 6f winner has early speed and could cope with this trip but looks the stable second-string
runners.owner: Anthony Paul Smurfit & Samuel M Mencoff

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Maiden

runners.name: Cardiff By The Sea
runners.spotlight: Chased home Charles Darwin on debut at Naas (5f, good); ran a cracker when a 1.75l fourth behind True Love in the Queen Mary (5f, gd-fm) at Royal Ascot; reportedly slightly lame post-race when disappointing on final run of 2025 in listed heat at Naas; sets a high standard and hard to beat if handles ground.
runners.comment: Smart form last year including in the Gr' 2 Queen Mary, the one to beat if handles ground
runners.owner: P J OBrien Mrs P Shanahan & M OFlynn

runners.name: White Smoke
runners.spotlight: Rated 80 and ran well in defeat at times last year; narrowly denied at the Curragh (yld-sft) in September; reportedly in season when disappointing at Navan in October and AW may not have suited when last seen at Dundalk in November; 5f could suit and looks the chief danger to Cardiff By The Sea.
runners.comment: Rated 80, some good runs in defeat last year, 5f could suit, looks the chief danger

runners.name: Fates Gambit
runners.spotlight: Went close on debut at Dundalk (7f) in January but below that level twice since including on turf debut at the Curragh (6f, heavy) 20 days ago; better ground could help and worth a go at this shorter trip; place claims.
runners.comment: Second on debut at Dundalk, heavy ground may not have suited latest, place chance
runners.owner: Gary Devlin & N W T Dalzell

runners.name: Wearing Stars
runners.spotlight: Left form of first two runs behind when a close third at Dundalk in November (7f); beaten 1l when third again at Dundalk (6f) when last seen in December; rated 73 and worth a try over 5f; place shout if she's ready after 106 days off.
runners.comment: Third at Dundalk the last twice, 5f worth a go, rated 73, place shout if fit after break

runners.name: Thenandnow
runners.spotlight: Officially rated 75 after four runs at two, the best of which was a 1.75l second of 8 on debut at Leopardstown (7f, gd-fm); well beaten both starts on easier ground at 6f-7f so probably needs the track to dry out on first try at the minimum trip.
runners.comment: Best efforts last year on good ground at 7f, needs the track to dry out on first try at 5f

runners.name: Starman Tom
runners.spotlight: Starman gelding; half-brother to winners Ms Willpower (6f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 94) and Kessaar Power (6f/1m inc AW; 83); dam 6f AW 2yo winner (75), half-sister to 1m Group 3 winner/Irish 1,000 Guineas runner-up Ardbrae Lady; market best guide.
runners.comment: Starman gelding, half-brother to two winners, dam 6f 2yo winner, market best guide
runners.owner: Poor Fools Syndicate/Deirdre Cotter/Thomas W. McGrath

runners.name: Cashel Blue
runners.spotlight: Space Blues filly; 3,000GBP yearling; dam 5f-6f winner (inc 2yo/AW/Listed; RPR 106), half-sister to US 5.5f winner Funny Page, out of unraced close relative of US 7f Grade 2 winner and leading sire Distorted Humor; likely best watched on debut unless backed.
runners.comment: Space Blues filly, dam a 5f-6f Listed winner, likely best watched on debut unless backed

runners.name: Merry Blacksmith
runners.spotlight: Never figured in maidens at Listowel (7f, heavy) and Navan (6f, soft) last year; will qualify for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Modest efforts in both starts last year, qualifies for handicaps after this

runners.name: Snap Decision
runners.spotlight: Huge odds and has run accordingly in three 7f AW maidens; not easy to make a case for dropped back in trip.
runners.comment: Huge odds and no show in three 7f maidens, hard to fancy dropped in trip

runners.name: Classical Symphony
runners.spotlight: 100-1 and beat just one rival home on Curragh debut in October 2024; gelded and has moved yards; can only watch after long absence.
runners.comment: Off since heavy debut defeat in Oct' 2024, gelded, new yard, best watched

runners.name: Ace King Queen
runners.spotlight: Two runs as a 2yo for the late Kevin Prendergast in 2024; promise on debut at Navan when not far behind a future Group 3 winner but last at Down Royal on second start; huge layoff to overcome and best watched on first run for new yard unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: Promise on debut at two, not seen last year, new yard, watch unless market speaks

runners.name: Sparky Sparky
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 11-2 on debut at Naas (6f, yld-sft) last September and made late gains from the back to finish a 5.5l fourth of 12; gelded since; not sure the drop back to 5f will suit on the evidence of that debut run.
runners.comment: 11-2 and late gains for a 5.5l fourth on debut at Naas, gelded since, drop to 5f a query

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Cork Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Bucanero Fuerte
runners.spotlight: Group 1 Phoenix Stakes winner returned from an aborted stud career to show hich-class form last year; won a Group 3 plus a Listed race and was third in the Group 1 Flying Five Stakes; goes well fresh and looks the one to beat with yard amongst the winners.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner goes well fresh and looks the one to beat with yard amongst the winners
runners.owner: AMO Racing Limited & Giselle De Aguiar

runners.name: King Cuan
runners.spotlight: Four wins at 6f-7f with his best effort coming when unluckily denied by Art Power in a Group 3 (soft) at the Curragh last autumn; below form next time at Ascot but is rated 109 and this ground suits so can be a big player.
runners.comment: Just denied in a Group 3 last term; rated 109 and this ground suits so can be a big player

runners.name: Wannabe Royal
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over 7f ran well over that trip on return in the Listed Gladness Stakes last week; has early speed so could cope with this trip but has loads to find with some of these.
runners.comment: Decent run in the Listed Gladness Stakes last week; has loads to find with some of these

runners.name: Kerdos
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won since taking the Group 2 Temple Stakes in 2024 when with Clive Cox; posted a reasonable debut run for this yard at Dundalk and this ground will suit but needs a return to his best to score.
runners.comment: Reasonable debut run for this yard at Dundalk but needs a return to his best to score
runners.owner: Michael Scaife/Liam Ferguson/Simon Murphy/H S Khunkhuna

runners.name: Unique Journey
runners.spotlight: Smart handicapper has won three times at this trip and posted a solid return at the Curragh; his race-fitness helps and this ground is fine but has a fair bit to find with some of these.
runners.comment: Race-fitness helps and this ground is fine but has a fair bit to find with some of these
runners.owner: N W T Dalzell/James Morgan/Karl Kirwan/Mrs John Murphy

runners.name: Chicago Call
runners.spotlight: Won a Fairyhouse maiden over this trip last July and better form when just denied in a Listed event at the same track in September; below form over 7f final start; gelded; has a fair bit to find.
runners.comment: Second in a Listed event last year; below form over 7f final start; a fair bit to find
runners.owner: Samuel M Mencoff & Anthony Paul Smurfit

runners.name: Magical Vision
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over 5f-6f and solid handicap efforts in defeat lately but is rated only 81 so has loads to find in this grade.
runners.comment: Solid efforts in defeat lately but is rated only 81 so has loads to find in this grade

runners.name: Soldiers Tree
runners.spotlight: Easily won a 7f maiden at Dundalk on second start back in January; however, this is a major step up in class and he has loads to find.
runners.comment: AW winner takes a major step up in class and he has loads to find

runners.name: Thunderbear
runners.spotlight: Just five runs last year but won a Listed race at Naas over this trip in October before a disappointing run in France, even allowing for a less than smooth passage; can run well if back to his best.
runners.comment: Listed winner last backend and can run well if back to his best

runners.name: Powerful Nation
runners.spotlight: Speedy sort has won three times at 5f including twice at this track; his form deteriorated from mid-season onwards and has plenty to find with Bucanero Fuerte.
runners.comment: Form deteriorated from mid-season onwards and has plenty to find with Bucanero Fuerte

runners.name: Navassa Island
runners.spotlight: Just 1-20 but has been kept in quality company for most of her career, placed several times in Group 3 and Listed company; will have to find improvement to take this.
runners.comment: Placed in Group 3 and Listed company; will have to find improvement to take this

races.name: Mallow Garden Festival 22nd, 23rd & 24th May Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Signora
runners.spotlight: Smart form on debut behind Lady Iman in Naas Group 3 (6f, good); didn't back that up when midfield in the Albany at Royal Ascot; raced keenly when reeled in late on seasonal reappearance in a 7f Curragh maiden (heavy); rated 92, preferred by Moore and the one to beat.
runners.comment: Smart form at two, keen when reeled in late on return at the Curragh, the one to beat
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Star Of Beauty
runners.spotlight: Best effort at two a 0.5l third of 13 in a 6f maiden at Naas on her second start in August; first run since a 5.75l sixth of 14 in a 1m maiden at Dundalk 29 days ago; visor goes on and drops in trip; rated 75 and has a place chance.
runners.comment: Good third at Naas in August, had pipe opener at Dundalk, rated 75, place shout; visored
runners.owner: David K Kelly & Mrs Pauline Condon

runners.name: Expert Dancer
runners.spotlight: Eyecatcher when making late gains on debut at Naas over 6f in October; another solid run when a 3.5l third on heavy at the Curragh (6f) nine days later; yard in form and the step up to 7f looks sure to suit; place claims.
runners.comment: Two promising 6f maiden runs last term, 7f should suit, yard in fine form, place shout

runners.name: Flower Forest
runners.spotlight: Siyouni filly; half-sister to Canadian 7f turf winner War Strategy; dam 7f 2yo Group 3 winner (RPR 106), sister to Group 1 winners Gleneagles (7f/1m), Marvellous (1m), Happily (7f/1m) and Joan Of Arc (10.5f), out of 6f 2yo Group 2 winner; impeccably bred but Signora preferred by Ryan Moore.
runners.comment: Siyouni filly, dam a sister to Gleneagles, Marvellous & Happily, Moore prefers stablemate
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Girl Bear
runners.spotlight: Went close on debut at Galway (7f, soft) in September; 5-1 but soundly beaten on seasonal reappearance at the Curragh (7f, heavy); should strip fitter now but needs to improve markedly on that latest effort.
runners.comment: Good effort on debut at Galway, below that level on seasonal return, needs more

runners.name: Flagstone Lady
runners.spotlight: US Navy Flag filly; E1,000 foal bought back for E21,000 as a yearling; half-sister to winners Wonder Kid (1m2f/1m4f, RPR 101), Bavardages (7f-1m2f inc 2yo/AW; 83) and Doves Of Peace (6f AW; 61); dam French Listed-placed 7f-1m1f winner (inc 2yo; 94); likely best watched unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: US Navy Flag filly, dam French Listed placed winner, watch unless the market speaks

runners.name: Mardi Gras Queen
runners.spotlight: Charlatan filly; half-sister to yard's winner Aspen Grove (1m-1m3f inc 2yo/US Grade 1; RPR 114) and Edwardian (5f 2yo; 87); dam US Graded stakes-placed 1m turf 2yo winner, half-sister to winners Skitter Scatter (7f 2yo Group 1); entered in the 1000 Guineas and worth a market check.
runners.comment: Charlatan filly, half-sister to yard's US Grade 1 winner Aspen Grove, check market

runners.name: Magna Kotcha
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 100-1 on debut at Naas (1m, soft) 13 days ago and weakened late on into an 8.25l sixth of 10; Keane takes over now and drop to 7f a plus but much more needed.
runners.comment: 100-1 on debut at Naas (1m) and weakened late into sixth, much more needed

runners.name: Wild Bessie
runners.spotlight: 66-1 and never better than midfield on debut in a 7f Dundalk maiden in September; not seen since and needs to improve plenty to feature here.
runners.comment: Off since midfield finish on Dundalk debut in Sept', needs to improve plenty on that

runners.name: Zermatt Star
runners.spotlight: Raced keenly on both starts last year when a 4.75l sixth at Punchestown (1m, gd-yld) and a 6.25l fifth at Gowran (8.5f, heavy) when sent off the 9-4 favourite; drops in trip now and needs to settle better.
runners.comment: Keen in both starts at two at around 1m, needs to settle better dropped in trip

runners.name: Divine Legend
runners.spotlight: Sent off at 9-1 on debut at Leopardstown (7f, yielding) in October and was beaten 14.5l into fifth of 13; this Irish 1000 Guineas entrant will need to step forward massively from that.
runners.comment: 9-1 and beaten 14.5l on debut in Oct', needs to step forward massively from that
runners.owner: Exors of the Late J Connaughton

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Noblesse Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies & Mares)

runners.name: Wemightakedlongway
runners.spotlight: Won a Navan 1m2f Listed race on soft last year; loads of good runs in defeat after in Group 1s up to 1m4f including in Oaks/Irish Oaks and a French Group 1; is ground-versatile and rated 110 so is the one to beat if ready enough on return.
runners.comment: Listed winner; ground-versatile and rated 110 so is the one to beat if ready on return

runners.name: Magical Zoe
runners.spotlight: Grade 3-winning hurdler turned into a useful Flat performer in 2024; finished third in a Fairyhouse Grade 3 over 1m6f before winning the Ebor over the same trip; however, hasn't been seen since and difficult to get behind her after a long absence; the market might reveal her readiness.
runners.comment: Won the 2024 Ebor on last start; difficult to get behind her after a long absence

runners.name: Elana Osario
runners.spotlight: Tremendously progressive last year when her three wins included the Group 3 Give Thanks Stakes over C&D last August; raced keenly when not at her best over 1m6f next time; should be a big player on seasonal debut despite carrying a 5lb penalty.
runners.comment: A Group 3 C&D winner; big player on seasonal debut despite carrying a 5lb penalty

runners.name: Snapretend
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m1f Punchestown maiden and a 1m4f Bellewstown conditions race last year; a creditable effort when third in a heavy-ground Curragh Listed race (also 1m4f) final start; has a bit to find with some of these rivals on return but could run well.
runners.comment: Decent sort has a bit to find with some of these rivals on return but could run well

runners.name: Its A Heartbeat
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden and handicap at the Curragh over this trip last year; started favourite but finished lame when poor in a Naas Listed contest final start; however, can be a player on return if resuming her progression.
runners.comment: Finished lame when poor in a Naas Listed on final start; can be a player on return though

runners.name: Moody
runners.spotlight: Won a Clairefontaine maiden over 1m1f on second start for Andre Fabre last year; sold for E125,000 to current trainer since; has a fair bit to find on form and seems the yard's second-string; tongue-tie plus cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Maiden winner in France; a fair bit to find on form and seems the yard's second-string

runners.name: Carmels Phoenix
runners.spotlight: The best of two maiden runs last year when beaten 1.75l at Bellewstown over this trip; however, this looks an ambitious starting point for this term.
runners.comment: Beaten 1.75l at Bellewstown over this trip on last start; however, this looks ambitious

runners.name: Speckled Meadow
runners.spotlight: Four handicap wins up to 1m2.5f but seems to stay this trip too; however, is rated only 74 so has a mountain to climb.
runners.comment: Four handicap wins up to 1m2.5f but is rated only 74 so has a mountain to climb

races.name: SIS Supporting Irish Racing Handicap

runners.name: Perisher
runners.spotlight: Promise in maidens at 7f-8.5f last season on varying ground; in good hands, has been gelded ahead of seasonal return and the market should reveal expectations on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Promise in maidens last year, gelded, handicap debutant worth market check

runners.name: Chapel Lane
runners.spotlight: Ran well in two of three maiden starts last year at 6f-1m on good and good to yielding ground; was 3l behind the 94-rated Killashee Warrior on final run of 2025 at Thurles (1m, gd-yld) in first-time visor (discarded); opening mark of 75 could be workable; could go well.
runners.comment: Solid maiden form, opening mark could be workable, go well if fit and handles ground

runners.name: Invincible Will
runners.spotlight: Won off 6lb lower at the Curragh (7f, yld-sft) last September on his seventh start (beat Sunrise Angel by a neck); never involved off 6lb higher upped to a mile at Leopardstown the following month; off since and has been gelded; yard amongst winners recently.
runners.comment: Won off 6lb lower last term, off since well beaten in Oct', gelded, yard amongst winners

runners.name: Felix Somary
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Sligo (6f, gd-yld) but no joy in three starts since, the last twice in handicaps; another modest effort on seasonal return; may have needed that but needs more off 2lb lower.
runners.comment: Sligo maiden winner, no joy since, dropped 2lb after modest comeback run, needs more

runners.name: Sunrise Angel
runners.spotlight: Beaten a neck by Invincible Will on handicap debut at the Curragh (7f, yld-sft) in September and 3lb better off with that rival here; raised 4lb and finished a 1l fourth of 13 on final run of 2025 at Dundalk (7f); yard in flying form and dangerous to discount.
runners.comment: 3lb swing for neck defeat to Invincible Will last year, yard in form, dangerous to dismiss

runners.name: Adel
runners.spotlight: Modest efforts in 1m maidens last year; gelded and returned with a midfield finish on handicap bow at Naas in the Madrid (7f, soft) from 4lb out of the handicap; shaped as though that was needed and this is less demanding; interesting.
runners.comment: Not a bad run when 4lb wrong in Madrid, this is less demanding, interesting runner

runners.name: Empty Wallet
runners.spotlight: Bellewstown maiden winner (1m, good); not disgraced in Listed heat at Tipperary (7.5f, good); stayed on after being outpaced on handicap debut at Leopardstown (1m, yld-sft) in October; 3lb lower now and yard had first time out winner on Wednesday.
runners.comment: Down 3lb after midfield run on h'cap bow in Oct', yard had first time out winner on Wed'

runners.name: Hambelton
runners.spotlight: Ran to a similar level in a trio of maidens last year at 7f-1m on easy ground and on the AW; has been gelded and open to improvement on handicap debut off what looks a fair mark; market can guide.
runners.comment: Similar form in three maidens last year, open to improvement on h'cap debut, check market

runners.name: Nod Of Approval
runners.spotlight: Promise in maidens and again on handicap debut at Leopardstown (1m, yld-sft) when last seen in October; dropped 1lb for that staying on 6.25l fifth of 12 and worth a market check on return.
runners.comment: Promise in maidens, okay run on h'cap debut, down 1lb, lacks a run, check market

runners.name: Love Orchid
runners.spotlight: Best effort in maidens came on her third start at Dundalk (6f) in August; non-runner on account of yielding to soft ground at Naas in September so may need the track to dry out to make an impact on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Best maiden effort over 6f at Dundalk, handicap debutante might prefer better ground

races.name: Inglis Sires' (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Streisand
runners.spotlight: Form superb. Blue Diamond winner then on speed, outgunned late but had enough in the tank for second in the Golden Slipper. Might be looking for this trip now. Hard to beat.

runners.name: Campione DItalia
runners.spotlight: Broke maiden at Randwick 28 Feb. Last at turn, ran on late in the Slipper. Decent alley and 1400m looks perfect. Go close.
runners.owner: Newgate Bloodstock China Horse Club Racing Et Al

runners.name: Paradoxium
runners.spotlight: Todman winner two back. Sent out $13 when unable to threaten in the Slipper. All the better and capable of making an impact if he gets the breaks and runs the trip.

runners.name: Rachini
runners.spotlight: Steps out for third run new stable/region. MIdfield in the Reisling then well rated in front and kept on to win at Wyong in second run from spell. Could give some cheek at odds.
runners.owner: Bjorn Baker Racing B E Risetto Et Al

runners.name: Zambales
runners.spotlight: Well back and struck some trouble in the Slipper. Fit from racing and not out of this.

runners.name: Miss Chanel
runners.spotlight: Very honest. Settled handy and responded well to fill the minors at Caulfield. More ground a plus. Warrants attention.
runners.owner: A G Harvey Yu Long Investments Et Al

runners.name: Fireball
runners.spotlight: Won the Inglis Millennium (RL) Randwick 7 Feb. Off pace, made limited headway and tough in the Slipper. Can challenge with even luck.
runners.owner: James Harron Bloodstock Colts TFI Et Al

runners.name: Tornado Valley
runners.spotlight: Freshened. Magic Millions runner-up. Got back, tempo against and wasn't totally disgraced in the Todman. Since impressed with a Rosehill trial win 24 Mar. Rise to 1400m a new challenge.
runners.owner: Bennett Racing P/L Rosemont Stud P/L Et Al

runners.name: Rich On Bubbles
runners.spotlight: Has a picket fence form line. All honours in the small field at Caulfield. Untried in the wet. Stiffer challenge here.
runners.owner: Lindsay Park Bloodstock T Poli Et Al

runners.name: Am I Dreaming
runners.spotlight: Knuckled down with purpose to score at Hawkesbury on debut when in the market. Big step up here but untapped.
runners.owner: J N Pilkington Ms G S Cam Et Al

runners.name: Dr Hook
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Ex M, W & J Hawkes in Victoria. Settled close and ran an honest race at Flemington second-up. Unknown in wet and needs a little bit more.
runners.owner: Mrs P L Crosby & R R Crosby

runners.name: Outspan
runners.spotlight: Handy, knuckled down with purpose in the Pago Pago at Rosehill as an odds drifter. Can again fill the minors here.

runners.name: Alibaba
runners.spotlight: Let-up (42-days). Blue Diamond Prelude winner three back. Warming into it late at Caulfield. Each way.
runners.owner: K M Keys Mrs L A Keys Et Al

races.name: Asahi Super Dry T J Smith Stakes (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Tentyris
runners.spotlight: Fresh. Won Black Caviar Lightning Flemington 14 Feb. Ran on late from the back at Flemington second-up. Jumps from better barrier.

runners.name: Joliestar
runners.spotlight: Freshened. High class galloper. Gave solid kick from handy position to triumph here in second run from spell. Since trialled at Rosehill 24 Mar.

runners.name: Jimmysstar
runners.spotlight: Gave solid chase in a good effort at Caulfield first up when in the market. Fitter second-up. Can make his presence felt.
runners.owner: A Kheir J A ONeill Et Al

runners.name: Magic Time
runners.spotlight: In the thick of it at Flemington two back 8 Nov. Ran on soundly from midfield at Caulfield first up. Shown can handle the wet here. Will be fitter second-up.

runners.name: Skybird
runners.spotlight: Given time to freshen up after 1st-up run. Kept finding the line without threatening here when off an extended spell. Since trialled at Caulfield Heath 26 Mar. Others are packing more punch.
runners.owner: M J Scalzo D J Scalzo Et Al

runners.name: Briasa
runners.spotlight: Valiant when just denied the minors at Rosehill first up when in the market. Will be fitter second-up. Can play a role with even luck.
runners.owner: I R Johnson & Dr A R Johnson

runners.name: Giga Kick
runners.spotlight: Now 4 weeks between runs. Quietly handled in back half, shaken up and finished full of running this track at second run back. Since trialled at Rosehill 24 Mar. Could give some cheek at odds.

runners.name: Mazu
runners.spotlight: Close placing in the Challenge Stakes at Randwick. Showed courage and tenacity when nailed late in soft going Kia Ora Galaxy at Rosehill at second run back. Ready to peak third-up. Outer barrier but has good gate speed.
runners.owner: Triple Crown Syndications M De Stoop Et Al

runners.name: Overpass
runners.spotlight: First-up (16-weeks). Right amongst it in top company. Dangerous fresh while indicating was forward winning a Randwick trial. Can lead or take a sit.
runners.owner: S G Darby P Halfhyde Et Al

races.name: Doncaster Mile (Group 1) (Handicap) (Turf)

runners.name: Sheza Alibi
runners.spotlight: Fresh. 100% win-place strike rate. Came from back half to post a commanding 3.2 len Open win this venue at $2.10. Nicely weighted.

runners.name: Encap
runners.spotlight: Never a threat after showing no early speed from the barriers at Rosehill at second run back. Good draw and a nice weight drop. Form sound at this course and journey and under less weight. Ages since last saluted but Sharper now and not out of this.
runners.owner: G Portelli R El Daghl Et Al

runners.name: Sixties
runners.spotlight: Scrubbed up from the wide gate and boxed on bravely for a 0.7 len win at Rosehill. Gate a worry. New to Randwick. Goes into wider exotics.

runners.name: Attica
runners.spotlight: Went home tamely at Rosehill at $71. Query wet. Could give some cheek at odds.

runners.name: Autumn Boy
runners.spotlight: Simply too good for them over a longer assignment at Rosehill. Has a light weight. Drawn favourably and a nice weight drop. Coming back in distance.

runners.name: Cristal Clear
runners.spotlight: In touch and fought on dauntlessly at Rosehill at first run back. Strips fitter second run back. Shapes as a rough place prospect.
runners.owner: Group One Thoroughbreds J J Azzopardi Et Al

runners.name: Linebacker
runners.spotlight: Rider found a forward spot at Rosehill but he put in a very lacklustre finish at first run for new trainer. Ready to peak third-up. Gets in light. Effective this track and distance and has a good history under Zac Lloyd this track.
runners.owner: F W Cook Mrs C M Cook Et Al

runners.name: Steparty
runners.spotlight: Now 4 weeks between runs. Won Hellova Street Stakes Launceston 25 Feb. Close up in sound performance All-Star Mile at Flemington over this trip. Handles the wet.
runners.owner: G J Baldwin D M Drum Et Al

runners.name: Vivy Air
runners.spotlight: Got back and hit line with a full head of steam at Rosehill. Fitter second-up. Goes into wider exotics.
runners.owner: Ciaron Maher Racing A R E Investments Et Al

runners.name: Scheelite
runners.spotlight: 4 weeks since last run. Fired up to lead and kept on valiantly at Flemington. Sticky draw to negotiate. New to Randwick.
runners.owner: Mrs L A Keys K M Keys Et Al

runners.name: Headley Grange
runners.spotlight: Won the 3yo+ Qlty Rosehill 11 Oct. Not disgraced in George Ryder Stakes at Rosehill in second run from spell at $41. Fitter for those two runs and ready to show best. Has wet form.
runners.owner: R J Manwaring S C Foley Et Al

runners.name: Militarize
runners.spotlight: Long time since last saluted. Ran on solidly at Rosehill. Tackling stronger line-up.
runners.owner: Newgate Bloodstock China Horse Club Racing Pty Ltd Et Al

runners.name: Evaporate
runners.spotlight: Close placing in the All-Star Mile at Flemington. In touch but couldn't find anything when pressured at Rosehill. Has form in wet. Ticks the track and distance win box.
runners.owner: Mystery Downs Mrs C M Cook Et Al

runners.name: Gringotts
runners.spotlight: Ridden cold, produced solid finish late at Rosehill second-up. Fitter for those two runs and ready to show best. Decent alley. Form sound this track and distance.
runners.owner: A Kheir J A ONeill Et Al

runners.name: Hellsing
runners.spotlight: Established lead at Muswellbrook Open, stuck to task and only denied late. Strikes a harder race.

runners.name: Pericles
runners.spotlight: Produced a searching finish into the minors at Rosehill at second run back. Could get into the placings.

races.name: ATC Australian Derby (Group 1) (Turf)

runners.name: Road To Paris
runners.spotlight: NZ Derby winner on a Good track. Freshened. Purton rides. Among the dangers.
runners.owner: R M Wanless & Mrs J M Wanless

runners.name: Observer
runners.spotlight: Vic Derby winner. Was a valiant but vanquished third in the Rosehill Guineas at Rosehill. On pacer. McDonald rides and makes a strong case out to 2400m.

runners.name: Storm Leopard
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Finished too well winning Tulloch Stakes at Rosehill. Unexposed at trip. Super fit from racing and not out of this.
runners.owner: MyRacehorse No 1 D G Alford Et Al

runners.name: Victorious Spirit
runners.spotlight: Caught deep and failed to threaten at Rosehill. Has his first look at 2400m here. Could bounce back.
runners.owner: P G Moody Pope Racing No 2 Et Al

runners.name: Green Spaces
runners.spotlight: Godolphin 3yo. Reserved at rear, late into the frame at Rosehill. Peaks now and added journey should assist. This looks very achievable.

runners.name: Deal Done Fast
runners.spotlight: Vic Derby placegetter. Never a threat after showing no early speed from the barriers at Rosehill in second run from spell. Query wet. Significantly up in distance. Can improve.

runners.name: Federalist
runners.spotlight: Close placing in the Phar Lap Stakes at Rosehill. Fair performance at Rosehill at $12. Unexposed at trip. Can make his presence felt.
runners.owner: Arrowfield Pastoral East Coast Agricultural Invstmnts Et Al

runners.name: Bingi
runners.spotlight: Got the wobbles and faded out of the placings at Rosehill. Likes the sting out. Leads, handy. Query going to 2400m but doubt they'll lead this time. Could improve.

runners.name: One Step Closer
runners.spotlight: Ran 4th Vic Derby. Not disgraced at Rosehill and looking for this trip. Each way.
runners.owner: C E Holt R J Miller Et Al

runners.name: Kaye Jay
runners.spotlight: Made all and just picked off late at Caulfield. Query wet. Fit and could give some cheek at odds.
runners.owner: Mrs B K Saunders & B A E Saunders

runners.name: Savisanta
runners.spotlight: Ran a cracking race when just edged out at Caulfield 21 Mar. Blinkers on. Could add some value to exotics.
runners.owner: C J Waller D B Flynn Et Al

runners.name: Dezignation
runners.spotlight: On a quick back-up. Well back and made limited headway in the Tulloch. Should get the trip but prefer easier grade.
runners.owner: MCS Racing Letmar Investments Pty Ltd Et Al

runners.name: Are You Kidding
runners.spotlight: Disposed of them easily on a soft deck at Beaumont. Bred to stay but this is a big step up from a Prov Bm58.
runners.owner: Arrowfield Pastoral Pty Ltd P B OBrien Et Al

races.name: Resolute Racing Madison Stakes (Grade 1) (Fillies & Mares) (Dirt)

runners.name: R Disaster
runners.spotlight: Smart mare, never out of first two in 14 starts (eight wins); yet another strong effort to win 6.5f Grade 3 Hurricane Bertie at Gulfstream four weeks ago by just over 5l from Sterling Silver, setting a good gallop and widening her advantage; admirable and speedy, but has had only a single Grade 2 strike and one 7f win; chance but may just prove vulnerable at this level.
runners.comment: Admirable front-runner with eight wins from 14, including easy Grade 3 latest; chance
runners.owner: Averill Racing LLC Two Eight Racing LLC & ATM Racing

runners.name: Mystic Lake
runners.spotlight: Smart form at best in 2025, winning Grade 2 Inside Information Stakes at Gulfstream in January; has generally competed in much lower grade of late, winning four black type events and minor handicap in last six starts; in good form at present but challenged at this level (only fourth in this last year).
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner early in 2025, has won five out of six minor events of late; hard to assess
runners.owner: Miller Racing LLC BAG Racing Stables LLC & Stefania Farms

runners.name: Praying
runners.spotlight: Won Grade 3 Prioress at Saratoga and Grade 2 Thoroughbred Club of America here in 2025, both at 6f; down the field in the 7f Breeders' Cup Filly & Mare Sprint afterwards; makes her reappearance and best form over shorter.
runners.comment: Smart sprinting filly in 2025, winning a Grade 3 and here in Grade 2; has to prove stamina

runners.name: Eclatant
runners.spotlight: Progressive form first four starts, culminating in her Grade 3 Forward Gal Stakes win (7f) at Gulfstream in February; inconsistent since but on latest start she posted a fast time to win an allowance race (6.5f) on same course comfortably by nearly 3l; could have regained threads of her improvement.
runners.comment: Inconsistent; last two wins at Gulfstream in Grade 3 and allowance, latter by 3l; chance

runners.name: Clicquot
runners.spotlight: Completed four-timer last year in blanket finish to Grade 1 Cotillion (8.5f) at Parx in September; 11-1, respectable fourth in Grade 1 Breeders' Cup Distaff (9f) at Del Mar subsequently, no extra closing stages; drops back to 7f for her reappearance and may just need this run.
runners.comment: Progressive in 2025, winning Grade 1 at Parx before Breeders' Cup fourth; may need opener
runners.owner: X-Men Racing Madaket Stables Et Al

runners.name: Sterling Silver
runners.spotlight: Tough 7yo, prolific at slightly lower level than Graded stakes; in good form lately, including Listed win at Gulfstream and second to R Disaster in Grade 3 Hurricane Bertie there four weeks ago; would need to run the race of her life in first-time blinkers (only fourth in this race in 2024).
runners.comment: Very useful 7yo, prolific at a lower level; in good form though beaten in Grade 3 latest

runners.name: Grand Job
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish mare, lightly raced; favourite on second start in ten months, improved to win Grade 2 Inside Information Stakes (7f) at Gulfstream in January by nearly 3l from Sterling Silver, taking a good hold tracking strong pace, clearing away turning in; unexposed at this trip and has good claims.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; improving on dirt and impressed in Grade 2 at Gulfstream in January; big chance

runners.name: Ragtime
runners.spotlight: Won three times in 2025 in late-running style, notably Grade 3 Dogwood Stakes at Churchill Downs in September (7f); favourite, only third in Grade 2 Raven Run (7f) here in October, left with too much to do; has gone well fresh (maiden winner on debut) and could make a bold bid.
runners.comment: Talented filly, best at 7f as when won Grade 3 last September; has gone well fresh; claims

races.name: Toyota Blue Grass Stakes (Grade 1) (Dirt)

runners.name: Further Ado
runners.spotlight: 20l winner of maiden at Keeneland in October in a fast time; has confirmed talent over 8.5f since in Grade 2 Kentucky Jockey Club at Churchill Downs (edged clear late) and Grade 3 Tampa Bay Derby (good second to very smart The Puma, keeping on but with head bit high); should be suited by 1m1f.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner then second in 8.5f Grade 3 at Tampa Bay; good claims on first go at 1m1f

runners.name: Class President
runners.spotlight: Won maiden at Gulfstream in December on debut; much improved to take Grade 2 Rebel Stakes at Oaklawn (8.5f) last month, always prominent before finding tons under pressure to outbattle smart Silent Tactic by a nose, hitting the line hard; tenacious and thriving with racing; 1m1f should suit.
runners.comment: Nose winner of 8.5f Grade 2 Rebel at Oaklawn, finding tons; 1m1f will suit; thriving
runners.owner: WinStar Farm LLC First Go Racing & CHC Inc

runners.name: Reagans Honor
runners.spotlight: Comfortable winner at Fair Grounds of a maiden in January and good quality allowance race (8.5f) in February; posted a fast time on that latest start, setting good pace, clearing off to win by nearly 7l without being at all hard ridden; shaped like a smart prospect and an intriguing runner in Graded company.
runners.comment: Comfortable winner of maiden and allowance (by nearly 7l) at Fair Grounds; smart prospect
runners.owner: West Point Thoroughbreds Ingordo Et Al

runners.name: Talkin
runners.spotlight: Good winner of Saratoga maiden before second (just over 6l behind impressive winner Napoleon Solo) in Grade 1 Champagne at Aqueduct as 2yo; disappointing in Grade 2 Remsen (drawn wide but tired badly) in December and Grade 3 Tampa Bay Derby (5l behind The Puma) four weeks ago; something to prove now.
runners.comment: Grade 1 second as 2yo but disappointing since in Grade 2 and Grade 3; doubts now
runners.owner: Reeves Thoroughbred Racing Pine Racing Stables Et Al

runners.name: Creole Chrome
runners.spotlight: Winner of three of his four starts at Fair Grounds, notably valuable state-bred event (extended 1m) on latest start; it's hard to assess the form of that nearly 7l win, in which he set a good pace and kept on strongly; speedy and far from certain to be suited by 1m1f in this much better grade.
runners.comment: Impressive winner of state-bred event latest, again good speed; lots to prove at 1m1f

runners.name: Great White
runners.spotlight: Successful at Turfway Park on Tapeta in a maiden in December and 8.5f Listed event in February, in the latter all out to beat Fulleffort by a neck after that rival was forced wide; useful and progressive but needs to improve markedly while switching surface to dirt.
runners.comment: Useful form at Turfway on Tapeta, winning Listed race by neck last time; lots more needed

runners.name: Ocelli
runners.spotlight: Maiden with fairly useful form, though nothing at this level; would be a shock winner.
runners.comment: Maiden with nothing in five runs to suggest he is this level
runners.owner: Ashley Durr Anthony Tate & Front Page Equestrian LLC

runners.name: Moonstrocity
runners.spotlight: Respectable third in maiden at Gulfstream on second start, always prominent; can win a race, but probably not this one.
runners.comment: Second in Gulfstream maiden; very stiff task in this league

runners.name: Ottinho
runners.spotlight: Game winner of 1m1f maiden at Aqueduct on second start as juvenile; never in contention behind 11l winner Talk To Me Jimmy when third in Listed race there, but kept on really well on slower part of track; stays well, capable of a fair bit better (especially in first-time blinkers) but a lot better is needed.
runners.comment: Aqueduct maiden winner; distant third in Listed there, wide of fastest ground; more needed

races.name: Wood Memorial Stakes Presented By Resorts World Casino (Grade 2) (Dirt)

runners.name: Napoleon Solo
runners.spotlight: Made all in Grade 1 Champagne Stakes here as juvenile, winning by nearly 6l on track which favoured speed (form has not worked out yet); only fifth in Grade 2 Fountain of Youth Stakes at Gulfstream in February, never able to challenge after missing break; scratched from Arkansas Derby; could bounce back.
runners.comment: Made all when easy winner of Grade 1 here as 2yo; slow start when disappointing last time

runners.name: Iron Honor
runners.spotlight: Beat a strongly fancied rival (pair clear) in 6f maiden on debut, then won 1m Grade 3 Gotham here when always prominent and battling on well to beat Crown the Buckeye by 1l, pair clear; thriving and very much the one to beat, though outside draw in big field does not help; should relish 1m1f.
runners.comment: Stayed on well to win Grade 3 Gotham here over 1m; the one to beat, as 1m1f trip will suit
runners.owner: St Elias Stable William H Lawrence & Glassman Racing

runners.name: Talk To Me Jimmy
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner here (New York-breds) and ran a remarkable race when winning Listed Withers Stakes over C&D by 11l, having field strung out down back stretch and keeping on strongly to draw away; needs to prove he can repeat the dose with more pace pressure, but clearly talented when allowed to dominate.
runners.comment: Spectacular 11l winner of Listed race over C&D; harder to dominate here but lots of talent
runners.owner: SEI Thoroughbreds Rudy R Rodriguez & Michael Imperio

runners.name: Albus
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at Tampa Bay on his third go in a maiden, tracking leader before taking command in straight and running on strongly to win by nearly 7l (fair time); open to improvement and is a quality individual physically with a long, linear stride; could easily be this good.
runners.comment: Impressive 7l winner of Tampa Bay maiden; much stiffer test here but looks good

runners.name: Red Zone Runner
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful form, mostly competing in lesser Pennsylvania-bred events such as when making all at Parx last time (8.5f; first start for Hugo Padilla) by nearly 16l in fast time; hard to assess in this tougher company but faces a lot more competition for the lead.
runners.comment: 16l winner of Pennsylvania-bred event at Parx last time; tough to dominate these

runners.name: Steel
runners.spotlight: Eyecatcher when winning 1m maiden at Gulfstream in February, missing the break before improving smoothly on turn, running on relentlessly to lead close home and beat Royal Crescent by nose; long-striding type, sure to improve at 1m1f, and he's interesting if he does not get too far behind.
runners.comment: Missed break but managed to win 1m maiden at Gulfstream in February by nose; intriguing
runners.owner: L Barrett Bernard & Terry L Stephens

runners.name: Right To Party
runners.spotlight: Tough winner of 1m maiden in January, overcoming early bumping; 9-1, just over 7l third to Iron Honor in Grade 3 Gotham (again over 1m here) in February, staying on from rear; should improve over this longer trip and has place prospects, if keeping in touch early a bit better.
runners.comment: Beaten just over 7l by Iron Honor when third in Grade 3 here last time (1m); place chance

runners.name: Ocelli
runners.spotlight: Maiden after six starts though has shown fairly useful form; only sixth in Listed race and black type event last two starts; big outsider.
runners.comment: Only sixth in Listed race and black type event last two starts; big outsider
runners.owner: Ashley Durr Anthony Tate & Front Page Equestrian LLC

runners.name: Minorinconvenience
runners.spotlight: Stepping out of New York-bred events, having won maiden at Saratoga and black type event there last summer; beaten nearly 6l when second in 1m event here last time; faces a much stiffer test.
runners.comment: Won maiden and black type for New York-breds; third in similar last month; stiff task now
runners.owner: Amelia J Green Amanda L Gillman & Uplands Flat Racing

runners.name: Bravaro
runners.spotlight: Won maiden and black type event for New York-breds last autumn; in frame at 8.5f since in Grade 3 Holy Bull (6l second to Nearly) and Grade 2 Fountain of Youth (11l fourth behind Commandment) at Gulfstream; this is easier and he should go well, if getting 1m1f; blinkered.
runners.comment: Good efforts in frame in Grade 3 and Grade 2, both at Gulfstream; claims in softer event
runners.owner: Albert Ciuffetelli Stephanie S Brennan et al

runners.name: Buetane
runners.spotlight: Won maiden at Del Mar last August; placed three times after in Graded stakes, notably when second to Ted Noffey in Grade 1 Hopeful at Saratoga; favourite, not clear run more than once when about 6l fourth in black type event at Colonial Downs latest; fair claims.
runners.comment: Placed three times in Graded stakes and unlucky fourth in black type latest; fair chance

runners.name: Courting
runners.spotlight: Highly touted (cost $5m as yearling) before 1m maiden win here last November; has struggled in Grade 2 events on both starts since, latterly beaten 11l when only sixth in the Risen Star at Fair Grounds; not progressing but it's too soon to write him off back in New York.
runners.comment: Fairly useful maiden winner who has struggled in two Grade 2s since; something to prove
runners.owner: Whisper Hill Farm LLC Stonestreet Stables & Windancer Farm

runners.name: Ottinho
runners.spotlight: Game winner of 1m1f maiden here on second start as juvenile; never in contention behind 11l winner Talk To Me Jimmy when third in Listed Withers here, but kept on really well on slower part of track; stays well, capable of a fair bit better (especially in first-time blinkers) but more needed; place chance.
runners.comment: Maiden winner here but distant third in Listed event, wide of fastest ground; place chance

runners.name: Intrepido
runners.spotlight: 9-1 winner of Grade 1 American Pharoah Stakes here over 8.5f last October, surging late as leaders tired; second in Grade 3 Robert B. Lewis in February (duelled throughout with Plutarch, beaten under 1l); slight doubt whether he wants 1m1f (can race freely) but he should give another good account.
runners.comment: Grade 1 winner over 8.5f and good second in Grade 3 since; claims, if seeing out 1m1f well
runners.owner: Dutch Girl Holdings LLC & Irving Ventures LLC

runners.name: Robusta
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner here in January but much improved form in blinkers when head second to Potente in Grade 2 San Felipe Stakes (8.5f) here last month, albeit well held by winner late on; should stay 1m1f and fair prospects of a place.
runners.comment: Best effort head second to Potente in Grade 2 San Felipe here; others have more scope

runners.name: Cherokee Nation
runners.spotlight: Big, awkward-running colt (pulls hard); flashed lots of talent in first five starts, but only fifth in two Graded events; scored by 10l in 1m maiden here in February, close to strong pace and opening up in great style, clocking very fast time; big player, if the penny has dropped mentally.
runners.comment: Big, awkward sort; improved when 10l winner of maiden here; dangerous if confirming that
runners.owner: SF Racing LLC Starlight Racing Et Al

runners.name: Potente
runners.spotlight: $2.4m yearling; unbeaten here in 6f maiden in January (made all) and Grade 2 San Felipe Stakes (8.5f) last month, in the latter chasing leaders while short of room and keeping on strongly to beat Robusta by a head once switched (went well clear after the line); still looked green and sure to improve again at 1m1f.
runners.comment: $2.4m yearling who won 8.5f Grade 2 San Felipe here on second start; the one to beat

runners.name: So Happy
runners.spotlight: Overcame some trouble to win Grade 2 San Vicente over 7f here in January; good third to Potente and Robusta, no extra late on, when third in Grade 2 San Felipe over 8.5f; step up to 1m1f does not appear in his favour.
runners.comment: Grade 2 winner at 7f here, then outstayed in similar event over 8.5f; doubtful at 1m1f
runners.owner: Norman Stables Llc And Saints Or Sinners

runners.name: Start The Ride
runners.spotlight: 14-1 when winning valuable state-bred event here in January, well on top late on; well-beaten sixth (slowly away) behind Potente in Grade 2 San Vicente last time; must prove he belongs at this level after last time but should not be written off yet; needs to break better.
runners.comment: Won valuable state-bred event but well behind in Grade 2 here last time; could bounce back
